<translate>
<!--T:22-->
The '''Bunker Spider''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. After building a nest, it lies in wait to ambush employees who step into their webs. It then gives chase to employees approaching them or their traps, dealing immense damage and ensnaring them in a silk-covered cocoon if they are killed. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Experimentation]].

<!--T:30-->
Bunker Spiders are giant, beige-colored tarantulas with seven pairs of legs, with six pairs on their abdomen and one pair on their forehead. They have black eyes and dark brown pedipalps, with similarly-colored splotches of hair scattered around its legs.

== Bestiary Entry == <!--T:17-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|BunkerSpider}}
<translate>
== Spawn chances == <!--T:23-->
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
== Mechanics ==
The Bunker Spider does not attack while it is in its 'preparation phase', during which it places webs within its territory.  If it encounters an employee during this phase, it may sometimes freeze until line of sight is broken. It also targets and attacks employees if they pass through one of its webs or get within close proximity of it.

<!--T:8-->
Once a Bunker Spider has laid all its webs, it often climbs onto a wall or ceiling, waiting for someone to trigger the traps. If an employee does not touch the spider or its webs, they can walk by the spider without aggravating it.

<!--T:9-->
If a Bunker Spider kills an employee, it cocoons their body in webbing and hangs it from the ceiling. Once a body has been cocooned, it can no longer be picked up, and retrieval is only possible by means of a [[Teleporter]].

<!--T:4-->
== Strategy ==
Before Bunker Spiders lay their webs, they are best left alone, since they will not be aggressive unless provoked by going close. They will not lay webs when in close proximity to other employees. This also means it is possible for an employee to prevent the Bunker Spider from ever laying any webs, albeit doing so poses danger to said employee. Once a Bunker Spider has laid its webs, however, passing through its domain becomes dangerous. Unlike other enemies, Bunker Spiders will only become aggressive when they detect an employee when touching their webs or standing directly beneath them. In addition, moving far enough away from its webs will cause it to give up and return unless hit with a shovel. Although an unencumbered employee can easily outpace a pursuing Bunker Spider, its webs will slow a careless employee down. If going past it is necessary, be sure to communicate the danger to fellow employees so they can avoid the webs too.

<!--T:10-->
Bunker Spiders can be killed in 5 [[shovel]] hits, but deal enough [[damage]] to bring an employee to critical from full health.

<!--T:24-->
Because the Bunker Spider inflicts a massive 90 damage per hit, even slightly injured employees should be careful around it as it surpasses the critical injury threshold of 50. With a [[stun grenade]] or [[zap gun]], they can become quite easy to dispatch. In addition, their slow speed makes them vulnerable to the same circle-strafe strategy used on the [[Thumper]].

<!--T:25-->
Furthermore due to their activation method, they can sometimes be killed point-blank with a [[shotgun]].

<!--T:11-->
Laid webs may be cleared with shovel hits, which will allow an employee to safely provoke a chase and then reposition for an easier kill. It is also good practice to clean the webs after killing the Bunker Spider sooner rather than later, not just because it impedes employees, but if a second Bunker Spider spawns, it will be triggered by the old webs, causing a possible surprise for an employee on the other side of the building.

<!--T:12-->
Bunker Spiders are one of the only enemies that are ''not'' able to cross the gaps bridged by metal beams in the Facility map, meaning you can safely camp out on in the middle of walkways.

<!--T:26-->
Sprinting backwards can help the player avoid the Bunker Spider's biting range while damaging it, as its speed makes it faster than the player's walking speed, is still outmatched by the player's sprinting speed.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Trivia}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:31-->
An "[[w:Arachnophobia|arachnophobia]] mode" is an available option that, when active, changes the Bunker Spider's model into a floating 3D model of the word "spider" in red capital letters, though it retains the original model's eyes and fangs.

== Gallery == <!--T:27-->

</translate><gallery>
File:Arachnophobia_mode_example.webp|<translate><!--T:28--> "Arachnophobia mode" enabled.</translate>
File:Arachnophobia_mode_example_with_hitbox.webp|<translate><!--T:29--> "Arachnophobia mode" enabled, with added hitbox visualization.</translate>
File:Bunkerspiderphases.png|<translate><!--T:32--> A Bunker Spider in wait on a wall (top) and pursuing an employee (bottom).</translate>
</gallery><translate>

<!--T:5-->
== History ==
=== [[Version 45]] ===

<!--T:13-->
Added Arachnophobia Mode, which replaces the Bunker Spider model with a floating 3D model of the word 'spider' in red capital letters; the enemy itself is otherwise unchanged.

<!--T:14-->
Made enemy unable to cross gaps bridges by metal beams in the Facility map.

=== [[Version 47]] === <!--T:18-->

<!--T:19-->
Made enemy slightly faster when almost dead.

<!--T:20-->
Made webs have a slightly large collision radius.

'''Roaming locusts''' are small harmless insects found outdoors swarming in spherical formations.

<!--T:4-->
== Bestiary entry ==
{{Template:Terminal|text=Roaming locusts


Sigurd's danger level: 0%


Scientific name: Anacridium-vega


<!--T:6-->
Known as roaming locusts, this is a species of grasshopper. Unlike some species which are more prone to jump or fly, roaming locusts are almost never grounded and stay close together even when in smaller numbers. They will quickly disperse when a predator disrupts them but are highly attracted to light.}}
== Mechanics ==

<!--T:7-->
Roaming locusts will disperse when approached by a player, [[entities]], and eventually on their own after some time.

== Trivia == <!--T:8-->

<!--T:9-->
* Along with [[Manticoil|Manticoils]], Roaming locusts are one of the two entities that cannot harm players.
* Locusts were originally meant to drift towards light, having possibly served as a ship hazard. This behavior was never added.

Manticoil.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=Doublewing | name = Manticoil | sciname = Quadrupes-manta | danger = 0% | power_level = 1 | diversity_level = 0 | max_count = 16 | pip_size = Tiny | attack_damage = N/A | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|Doublewing}} | stun_multiplier = 1.0 }} '''Manticoils''' are passive ambient birds found outdoors. With a distinctive four-winged anatomy, they can be seen soaring overhead or hopping on the ground either alone or in small groups. == Bestiary entry == {{Template:Terminal|text=Manticoils

Sigurd's danger level: 0%

Scientific name: Quadrupes-manta
Mantacoils are a passerine bird of the family corvidae. Their bodies are quite large compared to their early descendants, and their wingspan ranges from 55 to 64 inches. Their most defining characteristic is their set of four wings. Their back wings are mostly used to stabilize when at low speed, while their front two wings create the majority of lift. Their round bodies are a striking yellow but with black outlines or stripes along their primary (rear) feathers.

Manticoils mostly feed on small insects but can also feed on small rodents. They are highly intelligent and social. They pose little threat and have a generally passive temperament towards humans, although they are capable of transmitting Rabies, Rubenchloria, and Pitt Virus. }} == {{section heading/Spawn chances}} == {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == When approached by a player, Manticoils will quickly fly away while squawking and cawing. [[Audible_sounds|Noise]] will also scare them off. == Trivia == * Their terminal entry spells "Manticoil" as "Mantacoil" in one instance. It is unclear if this was intentional. * As they emit sound when taking off and flying around, they can alert [[Eyeless Dog|Eyeless Dogs]]. * Along with [[Roaming Locusts]], Manticoils are one of the two entities that cannot harm players. * Despite being killable, Manticoils don't have a death animation. {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

{{Template:Direct Quote}}
<translate>
<!--T:23-->
{{redirect|Slime|the entity which leaves a slimy trail|Backwater Gunkfish}}

<!--T:21-->
The '''Hygrodere''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It moves slowly along the ground towards nearby employees or sources of noise while covering a large surface area, rapidly damaging employees upon contact. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Offense]].

<!--T:24-->
Hygroderes are large slimes or amoeba-like entities with green or cyan coloration.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:2-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary}}
<translate>
== Spawn chances == <!--T:22-->
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>
== Mechanics == <!--T:5-->

<!--T:6-->
A Hygrodere moves extremely slowly around the map and always attempts to move towards the nearest employee. It takes a few hits to kill an employee. Unlike most other indoor entities, Hygroderes are unable to open doors, but are able to squeeze through them if they are open. It is attracted to any sort of sound, including [[Clown horn]]s, [[Airhorn]]s, [[Boombox]]es, employee footsteps, etc.

== Strategy == <!--T:7-->

<!--T:8-->
Because of the Hygrodere's slow speed, avoiding it is simple unless the employee becomes cornered or is dealing with other entities in the facility. The best course of action is to locate the Hygrodere and track its bubbling sounds to prevent being cornered.

<!--T:19-->
Once a Hygrodere has spotted an employee, it will always know their location, so employees should keep its continual pursuit in mind. As they cannot pass through doors, employees can effectively contain a Hygrodere to a room only accessible by doors via closing them. Employees must bear in mind, however, that the entity’s slow movement speed can make trapping one a very time-consuming process.

<!--T:20-->
They are also attracted to music from [[Boombox]]es, they will chase you and stop right before they touch you, so one may be turned on and dropped to lure the Hygrodere if necessary to free a cornered employee or otherwise draw it away.

<!--T:9-->
An additional danger of the Hygrodere is that they may occasionally camp bunker entrances, possibly because they are attempting to reach employees outside of the building, and thus instantly kill employees upon entry. This scenario should be considered if an employee exited the bunker while a Hygrodere was nearby, but is generally an uncommon occurrence.

<!--T:10-->
Hygrodere can not attack employees that are standing on railings, this can be used to easily get past them in narrow spaces.
</translate>

{{Template:Direct Quote}}
<translate>
<!--T:15-->
The '''Snare Flea''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It attaches itself to the ceiling and waits for employees to approach it, before dropping to the floor and attempting to suffocate them by wrapping itself around their head. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Assurance]].

<!--T:16-->
Snare Fleas are large, light-pink insectoid entities resembling lice or centipedes. They have five pairs of legs and an exoskeleton split into eight segments on  their back. Their heads have three pairs of black eyes arranged in a triangular formation, as well as small brown jaws, forcipules, and segmented antennae. 

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:14-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|SnareFlea}}

== {{section heading/Spawn chances}} ==
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
== Mechanics ==
Snare Fleas wait on the ceiling of any room or corridor. When an employee walks underneath a Snare Flea, it attempts to drop onto the employee's head. If the Snare Flea misses and falls onto the floor, it chases the employee for some time. If the Snare Flea successfully captures the employee, it wraps around their head, obstructing their view, muffling their voice, and forcing them to drop all of their items. It slowly damages the affected employee until it is removed or the employee dies. In singleplayer, the Snare Flea detaches on its own once the employee reaches critical health.

<!--T:7-->
Fleas can be killed by having the suffocating employee exit the facility, or when hit by a [[Shovel]]. If no one has a [[Shovel]] and the exit is near, the affected employee can be guided to the exit by voice.

<!--T:8-->
If the employee is alone and grabbed by a Flea, they may still free themself. If the employee has [[Shovel]] or [[Stop sign]], they should remain calm and scan the ground to locate their [[scrap]]. A Stop sign will be displayed however if your Shovel does not appear, it is likely beside your scrap items.
Once you've grabbed your weapon, attacking will hit the '''Snare Flea''' and free you.

<!--T:9-->
If a employee is successfully [[Teleporter|teleported]] while a Snare Flea is wrapped around their head, the Snare Flea dies.

<!--T:10-->
Stunning a Snare Flea while wrapped around the head of an employee will also cause them to release the employee, such as with a [[Stun grenade]] or [[Zap gun]].

<!--T:4-->
== Strategy ==
Snare Fleas are either lethal or a mere annoyance depending on your current situation. Hitting one while it has grabbed a worker will knock it off, but they can be difficult to hit otherwise. With this in mind, you can hit it off a coworker, try to get another hit in, then drop your shovel for your fellow coworker you rescued to repeat the process if the Snare Flea grabs you. If you find it while alone, your best bet is to avoid it as they don't attack outside a very small area, however this may make some halls or doorways difficult to pass.

<!--T:11-->
In the event that you are attached to by the Snare Flea and have no friends to assist you or a nearby exit to leave through, jumping into a pit is an effective means of killing the Snare Flea and increasing the survivability of your coworkers. In the event that you had a Shovel on you before being grabbed, you can pick it back up and hit the Snare Flea off yourself. 

<!--T:12-->
In singleplayer, if you are grabbed and do not have a Shovel or Stop Sign to knock it off, the Snare Flea will detach voluntarily once you reach critical health, regardless of your health beforehand. Be wary that it will not die if it detaches this way and if it grabs onto you a second time you will die.
[[File:Screenshot.png|left|thumb|222x222px]]
It is advantageous to carry a kitchen knife with you. When a flea bites you, use the scanner to quickly find the knife on the floor. Unlike a shovel, a knife is considered a scrap, so a scan can help you find it.

== History == <!--T:5-->
</translate>
=== {{section heading/Version X|35}} ===
* <translate><!--T:17--> The Snare Flea is added.</translate>

Bracken.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{Infobox entity
| name = <translate><!--T:25--> Bracken</translate>
| sciname = Rapax-folium
| power_level = 3
| diversity_level = 2
| max_count = 1
| door_speed_multiplier = 1.25
| pip_size = <translate><!--T:42--> Medium</translate>
| attack_damage = <translate><!--T:43--> Instant Kill</translate>
| hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|Flowerman}}
| stun_multiplier = 0.25
| id = flowerman
| cause_of_death = Strangulation
}}
{{Template:Direct Quote}}
<translate>
<!--T:1-->
The '''Bracken''', colloquially known as the "Flowerman", is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. Quietly stalking an employee from behind at a distance, it will eventually approach and attempt to snap an employee's neck before dragging away their corpse. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Vow]].

Brackens are lanky creatures with dark, beet-colored skin, small glowing white eyes, and rows of large dark green leaves adorning their backs. They also have long arms with five-fingered hands and hooved lower legs bent similar in shape to that of a horse.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:26-->
</translate>


<!--T:2-->
{{Terminal|text=<translate><!--T:44--> Bracken -- AKA flower man!</translate>

<translate>
<!--T:38-->
it was flower man, you can't say othrwise! i wanted to find his body .You were the cowards


<!--T:39-->
Scientific name: Rapax-folium

<!--T:40-->
There is debate on the genus to which the Bracken belongs. It is a bipedal vertebrate with skin the color and texture of a red beat. The name was coined for what appear to be leaves protruding from its upper spine. The purpose of these is believed to be for intimidation, however not much is known about the specifics of bracken behaviour due to its illusiveness and low population. 

<!--T:41-->
We know a little from accounts by wildlife experts who have encountered it. is a lone hunter with high intelligence. Its behavior can seem aloof; it exhibits high aggression even when unprovoked, yet it quickly backs off when confronted. However, brackens are known to up their hostility when cornered or simply watched for a long time. For this reason it's recommended to keep an eye on it but not to stare it down. No specimens have been collected dead or alive. It's theorized that upon death their bodies undergo a rapid decomposition process which is unique from other large animals.</translate>}}
<translate>
== Spawn chances == <!--T:46-->
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>
== Mechanics == <!--T:28-->

<!--T:3-->
=== States ===
The Bracken has three possible states: sneaking, evading, and enraged.

<!--T:4-->
==== Sneaking ====
The Bracken spawns in its sneaking state. While sneaking, the Bracken moves silently towards the nearest employee. If it successfully reaches them without being noticed, it will snap their neck.

<!--T:5-->
==== Evading ====
When spotted by an employee (within the middle 50% of their screen), the Bracken will stand up making rain stick sounds, and then immediately start evading where they will move away from the employee towards a chosen room (the one furthest to the employee) for 24 seconds, or 11 seconds for every third time it is spotted. The Bracken will then return to sneaking and repeat the cycle.

<!--T:6-->
==== Enraged ====
The Bracken has an internal anger meter. It is increased when the following conditions are met:

<!--T:29-->
{| class="wikitable"
! Action !! Extra Conditions !! Anger Meter !! Note
|-
| Staring at or being within 5 meters of Bracken || - || +.2/.2s || -
|-
| Being within 5 meters of Bracken || while evading (backing away) || +.2/.2s || -
|-
| Staring at Bracken || for the first 1.25s of evasion || +1.5/s || -
|-
| [[Stun grenade|Stunning]] Bracken || - || Sets to 6 || Doesn't add anger, so not causing an immediate check
|-
| Hitting Bracken with a weapon || - || Sets to 11, also +.1 || Adds anger, so Bracken would immediately turn into enraged state
|}

<!--T:13-->
Every 1 cumulative anger added (not set) results in a randomized check for its enraged state, with a 0% chance of becoming enraged at 0 anger and a 100% chance of it becoming enraged at 11 anger.

<!--T:14-->
Formula:
</translate>

 <translate><!--T:15--> random(0, .99) < anger * .09</translate>

<translate>
<!--T:16-->
Upon being enraged, the Bracken will immediately chase the nearest employee in an attempt to snap their neck, with its anger meter slowly ticking down at a rate of 1 anger/second. Once it hits 0 or the employee is dead, the Bracken will return to its evading state. 

=== Corpse dragging === <!--T:30-->

<!--T:7-->
Upon killing an employee, the Bracken will drag their corpse towards its chosen room (it being the one that it moved towards during its evading state). The Bracken will drop the employee's body under these conditions:
* It reaches the room
* It cannot reach the room
* It has not chosen a room
* It exits its evading state (including a transition to an enraged state)

== Strategy == <!--T:31-->

<!--T:8-->
Diligently checking surroundings indoors will ensure the Bracken is unable to sneak up on employees. Once spotted, as long as employees neither approach nor stare, it will immediately flee further into the building. While it is evading, a faint descending hum sound will be heard as long as it can still see you; when this sound cuts out, it is safe to turn around. The Bracken will also open doors to get to an employee, and can be identified by doors that are already opened or the sound of opening doors without any footsteps accompanied by it. Doors can be closed behind an employee to provide sound cues for a pursuing Bracken.

<!--T:17-->
[[Shotgun|Shotguns]] are the easiest and most effective way to dispose of a Bracken, as they deal more than enough damage to kill it in one shot if close enough. [[Zap gun]]s are also effective against the Bracken, making it possible to kill if paired with another employee holding a [[shovel]]. However, the Bracken will be angered by this and immediately attempt to snap an employee's neck if not killed before the stun's duration ends. A Bracken can be killed with a shovel alone by sprint-strafing diagonally in circles in a large area, such as the main entrance on Facility or Mansion interiors, but takes practice to ensure a successful attempt. Alternatively, the Bracken can be killed by triggering [[Landmine|landmines]], either by having the Bracken dragging a body on top of one or through other typical means of triggering a landmine. Stun grenades have no effect on the Bracken, and instead simply anger it.

<!--T:51-->
Another strategy for killing the Bracken with a shovel alone involves forcing it away, then attacking while moving backwards. First, spook it by glancing at it to initiate a retreat, then follow it if it isn't stationary; if it stops, look away and let it retreat again. When close enough, perform the initial attack on the Bracken, then let go of sprint, move backwards, and re-enable sprint to maximize acceleration, all while readying the next hit. When the Bracken is in range again, swing again.

[[Kitchen_Knife|Kitchen knives]] can be utilized in a similar fashion to shovels, but due to its much shorter range and the severe punishment for contacting with the Bracken's collider, using it is strongly discouraged.

<!--T:18-->
Employees are also able to lock the Bracken away using security doors by luring it in and closing the door once it is inside, with the employee escaping through [[Teleporter|teleportation]].

<!--T:45-->
Once an employee is grabbed, they will be unable to use any items and will die shortly after. While it is possible to be teleported in the short window after being grabbed but before being killed, this will usually happen accidentally and should not be relied on as a useful strategy.

<!--T:19-->
If an employee is killed by the Bracken, their body will be dragged far from any exit, making retrieval challenging without the use of a teleporter. If necessary, a body can be found manually by following its blood trail in the direction that goes deeper into the facility.

<!--T:36-->
Because the Bracken knows the facility and the Bracken's chosen room is selected at random, a careful employee can chase it down to go deeper into the building as it will continue to flee as much as it can, preferring said room. If sightings of the Bracken become very frequent, the usage of the strategy should be halted. Caution should be addressed if visiting the Bracken's chosen room, as it will often be angered and trying hard to return to it.

<!--T:50-->
A bracken's chosen room will not be a hallway, but rather an ordinary room, such as the facility room with a staircase. In the Mansion, it gets a bit trickier; despite them appearing to be ordinary rooms, the spiral staircase, the tiny door rooms, and the featureless rooms with a rug and a bookshelf are not chosen by the Bracken. 

<!--T:52-->
The easier way to know where the bracken might come back is remember where you first spotted it, remember the facility tiles, and put on a timer to either 11 or 24 seconds depending on the room it chooses from a section where it was first spotted.

== Sounds ==
</translate>
{{audio gallery
|<translate>A Bracken that has been spotted by an employee</translate>|File:Found1.ogg
|br|<translate>An enraged Bracken</translate>|File:Angered.ogg
|br|<translate>An employee's neck being snapped by a Bracken</translate>|File:CrackNeck.ogg
}}

== Trivia == <!--T:34-->
</translate>

This myth could have possibly spawned from a Bracken choosing this room during the game's early days of release, causing players to spread the idea it was specifically designed for itself.</translate>

Forest Keeper.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=ForestGiant | name = Forest keeper | sciname = Satyrid-proceritas | danger = 50% | power_level = 3 | diversity_level = 1 | max_count = 3 | door_speed_multiplier = 1.0 | see_through_fog = No | pip_size = Large | attack_damage = Instant kill | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|ForestGiant}} | stun_multiplier = 1.2 | cause_of_death = Crushing (eaten)
Gravity (crushed) }} {{redirect|Giant|the big bird creature|Giant Sapsucker}} The '''Forest Keeper''', also known as the "Forest Giant" or simply "Giant", is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. While unable to hear sounds, it relies on an enhanced line of sight to detect and pursue employees, before capturing them in its grasp and eating them whole. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Vow]]. Forest Keepers are colossal [[w:Ent|ent]]-like creatures composed of a rough, pale green skin resembling tree bark, with wider upper bodies and muscular arms with four-fingered hands. Large thorns jut out of their clavicle and upper forearms, and several black and yellow eyespots adorn their torso and legs, with two larger eyespots on their pectorals. Although they lack a head, they have a large hole which functions as a mouth on their chest. == Bestiary entry == {{Template:Terminal|text=FOREST KEEPER

Sigurd's danger level: 50%

Scientific name: Satyrid-proceritas
Believed to share a common ancestor with rapax-folium, these behemoths are called Forest Keepers for the biomes they often inhabit. Their bodies bare markings on their front and back which mimic eyes--this trait is more helpful in their youth, as they are not agile. Their skin is a unique, dense material which hardens further throughout their lives; the large spikes and bumps across their bodies form as a result of aging.

It's been said Forest Keepers exhibit a curious behavior similar to that of a human child the age of 5 or 6. They will eat anything they find fascinating. Forest Keepers don't actually need to put anything into their mouths, and it's theorized their main source of energy is a process similar to photosynthesis. Still, this makes them relatively dangerous to observe. They can see across long distances, so staying low and making use of cover is a must. They cannot enter small spaces and are not generally destructive, so stay close to shelter or overhangs. }} == {{section heading/Spawn chances}} == {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == === Suspicion meter === Forest Keepers assign a suspicion meter to each employee. This meter will rise any time the employee is within the Keeper's field of view. Formula: Clamp(Time.deltaTime / (distanceToPlayer * .21) * visibilityModifier, 0, 1) Crouching without moving will multiply how quickly the meter rises by 1x. Being uncrouched and moving (whether walking or sprinting) will each add 1 to this multiplier, with a max of 3x. Additionally, as long as a player is in the Keeper's field of view, the suspicion meter of every player that ''isn't'' in its field of view will decrease by 1 every 3 seconds, and can become negative. This makes it less likely for one Keeper to begin chasing a different target. === States === Forest keepers have three possible states: roaming, chasing, and searching. ==== Roaming ==== While roaming, a Forest Keeper will slowly wander around the map. Occasionally, it will stop and look around. If it spots an employee in this phase, once their suspicion meter is greater than .35, the Keeper will stop and stare at them for 3 seconds. If line of sight is not broken, but their suspicion meter is still less than 1, it will walk towards them to investigate. ==== Chasing ==== Once an employee’s suspicion meter is greater than 1, the Keeper will begin to chase them until either the employee has died, or has been out sight for 3 seconds. In the latter case, it will transition to its searching phase. ==== Searching ==== The Keeper will run around the immediate vicinity (about 25 units) for 9 seconds. If it fails to locate an employee within this time, it will then transition back to its roaming state. == Strategy == Staying crouched and avoiding their line of sight using the environment (such as hiding behind trees and rocks) will avoid employees alerting a Forest Keeper of their presence. If you are in a Keeper's line of sight, you will hear a continuous low-pitched horror ambience, as if you just saw a Bracken; use this as your cue to break line of sight. Always conserve stamina while outdoors in case you need to sprint away from an alerted Keeper; you are faster than them when sprinting with up to about 35 pounds. Though Forest Keepers cannot reach employees inside the [[ship]], they can see through an open door, and standing too close to the exit puts you at risk of being grabbed. If an employee is grabbed, others can use the [[Zap gun]], [[Stun grenade]], [[Homemade flashbang]], or a flashing [[Radar-booster]] to temporarily stun the Keeper and free the employee. Alternatively, using a [[teleporter]] or setting the Keeper on fire can free them. Currently there are only 4 ways to set a Forest Keeper ablaze: * The explosion from an [[Easter egg]] * The explosion from a [[Jetpack]] * Lightning * [[Old Bird|Old Birds]] Keepers will die shortly after catching fire. Although it is possible to kill a Keeper with shovels, this is not recommended due to how easily the employee can be grabbed. If you plan on taking this route, having more crew members would assist with killing the Keeper. Taking many [[Stun grenade]]s, a [[Zap gun]], or a flashing [[Radar-booster]] can also help to keep it stunned, allowing your crew members to hit it with shovels until it dies. An [[Earth Leviathan#Emerging|Earth Leviathan]]'s emerging is another method to kill a Keeper, so you may want to try to get a Leviathan to attack. However, this strategy is risky, as you have to handle 2 lethal threats at the same time, and may run out of stamina too soon. Forest Keepers can be held at the side or back of the ship if an employee stands in the middle of the oval shape on the roof of the ship to act as bait. Straying too far to the edge of the oval, or standing on another part of the ship, will result in the Keeper being able to reach you. This method allows the rest of the crew to safely get in and out of the ship, deal with other threats, or kill the Keeper by hitting it through the walls of the ship. You can outrun the forest keeper, just when you see the hands on your screen (as it's catching up to you) sprint away. == {{section heading/Sounds}} == {{audio gallery | A Forest Keeper's footsteps heard nearby|File:GiantStomp1.ogg|File:GiantStomp2.ogg|File:GiantStomp3.ogg|File:GiantStomp4.ogg|File:GiantStomp5.ogg |br| A Forest Keeper's footsteps heard far away|File:DistantRumble1.ogg|File:DistantRumble2.ogg|File:DistantRumble3.ogg |br| A stunned Forest Keeper|File:StunGiant.ogg |br| A Forest Keeper grabbing an employee|File:Roar.ogg |br| A Forest Keeper eating an employee|File:FGiantEatPlayerSFX.ogg |br| A Forest Keeper burning|File:ForestGiantBurn.ogg |br| A Forest Keeper dying|File:ForestGiantDie.ogg |br| A dying Forest Keeper falling over|File:ForestGiantFall.ogg }} == Trivia == * The myth that Forest Keepers chase laser pointers is false. They are only attracted to employees. * [[Baboon Hawk]]s and [[Old Bird]]s will target and kill Keepers, the latter setting them on fire. [[Giant Sapsucker]]s will attack Forest Keepers that approach close to their nests. [[Eyeless Dog]]s can also kill Keepers, though they will not target them on purpose, and instead deal damage by coincidentally walking through them. Forest Keepers will neither retaliate nor avoid threats. * If an employee is to stand behind a Keeper during its death animation, they will be crushed and killed. == History == === [[Version 45]] === * Forest Keepers nerfed, making it easier to hide from them. === [[Version 50]] === Forest Keepers are now killable. They have 30 HP. They are very vulnerable to fire. {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

<!--T:12-->
The '''Spore Lizard''' is a docile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It flees from employees when spotted, and releases a dense cloud of spores from its tail or bites in self-defense when cornered. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Experimentation]].

<!--T:13-->
Spore Lizards are quadrupedal entities, and have olive-green scales, a large sucker-shaped mouth with pink lips and small, sharp jaws in place of their face, and a large purple ball at the ends of their tails.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:10-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|SporeLizard}}

== {{section heading/Spawn chances}} ==
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>
== Mechanics == <!--T:3-->

<!--T:7-->
Spore Lizards are passive entities, only attacking employees when threatened or cornered. In their normal state, they will wander the [[Interiors|facility]], making squelching sounds as they walk. When a Spore Lizard detects an employee from afar, it will stare at them, hissing and rattling its tail. It will then attempt to back away, still looking at the employee as it attempts to break line of sight, before skittering off. If approached too closely, the Spore Lizard will bite the employee and release a cloud of pink spores that limit visibility, giving the Spore Lizard an opportunity to escape.

<!--T:4-->
== Strategy ==
While the Spore Lizard may come across as intimidating, it most certainly isn't dangerous. It will only attack with a non-lethal bite in very close range, so an employee's best option is to simply respect it and leave it alone. The Spore Lizard will often hiss long before a employee even comes close enough for it to bite, so evading them is usually trivial.

<!--T:8-->
The only real danger of a Spore Lizard presents itself when other wildlife is nearby. A Spore Lizard releasing its spores during a time in which another, much more lethal creature is on the hunt, can pose a serious risk. To avoid this situation, simply take note of the general area a Spore Lizard is wandering in, and try to avoid it when being pursued by more dangerous wildlife.

<!--T:14-->
Due to its sheer retreating speed, an employee can opt to chase down the spore Lizard as it runs after being frightened. This strategy operates similar to the [[Bracken]] since the spore Lizard also knows the facility more than the employee. However, unlike the Bracken, the spore Lizard will prioritize spacing itself from you first rather than sprinting through the building, so the employee has to give the spore Lizard room to run away and follow its footsteps after. Also unlike the Bracken strategy, the spore Lizard does not have a favourite room, so following it will lead an employee to random rooms rather than deep rooms, which can be a downside for most. The upside is that a spore Lizard is far, far safer than a Bracken.

== History == <!--T:5-->
</translate>

Eyeless Dog.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=MouthDog | name = Eyeless dog | sciname = Leo caecus | power_level = 2 | diversity_level = 1 | max_count = 8 | door_speed_multiplier = 0.3 | see_through_fog = No | pip_size = Medium | attack_damage = Instant kill (employees)
3 (entities) | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|MouthDog}} | stun_multiplier = 0.7 | cause_of_death = Mauling }} {{redirect|Dog|the smaller canine entity|Kidnapper fox}} The '''Eyeless Dog''' is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. Commonly found roaming in packs, it is completely blind, and instead uses its exceptional hearing to hunt down and ruthlessly maul employees who alert it to their presence. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Titan]]. Eyeless Dogs are large, quadrupedal feline creatures with maroon fur, with a thin mane of darker crimson hair running down their back, along with a long tail. Their heads are almost entirely featureless, save for a large, gaping mouth with a pair of sharp jaws containing off-white needle-like teeth. == Bestiary entry == {{Terminal|text=EYELESS DOG

Scientific name: Leo caecus
A large mammal of the family Saeptivus. They are social, hunting in very large packs. They have also been called "breathing lions" for their recognizable sound and large mouths. They are endurance hunters and attempt to make up for their lack of sight with their sense of hearing. It's a popular myth that they often mistake the sounds of their own kind for prey, entering fights within their own packs.

Their behaviour is unique from other pack animals in their tendency to spread out far to cover distance. When an eyeless dog has found prey, it roars to alert others in the near vicinity, who will also sound the alarm, sometimes resulting in a kind of chain reaction. Eyeless dogs can be dangerous in swarms. However, they are characteristically clumsy, taking guesses at their prey's exact location which are often incorrect.}} == {{section heading/Spawn chances}} == {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == === Noise loudness meter === Each [[Audible_sounds|sound in the game]] has its own specified "noise loudness" meter, being increased and decreased depending on the distance from an entity (such as an Eyeless dog). Formula: Distance < 18 * Noise Loudness '''''Note:''' Noise Loudness is halved if there is no line of sight between the player and an entity.'' === States === Eyeless dogs have three possible states: roaming, suspicious, and enraged. ==== Roaming ==== While roaming, Eyeless dogs will wander aimlessly, and not specifically stay by the player. Their behavior is based entirely on sound detection, not proximity to a silent player. ==== Suspicious ==== If a sound with a noise loudness of 0.25 or more is heard by Eyeless dogs, it will add one counter to their "suspicion level". They will then investigate the sound, with their suspicion level decreasing by 1 every 4 seconds. When in the suspicious state, Eyeless Dogs will boost their heads up, and rumble their head while walking confidently. Once their suspicion level is less than 1, they will begin to roam again. ==== Enraged ==== If their suspicion level reaches 9 or more, or the noise loudness formula above is true, Eyeless dogs will go into a frenzy, quickly lunging towards the approximate location of the noise once close enough. While not lunging, their suspicion level will decrease by 1 every 3 seconds. Once the suspicion level is less than 9, they will stay still until their suspicion level drops to less than 1 (where they will begin roaming again) or another sound is heard. If an employee is caught by an Eyeless Dog in this phase, they are torn to shreds. The Eyeless Dog will violently shake its head side to side with the player in its jaws. In this temporary moment, the eyeless dog will be stunned for a couple seconds, as it is busy with its meal. == Strategy == Spotting Eyeless dogs from afar, players can maneuver around them without alerting them through noise. Alternatively, crouch walking (which eliminates sound made through moving), remaining silent and out of Eyeless dogs' path to avoid a head-on collision allows players to pass by without detection. However, those carrying noisy [[scrap]] (even if unequipped in their inventory) such as [[Teeth]], [[Old Phone]]s, and [[Robot Toy]]s will alert nearby Eyeless dogs. Dropping any item, regardless of its weight, creates a sound that the dogs can hear and will cause them to investigate the area. Their alert system is triggered by specific noises (sounds with a loudness pitch of 0.25 or more), not the item's mass. For example, dropping a [[bell]] will alert the dogs system more than it would dropping a [[Whoopie cushion]], because the [[bell]] has a higher loudness pitch. Eyeless dogs are especially challenging for those staying inside the [[ship]]. Hearing or seeing them nearby, players are able to warn others that there are Eyeless dogs outside before avoiding making sound altogether. Rushing to close the [[Door Controls|door]] can attract Eyeless dogs through the noise made, which may enter the ship once the door reopens. If inside the ship, players can either stay silent in the corner to avoid its path while it walks through, or use the [[Loud Horn|loud horn]] to draw them to the front. Additionally, Eyeless dogs can be drawn away by purchasing an item from the [[store]], as the jingle of the pod will attract them until it departs. Even if being silent and crouching, an Eyeless Dog can still go into its enraged state if it is aggroed, and will proceed to lunge into the [[ship]]. All players in the proximity of the eyeless dog, which is usually any corner of the [[ship]], will get killed. If an eyeless dog is not aggroed, and comes into the ship, the eyeless dog will not detect the player hitbox even if it is touching you. This only applies if a player crouching (Preferably at a corner of the [[ship]]) and staying silent. Hitting Eyeless dogs with a [[shovel]] will cause them to instantly charge in the direction that they're facing. Players can take advantage of this by forcing them to charge into a wall, followed by a series of repeated shovel hits to stun lock and eventually kill with 12 hits. == {{section heading/Sounds}} == {{audio gallery | An Eyeless Dog's footsteps|File:Stomp1double.ogg|File:Stomp2double.ogg|File:Stomp3double.ogg |br| An Eyeless Dog's breathing|File:BreatheDog1.ogg |br| An Eyeless Dog growling from a nearby noise|File:Growl.ogg |br| An Eyeless Dog's roar when alerted by a noise|File:Roar 0.ogg |br| An Eyeless Dog lunging|File:LungeDog1.ogg |br| An Eyeless Dog being stunned|File:StunDog.ogg |br| An Eyeless Dog mauling an employee|File:KillPlayerDog.ogg }} == {{section heading/Gallery}} == File:Dog.png| An eyeless dog searching for prey. File:EyelessDogLunge.png| An eyeless dog lunging. == {{section heading/Trivia}} == Eyeless dogs and [[Feiopar]]s are closely related, both belonging to the family Saeptivus. == {{section heading/Notes}} == Due to [[Known_bugs#Shotguns_can_deal_double_or_possibly_triple_damage_to_some_entities|a bug]], Eyeless Dogs can take double damage (10) from a [[Shotgun]] at close range, allowing them to be killed in as few as 2 shots. {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

Circuit Bees.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom|Circuit_Bee}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=RedLocustBees | name = Circuit Bees | sciname = Crabro-coruscus | altname = Red Bees | danger = 90% | power_level = 1 | diversity_level = 0 | attack_damage = 10 | attack_speed = 400ms | pip = Tiny | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|RedLocustBees}} | cause_of_death = Electrocution }} {{Template:Direct Quote}} {{redirect|Bee|Butler bees|Mask Hornets|the harmless insect swarms|Roaming locusts}} '''Circuit Bees''', also known as '''Red Bees''', are hostile outdoor [[entities]]. In order to protect their [[Bee Hive|hive]], they attack employees that approach them or their hive by conducting electricity, and become enraged if the hive is stolen or they are otherwise unable to return to it. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[March]]. Circuit Bees are red-colored bees with two thin pairs of legs and two pairs of light brown wings. == Bestiary Entry == {{Terminal|text=Circuit Bees Sigurd's danger level: 90% Scientific name: Crabro-coruscus The circuit bee, also known as red bee, is a eusocial flying insect of the genus Apis, a descendent of the honey bee. Their appearance is quite recognizable from their hairy, red bodies and two sets of wings. Like their ancestors, they are well-known for their intelligent social BEEhavior, large colony size, building wax nests which they use to store honey, and their important role in pollination. Unlike the honey bee, which often chose high places such as trees to construct its hive, red bees create their hives on the ground. Red bees are highly defensive. They will leave the nest to attack any creature that comes within several meters, leaving BEEhind only the queen and drone bees. This bold BEEhavior is enabled by their most defining aBEElity, which is their electrostatic charge. Red bees produce friction with the air. They also produce friction by rubbing their two pairs of wings against each other and by rubbing against one another while in the hive. What allows them to create such a surplus of electric field compared to the honey bee is still under research, as they generate a stronger electric field when panicked or angered. This ability is especially useful for them around water. It's BEEst to keep your distance. If a red bee hive is stolen, red bee swarms will enter an onslaught in which they attack any living creature. This destructive BEEhavior will last until they have located the hive or completely exhausted themselves, which can take hours to days. They have BEEn known to leave BEEhind fields of bodies of small rodents, insects and even some larger mammals, and in rare cases they can start fires. Their strong BEEnefits and drawbacks to their ecosystems are highly debated. BEEbated !! - the indomitable Sigurd}} == Spawn chances == {{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == Circuit Bees will remain within their hive, while emitting a quiet buzzing noise until an employee enters 10 units of its hive. The swarm will then take off and pursue the intruder until they have either killed them or if the trespasser has left the hive's range. They deal [[damage]] quickly and can kill an employee in about 3-4 seconds of uninterrupted attacks. If Circuit Bees cannot navigate to their hive, either because an employee took it inside, into the closed ship, or they cannot navigate to the hive, then they will wander around attacking any employee in sight. They can be very dangerous when wandering around a moon, as they have an increased movement speed and aggro range. == Strategy == Circuit Bees will not be a major problem for employees not intending to collect their hives, and being vigilant about walking into the radius of a beehive is the most strategy that will be necessary to avoid death. However, taking a Circuit Bees' hive is almost always worth it if you have time, as it is usually very high value. For more details on hive collection, consult the [[Bee_Hive#Strategy|Bee Hive Strategy]] section. {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

Coil-Head.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Template:Direct Quote}} {{Infobox entity | name = Coil-Head | sciname = Vir colligerus | danger = 80% | power_level = 1 | diversity_level = 2 | max_count = 5 | door_speed_multiplier = 0.06 | see_through_fog = No | pip_size = Medium | attack_damage = 90 | attack_speed = 200ms | dps = 450 | contact_damage = 90% | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|SpringMan}} | stun_multiplier = 3.25 | id = SpringMan | cause_of_death = Strangulation }} The '''Coil-Head''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It remains stationary when it is within an employee's line of sight, but chases and attacks nearby employees extremely quickly while not being observed. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Offense]]. Coil-Heads are tall, mannequin-like entities, with mostly featureless beige skin, save for the numerous screws embedded around their bodies with several small, dried wounds. They have missing arms and a large spring in place of their neck, topped with a head with featureless, hollow eye sockets and a gaping mouth. == Bestiary entry == {{quote bestiary|CoilHeads}} == Spawn chances == {{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == === Patrolling === Once a Coil-head first enters the map, it starts patrolling an area. During this patrol, it moves on a set route back and forth at a slower speed than when chasing an employee. DoAiInterval - https://pastebin.com/9GHsGnYw While the Coil-head is patrolling, it constantly scans for employees, only attacking if an employee meets the following conditions: * Employee and the Coil-head have an unobstructed view of one another * AND either: ** The employee is looking at the Coil-head, meaning they are less than 60 units away from one another and the Coil-head is visible within a 68° horizontal FOV (default camera FOV is 66°) ** OR Coil-head is less than 30 units away and has a traversable path to the employee's position Once successfully passed, the Coil-head progresses into its attacking phase. === Attacking === Once elevated to this phase, its movement speed ramps up significantly, increasing from 6 to 14.5 SpringManAi - https://pastebin.com/NueWGKjB. In this state, the Coil-head will '''always''' target the closest employee '''that has a reachable path''', regardless of line of sight, distance or its previous target, and will accelerate towards them unless observed by at least one employee or stunned (see [[#Observation]]).Update - https://pastebin.com/j93DwH3B ==== Losing Aggro ==== There are two ways to de-aggro a Coil-head and revert it to the [[#Patrolling]] phase: * While there are no targetable employees (ones without a valid path for the Coil-head to reach), it will de-aggro if nobody is looking at the Coil-head for a cumulative total of 1 second. * OR if the Coil-head consecutively meets all of the following conditions for 4.5 seconds: ** Hasn't damaged any employees for at least 7 seconds ** Is actively moving toward an employee ** Is more than 40 units away from all employees ** Does not have a direct line of sight to any employees within 140 units ==== Dealing Damage ==== When a Coil-head is unobserved, in physical contact with an employee, and not stunned, it does 90 [[damage]] every 0.2 seconds to the employeeOnCollideWithPlayer - https://pastebin.com/RCaRyZk5. Since observation of a Coil-head requires that the employee be at least 0.3 units away, this means two things: * Once a Coil-head hits an employee once, it is nearly impossible to survive. * If an employee is within physical contact of an otherwise unobserved Coil-head, it will attack them regardless of that employee's visibility of it. ==== Recharging ==== While in its attacking phase, if a Coil-head has cumulatively spent more than 9 seconds '''actively''' moving toward any employee, it will enter a recharging/cooldown phase for 11 seconds. During this time, its neck will be retracted and the Coil-head will be unable to move or deal damage, regardless of whether it is being observed or not. ==== Observation ==== There are two circumstances where the Coil-head will be unable to move in its attacking phase: * Coil-head is stunned * OR an employee is observing the Coil-head, that is: ** The employee and the Coil-head have an unobstructed view of one another ** The employee is looking at the Coil-head, meaning they are less than 60 units away from one another and the Coil-head is visible within a 68° FOV (default camera FOV is 66°) ** The employee is at least 0.3 units away from the Coil-head == Strategy == A group can have a single employee watch Coil-heads while others loot. This employee would have to be especially cautious of other enemies, as the employee would be severely inhibited by the Coil-heads' presence. Considering that an attacking Coil-head will always head for the nearest employee, it would be imperative to warn other employees of its presence. A [[Stun grenade]]/[[Zap gun]]/[[Radar-booster]] can be used to disable Coil-Heads for several seconds, allowing for an escape. It takes a Coil-head a ludicrous amount of time (almost 17 seconds) to open an unlocked door. Employees can use this to their advantage in order to make an escape. Be wary, as Coil-heads can open doors even while they are being observed. Since Coil-heads are mostly unable to traverse onto any vertical obstacles, when an employee stands on top of many such objects, the Coil-head is unable to find a traversable path to the employee and cannot target them. This can be used to easily [[#Losing Aggro|de-aggro]] the Coil-head when all indoor employees are in such non-reachable positions and refrain from observing it for 1 second. Alternatively, if some of these positions satisfy the in-game path-finding logic, but result in the Coil-head being stuck or unable to reach the employee, then not observing the Coil-head for a total of 9 seconds in this state will result in an 11 second [[#Recharging|cooldown]] that will restrain and subsequently [[#Losing Aggro|de-aggro]] it as well. Should an employee find themselves in the presence of multiple Coil-heads, a good strategy is to attempt to bunch them close together, as this allows the employee to have every Coil-head within their field of view at the same time. Coil-heads are incredibly dangerous when paired with other entities, especially [[Bracken|Brackens]] and [[Snare Flea|Snare Fleas]], due to the Coil-head’s and Bracken’s conflicting requirements of whether to be looked at or not, and the risk of accidentally stepping into a Snare Flea’s trap while staring at the Coil-head. If encountered together, the best strategy is to go around a corner or obstacle to break line of sight with both the Coil-head and Bracken, but remain looking in the Coil-head’s direction so that it immediately enters your line of sight again when it turns the corner, simultaneously freezing it and scaring the Bracken off, or luring the Coil-Head to a location that you know has no Snare Fleas. (Keep in mind that one may have spawned while you were gone and latched to the ceiling.). == V.50 Beginning cutscene == During the cutscene, we see what appears to be an office in a factory that makes Coil-heads. Through the window, we can see one. But when the alarm goes off, we see it fall over, and in the background, we hear other Coil-heads killing and terrorizing employees and others as they chase them in the dark. == Sounds == {{audio gallery |A Coil-Head walking|File:BarefootStep1.ogg|File:BarefootStep2.ogg|File:BarefootStep3.ogg|File:BarefootStep4.ogg |br|A Coil-Head's spring upon stopping|File:Spring1.ogg|File:Spring2.ogg|File:Spring3.ogg |br|A Coil-Head's spring wobbling|File:SpringWobble1.ogg|File:SpringWobble2.ogg |br|A Coil-Head killing an employee|File:KillPlayerSpring.ogg }} == History == === [[Version 35]] === * Introduced. === [[Version 47]] === * The audible range of Coil-head’s footsteps has been slightly increased. === [[Version 60]] === * Coil-head now enters a recharge phase after chasing employees for a certain distance. * Coil-head can now lose track of an employee when they are out of range and line-of-sight for a long enough time. * Coil-head now cannot complete a jump as easily if they are spotted by an employee while jumping. === Version 61 === * Coil-head now will not move when looked at under any circumstances, including when they cannot reach the employee looking at them. === Version 62 === * Slightly decreased the time a Coil-head needs to spend moving before recharging * Decreased the time a Coil-head spends recharging * Coil-head’s timer before it needs to recharge now counts down 80% slower when it is pushing a door or otherwise stuck than when it is actually moving, regardless of wether it is being looked at or not. * Coil-head now eventually enters recharge mode if it is inside the [[Mineshaft]] elevator. == Gallery == File:Coil head 3d.webp| Alternative render of the Coil-Head. File:Coilhead concept art.png| Coil-head concept art + two unknown creatures. File:Employee Coiled.png|An employee's body after being killed by a Coil-head. == Sources == {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

Experimentation.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox | moon = Experimentation | image = Experimentation_map_outside.png | routecost = 0 }} '''41-Experimentation''' is the starting moon in Lethal Company. It is one of the easier moons to explore, reflected by its hazard level of "D". When the game starts, the ship starts in the orbit of Experimentation and employees can land there immediately. == Description == {{quote INFO moon|ExpInfo}} == Environment == Experimentation is a desert moon with various buildings scattered around the map. Near the entrance to the [[The Facility]] is a train track on an elevated platform that runs across the map. The Fire Exit is located to the left of the Main Entrance up a set of stairs. From the Fire Exit there runs a pipe all the way back to the ship that can be used for transporting items back. When employees enter the large hall where the main entrance is there is a chance that a large shutter door will close partially, with a loud jolt. If this happens enough times, the door will fully shut, forcing employees to instead use the Fire Exit. == Strategy == The optimal path to get to the entrance is to go in a straight line straight towards the ladder up to the rail, then straight toward the main entrance. You will not take fall [[damage]] jumping off the rail on either side, so you can ignore ladders when returning to the ship. It is possible for this ladder to be blocked by a large rock that can randomly spawn. If this happens, it is advised to walk to the right and find one of the alternative ladders. == Loot table == Rarity is the base values the scrap has for spawning. The equation scrap rarity / total rarities = X then X * 100 = % gives you the spawn chance for a scrap. For example if you take the rarity for the [[V-Type Engine]], which equals 90, and divide it by the total rarity value (the sum of all scrap rarities), which equals 613. 90/613 = 0.1468 and 0.1468 * 100 = 14.68 making our spawn chance for [[V-Type Engine]]s on Experimentation 14.68% for v45. {{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Experimentation}} {{special scrap spawn explainer|hives=y|facility=y|nutcrackers=y}} == Entities == === Indoor === [[Bunker Spider|Bunker Spiders]] and [[Snare Flea|Snare Fleas]] are pretty common on Experimentation. While spiders can be avoided pretty easily, snare fleas in multiplayer can be pretty annoying when a crew forgets to bring a shovel to quickly get rid of them. {{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}} === Nighttime === [[Eyeless Dog|Eyeless Dogs]] are fairly common on Experimentation. But because they only begin to spawn very late, they can be avoided rather easily. [[Old Bird|Old Birds]] and [[Forest Keeper|Forest Keepers]] are very unlikely, but may be a challenge due to the minimal cover of the terrain. Consider traversing the pipeline by the fire exit if these become a problem. {{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}} === Daytime === [[Circuit Bees|Bees]] are quite uncommon on Experimentation as it is one of the easiest moons. {{Template:WikiAutomated/DaytimeEntities}} === Spawn chance curves === Nighttime entities only start spawning at around 5pm on Experimentation. After 10pm enemy spawning drastically increases with its peak at 12am. Daytime entity spawning gradually decreases with time with most of the entities spawning at the beginning of the game. See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation. [[File:Experimentation_spawn_curve.png|950px]] == Trivia == * Experimentation has numerous rooms and hallways from [[Version 4|v4]]. These rooms are only accessible (in vanilla) via two extension ladders to a floating doorway past the fire exit. * There is a easter egg where the garage door near the main entrance closes half way, This is a reference to the early experimentation map found in v4. == Data chips == {{translators' notes| Name is exactly: {{WDLS|32995|1|experimentation}} (the noun form of {{WDLS|110|1|to experiment}}, meaning ''to test'' or ''to try [something] out'', which is an action done by new players) }} {{Spoilers}} There are 2 data chips on the surface: "[[Sigurd Logs#Swing of things - Aug 27|Swing of things]]" is on a water tower, on the right after leaving the ship.
"[[Sigurd Logs#Shady - Aug 31|Autopilot]]" is on the opposite side on the pipeline, reachable by jumping to the pipe from the fire exit and following it back towards the ship. {{navbox moons}}

Vow.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox|
| moon = Vow
| image = Map-vow.png
| routecost = 0
}}<translate>
<!--T:3-->
'''Vow''' is a rather dangerous [[Moons|moon]] and belongs to the category of Easy moons. It has a hazard level of "C" and offers accordingly little loot. Despite that, it is quite dangerous indoors as well as outdoors compared to the amount and value of loot that can be found here.

== Description == <!--T:4-->
</translate>
{{Template:Terminal|text=56-Vow

<nowiki>----------------------</nowiki>

<translate>
<!--T:5-->
CONDITIONS: Humid.

<!--T:6-->
HISTORY: Vow appears to have been inhabited by several colonies across its continents, but there is now no sign of life, and they have become a mystery.

<!--T:7-->
FAUNA: Diverse, teeming with plant-life</translate>}}
<translate>
== Environment == <!--T:8-->

<!--T:9-->
Vow has a lush forest environment full of hills and trees. In front of the Main Entrance are two bridges spanning a deep gully lead to the main entrance. One bridge is a solid dam, with the fire exit at its foot, and the other is a rickety metal bridge that collapses under too much stress.

=== The Fragile Bridge === <!--T:10-->
{{main|Bridge}}

== Strategy == <!--T:16-->

<!--T:17-->
The fastest path from the ship to the main entrance is to turn right when exiting the ship and to go in a straight line towards the fragile bridge.

<!--T:18-->
The optimal path to get in and out of the interior is to go straight towards the right bridge. It can be difficult to identify which direction the interior is in, since the landing area has few landmarks. The fragile bridge will usually hold one employee without breaking. If crossing with multiple employees or heavy items, it may be better to use the dam.

<!--T:30-->
While it may be necessary to take a detour to avoid outdoor entities, employees are encouraged to keep their trips across Vow's surface as short and direct as possible from evening onwards, as it is easy to get lost in the densely wooded terrain and the presence of at least one [[Forest Keeper]] is virtually guaranteed.

== Loot table == <!--T:19-->
</translate>
{{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Vow}}
{{special scrap spawn explainer|hives=y|sapsuckers=y}}
<translate>
== Entities == <!--T:20-->

=== Indoor === <!--T:21-->

<!--T:22-->
The [[Bracken|Flowerman]] is the most common entity on Vow. This makes Vow's indoor map rather dangerous to explore, especially when alone, as the Bracken can very easily sneak up on an employee.
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}}
<translate>
=== Nighttime === <!--T:23-->

<!--T:24-->
The [[Forest Keeper]] and [[Feiopar]] are by far the most common entity on Vow, with spawn rates of over 40% apiece. This makes the outdoor map of Vow one of the more dangerous areas. 
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}}
<translate>

=== Daytime === <!--T:25-->

<!--T:26-->
The [[Giant Sapsucker]] spawns on Vow much more often than any other moon. [[Circuit Bees|Bees]] are slightly more common than on [[Adamance]], but much harder to locate due to the larger map size. Due to the increased spawn chance of [[Forest Keeper|Forest Keepers]], searching far from the ship for hives or eggs should be limited to the early hours of the day if it must be done at all.
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/DaytimeEntities}}
<translate>
=== Spawn chance curves === <!--T:27-->

</translate>
[[File:Vow_spawn_curve.png|thumb]]
<translate>

<!--T:28-->
Nighttime entities start spawning at around 2pm on Vow with a rather large spawn deviation. Similarly to [[Assurance]], after 9pm entity spawning drastically increases with its peak at 12am. On Vow, daytime entities have an extended spawn timer at the beginning of the day before also gradually decreasing with time. See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation.

<!--T:29-->
== Data chips ==
There is 1 data chip on the surface: 

"[[Sigurd_Logs#12._Real_Job_-_Oct_1|Real Job]]" is on the support of the dam closest to the main entrance.

</translate>

</gallery>

Assurance.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox
| moon = Assurance
| image = Assurancemap.png
| routecost = 0
}}<translate>
<!--T:1-->
'''Assurance''' is the hardest of the easy [[Moons|moons]].  It can be slightly more dangerous than [[Vow]] and [[Experimentation]] but also offers the best loot out of the three. It has a hazard level of "C".

<!--T:24-->
The small map size and easily accessible fire exit make it the perfect choice for quick and efficient rounds.

== Description == <!--T:18-->
</translate>
{{Template:Terminal|text=220-Assurance

<nowiki>----------------------</nowiki>

<translate>
<!--T:19-->
CONDITIONS: Similar to its twin moon, 41-Experimentation, featuring far more jagged and weathered terrain.

<!--T:20-->
HISTORY: 220-Assurance is far younger than its counterpart. Discovered not long before 41-Experimentation.

<!--T:21-->
FAUNA: Unknown
</translate>}}
<translate>
== Environment == <!--T:2-->

<!--T:3-->
Assurance is a hilly desert moon with large rocks strewn about the environment. Random large machinery and pipes are laid over different sections of the rocks.

== Strategy == <!--T:4-->

<!--T:5-->
The optimal path to the main entrance is to head up the mildly sloped hill to the left of the ship or just jump on the big rock on the left during the ship's landing phase, then continue straight. When leaving, employees need to go around this hill instead, as the other side is too steep to climb. The [[Fire_exit|Fire Exit]] can be reached by climbing up the tall ladder inside the scaffolding in the front of the [[Ship]], then jumping off the big rock onto the large pipe. The Fire Exit is located at the end on the right side of the pipe.

== Loot table == <!--T:6-->
</translate>
{{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Assurance}}
{{special scrap spawn explainer|hives=y|facility=y|sapsuckers=y|nutcrackers=y}}

== Entities == <!--T:8-->

=== Indoor === <!--T:9-->

<!--T:10-->
[[Snare Flea]]s and [[Hoarding Bug]]s are the most common entity on Assurance. While not being the most dangerous entities, they can mess up a run pretty quickly if handled incorrectly. It is advised to bring a shovel to kill annoying snare fleas when playing multiplayer.
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}}
<translate>
=== Night time === <!--T:11-->

<!--T:12-->
[[Earth Leviathan|Worms]] are the biggest problem on Assurance. Together with [[Eyeless Dog]]s they create a deadly combo that can quickly erase entire crews if things get out of hand. It is not advised to visit this moon when [[Weather|eclipsed]] as the chances of both of them spawning are increased.
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}}
<translate>
=== Daytime === <!--T:13-->

<!--T:14-->
Although more common than on [[Experimentation]], [[Circuit Bees|Bees]] are also quite rare around here.
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/DaytimeEntities}}
<translate>
=== Spawn chance curves === <!--T:15-->

<!--T:16-->
Nighttime entities start spawning at around 3pm on Assurance with a rather large spawn deviation. Similarly to [[Experimentation]], after 10pm enemy spawning drastically increases with its peak at 12am. Daytime entity spawning gradually decreases with time with most of the entities spawning at the beginning of the game. See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation.
