Offense.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox | moon = Offense | image = Offense.png | dangerrate = B | routecost = 0 }} '''Offense''' is one of the more difficult [[Moons|moons]] in the game. It has a hazard level of "B", but tends to be much more dangerous than [[March]]. Offense is the only moon where the Fire Exit is not easily reachable. Offense offers the highest amount of loot of any free moon. == Description == {{Template:Terminal|text=21-Offense ---------------------- CONDITIONS: Believed to have splintered off from its cousin [[Assurance]], Offense features similar jagged and dry conditions but differs in its ecosystem. HISTORY: 21-Offense is categorized as an asteroid moon and seems to have not existed on its own for more than several hundred years. The industrial artifacts here have suffered damage; it's believed they were built long before 21-Offense was splintered off. FAUNA: A competitive and toughened ecosystem supports aggressive lifeforms. Travellers to 21-Offense should know it's not for the faint of heart.}} == Environment == Offense is a desert moon with steep hills and a lot of abandoned machinery. There is a large scaffolding in the middle of the map that connects both map parts. There are several scaffoldings and structures spread across the map. == Strategy == The fastest way to the main entrance is taking a slight right turn from the ship and following a straight line to the rocks. Once at the rocks, go through the small opening and hug the rock on the left. Follow the narrow path up the hill and turn right. The ladder to the main entrance is slightly downwards on the right. Employees can also climb the large rock on the right and then parkour onto the scaffolding if there are enemy entities in the opening between the rocks. To get to the fire exit, employees need to own an [[Extension Ladder]] or jump onto the large pipe that stretches across the map during the landing phase on the moon. Once employees master this jump, it can drastically increase the amount of loot and efficiency to get the loot back to the ship as the pipe leads all the way from the fire exit to the ship. This also allows easy and safe transfer of scrap during eclipse as long as the employees don't fall from the pipe. === Weather === ==== Flooded ==== The flood on Offense starts below ground level and slowly creeps up to Y 5. It is advised to utilize the pipe strategy when visiting this moon when flooded in order to have enough time to gather enough loot and bring it back to the ship. The terrain will be flooded at around 3pm. ==== Eclipsed ==== Offense spawns at minimum 4 nighttime and 4 indoor entities at the start of the game. Although this can be very dangerous, when using the pipe strategy, nighttime entities can almost be completely avoided. == Loot table == {{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Offense}} {{special scrap spawn explainer|facility=y|nutcrackers=y}} == Entities == === Indoor === [[Thumper|Thumpers]] and [[Bunker Spider|Spiders]] are the most common indoor entity on Offense. It is advised to bring a [[Shovel]] as both of these entities can be killed with it in a few hits. {{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}} === Nighttime === [[Eyeless Dog|Eyeless Dogs]] are by far the most common threat on Offense. They can spawn pretty early and in large numbers. Due to the rather large occurrence of [[Baboon_Hawk|Baboon Hawks]], dogs and hawks can oftentimes be seen fighting and killing each other. {{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}} === Daytime === Offense is the only moon that does not support [[Circuit Bees|Bees]] and [[Roaming_Locusts|Roaming Locusts]]. [[Manticoil|Manticoils]] make up for the insects as they always spawn on Offense. {{Template:WikiAutomated/DaytimeEntities}} === Spawn chance curves === Nighttime entities start spawning at around 2pm on Offense. Contrary to easy moons such as [[Assurance]] or [[Vow]], the nighttime spawn rate steadily increases from that point on until it peaks at 12am. Similarly to [[Vow]], daytime entities have an extended spawn timer at the beginning of the day before also gradually decreasing with time. See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation. [[File:Offense_spawn_curve.png|950px]] == Data chips == There is 1 data chip on the surface:
"[[Sigurd_Logs#6._Goodbye_-_Sep_7|Goodbye]]" is near the chainlink fence farthest from the ship. {{navbox moons}}

March.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox | moon = March | image = March.png | routecost = 0 }} '''March''' is one of the three Intermediate [[Moon|moons]] together with its desert counterpart, [[Offense]], and its forested sister, [[Adamance]]. Both intermediate planets are quite similar on the inside with March having a larger map and [[Offense]] offering slightly more loot. Compared to Offense, March can spawn a large amount of bees but also has a more dangerous outdoor map due to its patches of water and [[Forest Giant|Forest Giants]]. It has a hazard level of "B". == Description == {{Template:Terminal|text=61-March ---------------------- CONDITIONS: March undergoes constant drizzling weather. Its terrain is more expansive. HISTORY: This moon is overlooked due to its twin moon, Vow. FAUNA: Unknown}} == Environment == March has a deep swampy landscape. It does not support rainy weather and does not visually have rain. The landscape is covered in pits of water, some shallow enough for employees to trudge through, and some deep enough to hold risk of drowning. Two waterfalls exist, one covering a fire exit, as well as a set of wooden stairs leading to it. March has three fire exits. == Strategy == The main entrance can be reached by following the wooden bridge in front of the ship, being careful to mind the gap in it or risk drowning. Exploring all 3 fire exits is ideal for maximizing collected scrap, as well as map traversal. [[Hive|Bee hive]] hunting is more dangerous than on other moons due to the restricted space, but is still valuable due to their spawn chance being the highest here. == Loot table == {{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|March}} {{special scrap spawn explainer|hives=y|facility=y|nutcrackers=y}} == Entities == === Indoor === [[Thumper]]s, [[Backwater Gunkfish]], and [[Bunker Spider]]s are the most common indoor entities on March. It is advised to bring a [[Shovel]] as both dangerous entities can be killed with it in a few hits. {{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}} === Nighttime === [[Forest Keeper]]s and [[Eyeless Dog]]s are the most common nighttime entity on March. The branching paths to and from the ship on March make it easier to sneak past Forest Keepers. {{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}} === Daytime === March is the moon with the highest chance of finding [[Circuit Bees|Bees]]. It is a viable strategy to go around and search for bees if employees are experienced in bringing them back to the ship without dying. {{Template:WikiAutomated/DaytimeEntities}} === Spawn chance curves === [[File:March_spawn_curve.png|thumb]] On March, nighttime entities start spawning at around 2pm. Contrary to easy moons such as [[Assurance]] or [[Vow]], the nighttime spawn rate steadily increases from that point on until it peaks at 12am. Between 9pm and 11pm the spawn rate first spikes up and then down momentarily. Similarly to [[Vow]], daytime entities have an extended spawn timer at the beginning of the day before also gradually decreasing with time. See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation. == Trivia == March is the only moon with multiple Fire Exits. March is the only moon that does not support the [[Mansion]] or the [[Mineshaft]] as its interior, only the [[Factory]]. Directly north of the ship, far out of bounds, there is a secret circle of large, stone [[Toilet paper|Teliots]]. It is difficult to reach this on foot before the ship leaves, and requires at least one [[Extension Ladder]] to get out of bounds. Using [[TZP-Inhalant]], the [[Cruiser]], or multiple [[jetpack]]s to move faster is helpful. == Data chips == {{Spoilers}} There is 1 data chip on the surface:
"[[Sigurd_Logs#5._Behind_The_Wall_-_Sep_4|Behind The Wall]]" is on a rock path, right from the main entrance and near the fire exit on an island. == {{section heading/Gallery}} == MarchRevamp1.png| An early preview of March's [[Version 80|v80]] revamp.{{cite Patreon|date=2025-11-14|slug=marching-on-143536002|title=Marching on!}} MarchRevamp2.png| Another early preview of March's v80 revamp. Teliothenge2.png| The circle of Teliots, from above. Teliothenge1.png| The circle of Teliots, from ground level. == {{section heading/References}} == {{navbox moons}}

Dine.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox | moon = Dine | image = MapOfDine.png | routecost = 600 }} '''Dine''' is one of the three snowy [[Moons|moons]] together with [[Rend]] and [[Titan]]. It has a hazard level of "S", which makes it the third highest ranked moon in Lethal Company. The cost to change the ship's orbit to Dine is 600 credits. Dine has also had a map update in the version 50 beta. == Description == {{Template:Terminal|text=7-Dine ---------------------- CONDITIONS: Its planet orbits white dwarf star, making for inhospitable, cold conditions. Constant blizzards decrease visibility. HISTORY: Several famous travelers went missing here, giving it some reputation. Their bodies are unlikely to be found due to the planet's conditions. FAUNA: It's highly unlikely for complex life to exist here.}} (This description is identical to [[Rend]]'s.) == Environment == Dine is a very snowy and rocky moon, orbiting a white dwarf star. The constant snowstorm is similar to Foggy weather on other moons, except it's less dense. The snowstorm not only makes it harder to see but also muffles voices and entity sounds. The lights unlike on [[Rend]] do not lead directly to or from the main entrance but instead are a dead end. Dine and its snowy counterpart [[Rend]] both have the [[Mansion]] as their default indoor layout, although the [[Mineshaft]] is also quite common and there is a very rare chance to have the [[Factory]] spawn instead. == Strategy == The main entrance is slightly further away and is positioned almost directly ahead and slightly to the right of the ship's exit, albeit behind a lot of very rough and hilly terrain. Because of the large hills, there are multiple paths that can be taken to reach the main entrance but the best passes directly through a fence in the center of the map. The fire exit is located slightly to the left when leaving the ship and is closer than the main entrance. === Weather === ==== Flooded ==== Although Dine is in a constant snow-storm, it is possible for it to flood. The flood starts off blocking both entrances, but the water drains throughout the day, similarly to [[Adamance]]. Main entrance is still possible to reach and enter before drowning. The fire exit is easily accessible at any time with a [[Jetpack]]. ==== Eclipsed ==== Dine spawns at minimum all nighttime and 4 indoor entities at the start of the game. This makes it extremely dangerous to land on Dine when eclipsed and employees are strongly discouraged to do so. To deal with the immediate [[Forest Keeper|Forest Keepers]] that spawn in, it's advised to hug close to the hilly terrain and to cut corners quickly to lose their chase. == Loot table == {{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Dine}} {{special scrap spawn explainer|facility=y|nutcrackers=y|butlers=y}} == Entities == === Indoor === The most common threat on Dine is the [[Butler]], you will usually find several spawned in the facility at around the time you manage out your first trip of scrap. {{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}} === Nighttime === [[Eyeless Dog|Eyeless Dogs]] and [[Forest Keeper|Forest Keepers]] are the most common nighttime entities on Dine. In a similar fashion to [[Rend]], if multiple nighttime entities spawned together, they can pose a significant threat to employee safety if not handled correctly. To best manage, it's advised to sneak around the hilly terrain and to keep the sprint bar high for quick dashes away from forest keepers if they manage to lock onto you. {{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}} === Daytime === Dine does not support daytime entities. === Spawn chance curves === On Dine, nighttime entities start spawning at around 3pm. This rate steadily increases until it peaks at 12am. Indoor entities spawn in a linear fashion on Dine with two smooth peaks at the beginning and at the end of the day. Daytime entities are unable to spawn. See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation. [[File:Dine_spawn_curve.png|950px]] == Data chips == {{spoilers}} There is 1 data chip on the surface:
"[[Sigurd_Logs#13._Desmond_-_Oct_7|Desmond]]" is on top of the ledge above the fire exit. {{navbox moons}}

Rend.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox
| moon = Rend
| image = Rend Map.png
| routecost = 550
}}<translate>
<!--T:1-->
'''Rend''' is one of the three snowy [[moons]] together with [[Dine]] and [[Titan]]. It has a hazard level of "A" and is a substantial step up in difficulty from the moons that can be routed to freely. The cost to change the ship's orbit to Rend is 550 credits.

== Description == <!--T:10-->
</translate>
{{Template:Terminal|text=85-Rend

<nowiki>----------------------</nowiki>

<translate>
<!--T:11-->
CONDITIONS: Its planet orbits white dwarf star, making for inhospitable, cold conditions. Constant blizzards decrease visibility.

<!--T:12-->
HISTORY: Several famous travelers went missing here, giving it some reputation. Their bodies are unlikely to be found due to the planet's conditions.

<!--T:13-->
FAUNA: It's highly unlikely for complex life to exist here.
</translate>}}
<translate>
<!--T:27-->
(This description is identical to [[Dine]]'s.)

<!--T:2-->
== Environment ==
Rend is a very snowy and rocky moon, orbiting a white dwarf star. The constant snowstorm is similar to Foggy weather on other moons, except it's less dense. The snowstorm not only makes it harder to see but also muffles voices and entity sounds. Lights  are set up that lead directly to the main entrance. Rend and it's snowy counterpart [[Dine]] both have the [[Mansion]] as their default indoor layout.

<!--T:3-->
== Strategy ==
The walk from the ship to the main entrance is long, and it's not hard to get lost in the snow storm. It is important to check  your time to make sure you don't get caught off guard by nighttime creatures. It may prove helpful to leave someone at the ship with a [[Walkie-talkie]] as mansions are quite difficult to traverse, along with this map's constant snowstorms. The fire exit can be accessed without an extension ladder but is quite far away and it is very easy to lose track of the ship on the way back.

=== Weather === <!--T:25-->

<!--T:28-->
Rend can have stormy or eclipsed weather.

==== Eclipsed ==== <!--T:26-->

<!--T:4-->
Rend spawns at minimum 3 nighttime and 3 indoor entities at the start of the game.

=== Indoor === <!--T:15-->

<!--T:16-->
The [[Nutcracker]] is the most common indoor entity on Rend followed by the [[Jester]]. Both these entities are very dangerous and extreme caution is advised when encountering them. The Nutcracker can be killed when executed correctly whereas the Jester just has to be avoided.

[[Eyeless Dog]]s and [[Forest Keeper]]s are the most common nighttime entities on Rend. If spawned together, they can pose a significant threat to employee safety if not handled correctly.
</translate>

=== Daytime ===
Rend does not support daytime entities.

<!--T:9-->
=== Spawn chance curves ===
On Rend, nighttime entities start spawning at around 3pm. This rate  peaks at 12am. Indoor entities spawn in a linear fashion on Rend, with a steady upwards trend. Daytime entities are unable to spawn despite the curve's constant multiplier of 1 (This is how the game defines it). See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation.

"[[Sigurd_Logs#Nonsense_-_Sep_27|Nonsense]]" can be found by climbing out of the fire exit chasm, moving forward past the lamppost, then following the edge of the pit, then following the cliff walls into the clearing.

Titan.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox
| moon = Titan
| image = Titanmap.png
| routecost = 700
}}<translate>
<!--T:3-->
'''Titan''' is the second hardest moon in the game with a hazard level of "S+". The small map size and high nighttime entity spawn chances make it extremely dangerous to navigate the moon even early in the day. Despite that, Titan offers the largest loot amount and value with a whopping maximum of <tvar name="spawnMaximum">37</tvar> scrap pieces that can be found. Titan has by far the largest indoor map size.

== Description == <!--T:4-->
</translate>
{{Template:Terminal|text=8-Titan

<nowiki>----------------------</nowiki>

<translate>
<!--T:5-->
CONDITIONS: A frozen, flat landscape.

<!--T:6-->
HISTORY: It looks like this moon was mined for resources. It's easy to get lost within the giant industrial complex. There are many entrances to it littered about the landscape.

<!--T:7-->
FAUNA: Dangerous entities have been rumored to take residence in the vast network of tunnels.</translate>}}
<translate>
== Environment == <!--T:8-->

<!--T:9-->
Titan is a moon with heavy snowfall with a very small outdoor area. The ship lands with its doors facing directly in front of two sets of multiple flights of stairs which are used to access the main entrance. There are a second set of stairs that offer quicker access to Titan's fire exit, although the fire exit shares a landing with the Main Entrance which can also be accessed from the first set of stairs. It can also be used as an escape as entities such as [[Eyeless Dog|Eyeless Dogs]] can walk up the stairs.

== Strategy == <!--T:10-->

<!--T:11-->
Titan is a high-risk, high-reward moon where players must collect and secure as much scrap as possible before indoor and nighttime entities flood the map. Employees are advised not to go too far into the indoor maze as the large indoor map size makes it very easy to get lost which is the last thing you want on Titan.
</translate>
[[File:Titan route annotations.jpg|thumb|<translate><!--T:25--> Route annotations</translate>]]
<translate>
<!--T:26-->
Employees can quickly reach the Main Entrance by using a [[Jetpack]] over the stairs. This can also be performed before the ship lands by jumping off the ship platform as soon as the door opens, activating the jetpack, and leaning forward to direct yourself towards the entrance. It is worth noting that you can take significant fall [[damage]] or die if you direct it poorly and end up too high or deactivate the jetpack too early/late. Despite the risk, this is an efficient method of reaching the Main Entrance quickly and avoiding any hazardous [[weather]] while reaching it.

<!--T:12-->
Employees can quickly transport loot from the entrances to the ship by dropping items off of the edge. Using an [[Extension Ladder]]<sup>(2)</sup> allows employees to drop objects to ground level instead of landing on the secondary platform. Loitering at the main entrance or fire exit is strongly discouraged as [[Eyeless Dog]]s and [[Forest Keeper]]s are capable of climbing the stairs<sup>(1)</sup> killing employees. Employees can also quickly scale down the stairs without taking fall damage<sup>(3)</sup> by dropping onto the stair railing, then dropping onto a small lip in the wall below the edge, dropping onto the secondary platform, and finally falling off the railing on the lower set of stairs onto the ground.

== Loot table == <!--T:13-->
</translate>
{{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Titan}}
{{special scrap spawn explainer|facility=y|nutcrackers=y}}
<translate>
== Entities == <!--T:14-->

=== Indoor === <!--T:15-->

<!--T:16-->
[[Jester]]s are the most common entities on Titan. It is advised to keep your visits to Titan short and efficient as Jesters easily can force employees to leave early if they spawn in the wrong places.
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}}
<translate>

=== Nighttime === <!--T:17-->

<!--T:18-->
Just like on [[Rend]], [[Eyeless Dog]]s and [[Forest Keeper]]s are the most common nighttime entities on Titan. If spawned together, they can pose a significant threat to employee safety if not handled correctly. [[Earth Leviathan]] can spawn too but pose little to no threat as the ship is very close to the entrance.
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}}
<translate>
=== Daytime === <!--T:19-->

<!--T:20-->
Titan does not support daytime entities. 

=== Spawn chance curves === <!--T:21-->

<!--T:22-->
The nighttime spawn curve is almost equal to [[Dine|Dine's]] with the only difference being the map size as on Titan, all entities spawn in a very small area. Indoor entities start spawning at around 8am with a more or less steady rate increase until 12am. See [[Mechanics]] for interpretation.
</translate>
[[File:Titan_spawn_curve.png|950px]]
<translate>

== Data chips == <!--T:27-->
</translate>
{{spoilers}}
<translate>
<!--T:28-->
There is 1 data chip on the surface:

<!--T:29-->
"[[Sigurd_Logs#15._Letter_of_Resignation_-_Oct_13|Letter of Resignation]]" is sitting on a ledge to the left of the fire exit.


== Trivia == <!--T:23-->
</translate>
* <translate><!--T:24--> Titan is also the name of a [[w:Titan_(moon)|real-life moon]] that orbits Saturn.</translate>

{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{Template:Direct Quote}}
{{Infobox entity|id=BaboonHawk
| name = Baboon Hawk
| sciname = Papio-volturius
| danger = 75%
| pip = Medium
| power_level = 0.5
| diversity_level = 1
| max_count = 15
| see_through_fog = No
| spawn_delay = 15s
| pip_size = Medium
| attack_damage = 20
| attack_speed = 500ms 
| dps = 40
| hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|BaboonHawk}}
| stun_multiplier = 0.4
| cause_of_death = Unknown
}}
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
The '''Baboon Hawk''' is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. It exhibits fiercely territorial behavior and becomes more aggressive if it has the company of others, or when employees are intruding on it or its territory. It also hoards [[scrap]] from its environment and attempts to bite or skewer employees upon feeling threatened. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Adamance]].

<!--T:50-->
Baboon Hawks are rotund, bipedal entities with pale green fur, short legs, bat-like wings with a thin membrane, and a mane of reddish-brown hair on their backs. They have skeletal, bird-like heads, with bulging red eyes, a beak with a pink tongue and a row of tiny, black fanged teeth, and a pair of sharp horns growing from their cheeks.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:15-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|BaboonHawk}}
<translate>
== Spawn chances == <!--T:49-->
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>
<!--T:20-->
Baboon Hawks predominantly spawn on [[Adamance]].

<!--T:21-->
=== Spawn probability ===
Baboon Hawks always have the same spawn chance multiplier throughout the day but their spawning is affected by spawn count falloff. Meaning, they have a modified chance of spawning depending on how many Baboon Hawks are already on the map.

<!--T:11-->
Learn more about spawn probability on [[Mechanics]].
</translate>

[[File:Baboon_hawk_probability.png]]
<translate>
== Mechanics == <!--T:28-->

<!--T:29-->
Baboon Hawks have a fear level that determines whether they attack employees within their line of sight. This level is influenced by a number of factors.

<!--T:30-->
Baboon Hawks are '''more''' fearful when:
</translate>
* <translate><!--T:31--> they see dead Baboon Hawks</translate>
* <translate><!--T:32--> there are multiple employees near each other</translate>
* <translate><!--T:33--> the employee is holding a weapon<tvar name="note"><ref group="note" name="weapons" /></tvar></translate>
* <translate><!--T:34--> the employee is looking at the Baboon Hawk</translate>
* <translate><!--T:35--> it is before 10:24 AM</translate>
* <translate><!--T:36--> the employee is in the ship</translate>
* <translate><!--T:37--> the employee's HP is greater or equal to 30</translate>
* <translate><!--T:38--> there are less than 4 Baboon Hawks in their group</translate>
* <translate><!--T:39--> the employee uses certain noise-making items<tvar name="note"><ref group="note" name="noisemakers" /></tvar></translate>
<translate>
<!--T:40-->
Baboon Hawks are '''less''' fearful when:
</translate>
* <translate><!--T:41--> the employee's HP is less than 30</translate>
* <translate><!--T:42--> there are more than 4 Baboon Hawks in their group</translate>
* <translate><!--T:43--> the employee is not looking at the Baboon Hawk</translate>
* <translate><!--T:44--> it is after 9:36 PM</translate>
* <translate><!--T:45--> the employee is far away from the ship</translate>
* <translate><!--T:46--> the Baboon Hawk is close to its nest</translate>
<translate>
<!--T:5-->
Before attacking, Baboon Hawks will yell and flail at players as a warning, while staying a certain distance away. If ignored and the factors in the previous paragraph are met, they will attack.
</translate>
[[File:Baboon Hawk Territory.png|thumb|250px|<translate><!--T:27--> The Baboon Hawk's "predetermined territory".</translate>]]
<translate>
<!--T:6-->
Baboon Hawks have a predetermined territory, marked by 3 spikes in a triangular formation. When one either kills a player or steals an item, they will return to it and drop whatever they have.

<!--T:47-->
Each Baboon Hawk has a randomized leadership value that determines which Baboon Hawk leads the group. The higher this value is, the larger the Baboon Hawk is, meaning that the largest Baboon Hawk always leads the group.

<!--T:16-->
When undisturbed, they tend to partake in minor activities such as exploring or sitting. During night while at their base, Baboon Hawks will go inactive and sleep.

<!--T:8-->
== Strategy ==
Try to make trips to/from [[Ship|The Ship]] in groups, whenever possible. Baboon Hawks will usually stay a decent way away from you before attacking, so boarding the ship and closing the doors will get them to back off or buy you some time at least.

<!--T:9-->
Making [[Audible_sounds|noise]] will attract Baboon Hawks in your direction if they are not more interested in something else.

<!--T:17-->
When fighting Baboon Hawks, it is never recommended to engage more than one, and even then only fight if you have to. Strafing them with a shovel by running in, getting a hit, and retreating is the most effective attack strategy due to their low mobility. Do not let them come near you though, as they quickly tear through employees hp.

<!--T:48-->
Baboon Hawks back off when stared at by employees, maintaining a gap between themselves and the employee. However, they will eventually go into an attacking state if watched for too long
</translate>

Thumper.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{Infobox entity
| name = <translate><!--T:8--> Thumper</translate>

}}
<translate>
<!--T:9-->
The '''Thumper''', also known as the "Half" or internally as the "Crawler", is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It is incapable of hearing and relies on eyesight to hunt down and attack employees, rapidly accelerating when moving and decelerating when turning corners or upon hitting a wall. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Offense]].

Thumpers are bipedal entities with beige skin, consisting of a shark-like head, black eyes, a mouth with sharp teeth and a pink tongue, and three other separate mouths wrapping around their head. Their bodies only consist of a broad chest and long arms ending in stubby, bloodstained hands.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:10-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary}}
<translate>

<!--T:16-->
== Spawn chances ==
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}

<!--T:3-->
== Mechanics ==
Thumpers patrol the facility at a steady pace. They are deaf and rely entirely on sight. After spotting an employee they will pursue them and investigate their last seen areas to find them again if line of sight is broken. Thumpers steadily gain speed while moving and lose some speed when turning. If a Thumper is moving fast enough to hit a wall, it will lose most of its speed in doing so. They can reach speeds faster than any employee can under the right conditions. When a Thumper reaches its target, they will deliver strong and rapid bites until the target is dead.

<!--T:4-->
== Strategy ==
Thumpers are quick and dangerous. They take 4 [[Shovel]] hits to kill and kills an employee in 4 quick bites. They are very fast, steadily gaining speed as they move forward, and lose some speed when turning. If handled poorly, they will reach speeds faster than can be outrun by any means. Taking sharp turns around many corners will significantly aid escape attempts. [[Stun grenade]]s and [[Zap gun]]s can be useful if any are on hand. With good enough execution, an employee can run around a Thumper in a circle, leaving them helpless to attacks.

<!--T:6-->
You can also kill them with a single shovel with enough practice. Be warned that hitting a thumper will increase its speed slightly, which means it will make it harder to kill as the fight progresses. Try fighting the thumper by circling it or baiting it into walls, as these two methods help balance its speed increases.

== History == <!--T:5-->

<!--T:13-->
There used to be a bug that let you simply stand on railings to avoid a thumper's attack, but this was fixed in update 45. Before update 45, thumpers only lost a reasonable amount of speed when turning completely around, letting them get extremely fast, up to around 4x an employee's normal speed.

<!--T:14-->
After update 47, thumpers will now lose significant speed when running into walls. Due to the fact thumpers do not have very fast turning speed, this addition makes thumpers much easier to escape when not armed with a shovel or sign. Before update 47, thumpers would move at a crawl when starting its first chase, and if the thumper cannot find the employee they will continue to gain speed as they are searching for them. After update 47, thumpers now have a higher minimum starting speed, and now if they lose an employee, they will not gain anymore speed looking for them.
</translate>

[[Category:Entities]]

Hoarding Bug.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{Infobox entity|id=HoarderBug
| name = <translate><!--T:18--> Hoarding Bug</translate>
| sciname = Linepithema-crassus
| danger = 17%
| power_level = 1
| diversity_level = 1
| max_spawned = 10
| pip_size = [[File:RadarPipSize_HoardingBug.svg|50px]]
| door_speed_multiplier = 1.5
| hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|HoarderBug}}
| stun_multiplier = 0.3
| attack_damage = 30
| cause_of_death = Mauling
}}
{{Template:Direct Quote}}
<translate>
<!--T:21-->
The '''Hoarding Bug''', colloquially known as the "Lootbug", is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. Often seen roaming the facility in large colonies, it collects and carries [[scrap]] to a specific location it considers as a nest. It becomes aggressive towards employees if their territory is intruded or their scrap is taken from them. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Embrion]].

Hoarding Bugs have olive-green exoskeletons, brown inner bodies, and two pairs of legs. Their heads have a pair of large, round red compound eyes, long dark brown antennae, and small mouths with two sharp, tiny teeth. Their backs have three pairs of shorter antennae and two pairs of wings.

== Bestiary Entry == <!--T:1-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|HoardingBug}}
<translate>

<!--T:23-->
== Spawn chances ==
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}

<!--T:24-->
There is a 2.63% chance for a 'Hoarding Bug [[infestation]]' as of [[v65]]. This lowers to a 1.2% chance during the Anniversary period (October 23rd).

== Mechanics == <!--T:4-->

<!--T:5-->
Hoarding Bugs do not attack employees unless an employee gets too close to its nest or if an employee takes any "claimed" scrap. They search the map alongside employees for items, including scrap, tools, and player corpses, to collect at their nest. All Hoarding Bugs on a moon share one nesting site, unless there is a locked door preventing the Hoarding Bugs from sharing. 

<!--T:25-->
=== States ===
Hoarding Bugs have 3 possible states: roaming, guarding, and attacking.

<!--T:26-->
==== Roaming ====
The Hoarding Bug wanders around the map, searching areas for scrap. If it sees an item, it will beeline to pick it up and carry it back to a predetermined nesting site, then enter their guarding state for 15 seconds before returning to roaming.

<!--T:27-->
==== Guarding ====
The Hoarding Bug sits motionless on top of its nesting site to guard its items. It also enters this phase if it sees or hears an employee too close to its nest, and will not return to roaming until all employees are far enough away.

<!--T:28-->
==== Attacking ====
The Hoarding Bug makes a loud, angry chirp and lifts off of the ground to chase an employee. There are 3 subphases of the attacking state:

<!--T:29-->
===== "Soft" =====
Triggered when an employee gets too close to a nest without stealing anything, or stays too close to a roaming bug for too long. This can also be triggered if the Hoarding Bug sees you with an item. The bug will give chase for a short period, and return to being docile once enough distance is made.

<!--T:30-->
===== "Hard" =====
Triggered by hitting a Hoarding Bug. The bug will chase the employee as long as it can see them, and will only let up after killing them or after about 15 seconds of not being able to find them. Once this phase is over, it will return to its guarding state for 15 seconds, and will make a low, angry buzzing sound for that time; if it notices the employee while buzzing, it will return to hard attacking. If not, it will return to regular roaming.

<!--T:31-->
===== "Super" =====
Triggered by taking an item from a Hoarding Bug's nest. The same as hard attacking, except the Hoarding bug will never calm down. If an item is voided (such as a key being used to unlock a door, an egg exploding, or a [[Maneater]] eating an item), then the Hoarding Bug will never calm down.

== Strategy == <!--T:7-->

<!--T:8-->
In most cases, Hoarding Bugs should be killed as soon as possible, to avoid scrap being taken far away or having to face several at once later. One bug can be killed relatively easily by hitting it once, then strafing to avoid its attack and hitting it two more times as it flies by; they are quite slow to turn. One or multiple can be taken out at once with one shotgun shot, if available. 

<!--T:13-->
Employees should drop all [[scrap]] outside to prevent it being stolen by bugs, though this may result in [[Baboon Hawk|Baboon Hawks]] taking it instead on some moons. Keep in mind, however, that Hoarding Bugs can sometimes help; if the bugs' nest is close to an exit, employees may want to leave them to their devices, then kill the bugs and take the scrap later in the day.

== Trivia == <!--T:32-->

<!--T:33-->
* Hoarding Bugs have always had 3 HP. In previous versions, it seemed to be 2 HP due to a glitch where shovels and signs hit the bug multiple times in one swing.

== History == <!--T:16-->
</translate>

[[Category:Entities]]

Jester.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{Template:Direct Quote}}
{{Infobox entity
| name = Jester
| sciname = INSANEUS THINGUS
| danger = 90%
| power_level = 3
| diversity_level = 1
| max_count = 1
| door_speed_multiplier = 0.5
| latest_time = 12:00 AM
| pip_size = Medium
| attack_damage = Instant Kill
| hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|Jester}}
| stun_multiplier = 0.6
| id=jester
| cause_of_death = Mauling
}}<translate>
<!--T:14-->
The '''Jester''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. Initially, it passively searches for and follows a nearby employee before it randomly stops in place and starts cranking. This causes it to play a tune that becomes increasingly more frantic before it transforms into an extremely aggressive threat that hunts down every remaining employee left in the facility. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Rend]].

<!--T:15-->
Jesters resemble large [[w:Jack-in-the-box|jack-in-the-boxes]] with pale, doll-like limbs, consisting of a pair of legs and and a single left arm, above which is a thin crank. The box has a purple lining and a pink checkerboard pattern with red outlines. Each side of the box is decorated with a large yellow circle and a faint image of a baby embossed onto its center, with its face scratched out. The "jack" inside the Jester appears to be a large human skull attached to the inside of the box by a large neck made of flesh, veins, and muscle.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:16-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary}}
<translate>

<!--T:34-->
== Spawn chances ==
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}

== Mechanics == <!--T:17-->

<!--T:3-->
The Jester has four possible states: roaming, following, cranking, and chasing.

=== Roaming === <!--T:18-->

<!--T:4-->
Upon spawning, the Jester will roam and wander aimlessly, simultaneously initiating a random timer varying from 13 to 21 seconds (25 to 42 seconds in singleplayer), determining the duration before it switches to its cranking state.

=== Following === <!--T:19-->

<!--T:5-->
The Jester will roam until it detects an employee in its line of sight, where it will then continuously follow them unless a different employee is closer. While following, the Jester decreases its cranking timer by 1 every second, decreasing 15 times faster while stunned.

=== Cranking === <!--T:20-->

<!--T:6-->
Once the timer reaches 0, the Jester begins to crank its box while playing a distorted rendition of [[w:Pop_Goes_the_Weasel|Pop Goes the Weasel]] for a randomly specified amount time, varying from 35 to 40 seconds. The song will get faster and faster as the Jester gets closer to popping, though it will stop (for a moment) if stunned. If it is stunned while in the roaming or following state, it begins cranking after being stunned. While cranking, the Jester cannot move.

=== Chasing === <!--T:21-->

<!--T:7-->
Once the timer reaches 0, the Jester will pop and proceed to rush towards the closest employee, killing them upon contact. The Jester will gain speed while it is chasing someone, with its current speed being reset every time it kills an employee. It is able to detect employees regardless of range or line of sight, quickly narrowing the distance to them, and it persists in this "angered" state as long as any employee remains inside. When there are no living employees left in the building, it returns to its roaming state.

== Strategy == <!--T:22-->

<!--T:10-->
Employees should establish an escape route out of the building when a Jester is discovered, and follow this route as soon as it enters its cranking state. Employees can safely re-enter the building once 5 seconds have passed after the last living employee is removed from the building, as the Jester will have reverted to its roaming state.

<!--T:32-->
Due to Jesters' target selection, an employee standing on top of Mansions' main entrance doors will be targeted last by the Jester during its chase state. If the Jester makes its way towards an employee standing on top of these doors, it means there are no other employees in the building. This employee should then exit and inform the remaining crew, and the building can again be entered after the 5-second timer expires.

<!--T:11-->
Employees should go to great lengths to prevent the Jester from entering its cranking state while it is standing inside important doorways, as it will completely block passage until it begins chasing. If a following Jester is obstructing a doorway, the employee with its attention should drag it away and loop back around to safely escape. If it is already cranking and the doorway has a door, opening and closing the door might push it, too.

<!--T:23-->
It is possible to stand on top of a Jester's box while it is not chasing, and this will keep employees out of range of most other creatures. However, the Jester is able to crank as usual, and it will instantly kill any employee standing on its box when it pops.

<!--T:12-->
Using a [[Zap gun]] on the Jester while it is cranking will result in it being temporarily paused, buying other employees more time to make an escape. In addition, any sort of stun will remove all momentum that a chasing Jester has obtained, making equipment like the [[Radar-booster]] invaluable as it can completely stunlock the Jester with good timing.

<!--T:24-->
Standing on top of a bookshelf or other high place that must be jumped to will break the Jester's pathfinding. It cannot jump or climb, and will choose other targets in the building. If the only living employees in the building were out of reach and another employee enters the building, the Jester will attack this new target first. 

<!--T:25-->
Employees can easily trap the Jester if the crew is in the Facility and a Secure Door is available. One employee can lure the Jester into a dead end with a Secure Door and stand on the outside of the hallway, while another employee at the ship closes the Secure Door, trapping the Jester. This is rather effective since there can only be one Jester per day. However, the Jester will not revert from its chasing state even when trapped in this way so long as living employees remain in the building, so actions such as unplugging the Apparatus should be taken with care; the resulting power outage will open the Secure Door and release the chasing Jester.

<!--T:33-->
The Jesters speed is based on how far away the an employee is. So if you were walking while the jester's behind you, it will be in walking speed as the employee.
</translate>

Earth Leviathan.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{Template:Direct Quote}}
{{Infobox entity|id=SandWorm
| name = <translate><!--T:33--> Earth Leviathan</translate>
| sciname = Hemibdella-gigantis

| max_spawned = 3

| cause_of_death = Unknown
}}
<translate>
<!--T:38-->
The '''Earth Leviathan''', also known as the "Sandworm", is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. Burrowing deep within the ground, it tracks the movements of nearby employees before suddenly erupting from the ground, instantly consuming anything in its path. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Assurance]].

<!--T:39-->
Earth Leviathans are extremely large, leech-like entities which resemble [[w:Sandworm (Dune)|sandworms]]. They have tan, parched skin composed of large, ringed segments that end in a large, toothless, sucker-shaped mouth, where darker brown flesh can be seen within its interior.

== Bestiary Entry == <!--T:5-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|EarthLeviathan}}
<translate>

== Spawn chances == <!--T:37-->
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>

== Mechanics == <!--T:8-->

<!--T:9-->
'''Earth Leviathans''' will spawn during [[Entities#Nighttime_(Outdoor)|nighttime]] on [[moons]] in which they appear, burrowed in the ground. They will roam around underground looking for a target, erupting after a short delay when a player is above them. An attack from an Earth Leviathan is always fatal and leaves no body to recover or [[Teleporter|Teleport]] back. Held items will also be consumed, but dropped items will be unaffected and will remain there.

<!--T:14-->
Earth Leviathans have two possible behavioral states, as described below:

=== Roaming === <!--T:15-->

<!--T:16-->
Earth Leviathans will roam the map, seeking players at a speed of 4 units per second. When a player is not in close proximity (10 units) to the ship, and is within 15 units of an Earth Leviathan, it targets the player and switches to attack mode.</translate><ref name="sc_updating">[https://pastebin.com/HeU1F7wd SandwormAI - Updating]</ref>
<translate>
=== Attacking === <!--T:17-->

<!--T:18-->
Earth Leviathans will keep chasing the targeted employee, and will emerge from the ground under specific conditions.

==== Chase timer ==== <!--T:19-->

<!--T:20-->
During this state, Earth Leviathans maintain an internal chase timer. If the targeted employee meets '''all''' the following conditions:<ref name="sc_updating"/>

<!--T:21-->
* Is reachable to the Earth Leviathan,
* Is outside of the ship,
* Is within 22 units of the Earth Leviathan,

<!--T:22-->
the timer will reset to 0. Otherwise, it will count the time until the Earth Leviathan abandons the chase. Additionally, the timer also resets if the Earth Leviathan changes its behavioral state.<ref name="sc_updating"/>

<!--T:23-->
If the chase timer exceeds 6 seconds, <u>or the target employee is more than 19 units away from the Earth Leviathan</u><sup>[unverified]</sup>, it returns to roaming mode. Otherwise, when '''all''' four of the following conditions are met:

<!--T:24-->
* The chase timer is less than 1.5 seconds,
* The target employee is within 4 units of the Earth Leviathan,
* The Earth Leviathan is not within 9 units of ship,
* Successfully passes a 17% chance,

<!--T:25-->
the Earth Leviathan will emerge from the ground.<ref name="sc_updating"/>

==== Emerging ==== <!--T:26-->

<!--T:27-->
When the Earth Leviathan is about to emerge, an animation of dust particles rising from the ground begins to play around its emerge position. After a random interval between 1s to 3s, the Earth Leviathan emerges <tvar name="ref_sc_emerging"><ref name="sc_emerging">[https://pastebin.com/LhmdCn86 SandwormAI - Emerging]</ref></tvar>, killing any employee or killable entity in its path during its emergence <tvar name="ref_sc_oncollide"><ref name="sc_oncollide">[https://pastebin.com/e7Ccxxze SandwormAI - OnCollide]</ref></tvar>.

<!--T:28-->
10 seconds after it returns to the ground<ref name="sc_emerging"/>, the Earth Leviathan will reset its status, switching back to its roaming state.

== Strategy == <!--T:10-->

<!--T:11-->
The presence of an Earth Leviathan can be ascertained by listening for underground groaning sounds, and its attack is telegraphed by a distinct low growl as particles of dirt jump out of the ground. An Earth Leviathan cannot be killed and is present on every moon, so caution should be taken during nighttime when roaming outside. Avoid staying too close to your teammates, as one can kill your whole team at once if it catches you off guard. Keep some distance to minimize casualties, but stay close enough to warn each other if you notice something hostile. If necessary, use a Walkie Talkie, the Signal Translator, and the ship's Radar in order to warn your team of an Earth Leviathan before it gets the chance to strike.

<!--T:12-->
Despite the Earth Leviathan's bestiary entry advising the employee to "retrace your steps", this strategy rarely results in surviving an attack and often places you directly in the middle of its hitbox. Often times, when it begins growling, you are already at risk of being eaten. Instead, it is advised to drop any items you are carrying and run towards any area elevated from the ground. Examples of this include (but are not limited to): The ship, any ladders, large structures, or pipes. Entering the interior of the moon will also work, but the worm may still be at the door that you entered when you exit. If you cannot run towards one of these areas, dropping your items and running straight forwards will also help you survive.

<!--T:35-->
A very easy way to counter the worm is drop all items once you hear the first roar, walk around, then sprint as soon as the clouds of smoke arise.
</translate>

{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{autoinfobox item

<translate>
<!--T:3-->
The '''Apparatus''' (or, "Apparatice") is a special [[Scrap]] that can only spawn in one of 4 possible locations in the [[Facility]] interior. It resembles a glowing power cell,  provides energy to the interior, and has worn-out text on its underside reading "Model: GJH-851 #458agf-6" (with the "-" symbolizing a character that's too faded to be legible). Despite its value being shown as "???" in-game, it will always be worth {{credits|80}}.

<!--T:16-->
Unplugging the Apparatus from its machine will permanently disable the facility's power, making navigation more difficult: all lights turn off, all secure doors are forced open and can't be closed anymore. However, [[Traps|Turrets]], [[Traps|Landmines]] and [[Spike Trap|Spike Traps]] still function and can be disabled from the [[Terminal]] as usual. The only way to temporarily bring light back to the facility is by using a [[Zap gun]] to shock the Facility's [[Interiors#Breaker panel|Breaker box]], which will cause all lights to flicker for a moment when the electric current starts & stops. 

<!--T:4-->
Additionally, collecting the Apparatus has a 70% chance of increasing the interior enemy spawn rate (spawning 2 monsters minimum every "spawn wave") until you leave the moon. Unplugging the Apparatus will also instantly activate any dormant [[Old Bird]] outside the facility. This means that pulling the Apparatus early in your run can make things significantly harder.

<!--T:5-->
Once removed, it cannot be reinserted - it is best salvaged last, with an escape route planned out beforehand.

<!--T:6-->
Its properties can be used to open secure doors if opening them via the [[Computer Terminal|Terminal]] is inconvenient.

<!--T:17-->
== Possible spawning locations ==
There are 2 rooms in the [[Facility]] that are able to generate the machine containing an Apparatus, with each room having 2 possible spawning configurations:
</translate><br clear="all" /><translate>

Ghost Girl.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=DressGirl | name = Ghost Girl | power_level = 2 | diversity_level = 1 | altname = Dress Girl | pip = None | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|DressGirl}} | cause_of_death = Unknown }} {{Template:Spoilers}} The '''Ghost Girl''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. Depending on a number of unique factors, it targets and haunts a specific employee for an extended period of time, before eventually chasing after the targeted employee and instantly killing them upon contact by decapitation. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Rend]]. The Ghost Girl appears as a young girl with deathly pale skin, long brown hair, and an uncannily stern facial expression without eyebrows. She also wears a bright red dress with a floral checkerboard pattern. == Bestiary Entry == Cannot be scanned. == Spawn chances == {{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == When the Ghost Girl first spawns, it checks every employee's 'insanity level' and 'total turn amount'. Whichever employee has the highest of these two stats is saved and used later. === Insanity Level === An employee's insanity level slowly increases over time, affected by a scaling multiplier. Lone employees will gain insanity faster in certain locations, whereas sticking together with others (or listening to them over a [[Walkie-talkie]]) will reduce insanity. Additionally, the record player, a purchasable cosmetic item, provides the same effect as the radio, making it the only cosmetic item to provide a useful benefit. === Total Turn Amount === An employee's total turn amount is updated every time their camera's view is rotated. It does not update while using a ladder, but can update while operating the ship terminal. Turn amount builds even while in orbit. === Fear === After noting which employees have the highest 'total turn amount' and 'insanity level', she begins to build a Fear stat for every employee. This value starts at a base level of 80 for every employee. She loops through all employees and tries to apply each case to them. There are four cases. {| class="wikitable" ! Case !! Fear |- | The employee has the highest 'insanity level' || 50 |- | The employee has the highest 'turn amount' || 30 |- | The employee has not been critically injured before (fell below 10HP) || 10 |- | The employee is holding an item (in their hands) worth more than 150 credits || 30 |} An employee's total turn amount and flag for previous critical injury are not reset unless a lobby is recreated. These values persist even after moving to a different moon, dying, getting fired, or rejoining the lobby when not the host. These values are inherited through employee slot rather than SteamID or name: if players are shuffled while rejoining, they will swap Fear stats with each other. [[File:Ghostcam.png|thumb|250px| The Ghost Girl is visible to anyone through the ship camera.]] The chance of an employee being selected for a haunting is weighted based on the amount of fear they have. The higher the fear, the more likely you are to be haunted. After the Ghost Girl has chosen a victim, she will appear to that employee, who is the only employee who can see her at this time. However, she can always be seen through the thermal camera through the right window of the radar. She has two phases: ===Stalking=== In this phase, the employee chosen by the Ghost Girl begins to experience signs that they are being haunted. The first sign is the lights flickering for a second, which can be seen by all employees. Another sign is church bells ringing and/or the haunted player's audio getting distorted and muffled. Afterward, the Ghost Girl will spawn in every few minutes, always behind the employee, accompanied by a few audio cues: * Whispering * Giggling * Heavy breathing * Heart beat * Distorted audio, usually caused by prior effects If the employee looks at the Ghost Girl more than 2 times or has avoided looking at her 3 times, performing any of the following three actions can trigger the next phase: * Approaching the Ghost Girl (20% for her to disappear, 65% chance to phase trigger, 15% chance to do nothing) * Looking at her, line of sight (35% chance to phase trigger, 65% chance to do nothing) * Staring at her, line of sight (85% chance to phase trigger, 15% to do nothing) ===Attacking=== Once the attack phase begins, the lights flicker once more, then the Ghost Girl begins to skip toward the employee at moderate speed and instantly kills them upon contact by decapitating them. The Ghost Girl can follow her target outside the facility, making her one of the few entities that can reach both indoors and outdoors. After the current employee she is haunting dies, the process of choosing a new employee runs again and repeats until the entire party is dead or has left the moon. Getting 50 units away from the Ghost Girl or surviving for 20 seconds causes her to teleport somewhere outside of the employee's line of sight and stop chasing. == Strategy == The Ghost Girl is one of the most consistently difficult threats a team can face. She is unique in that, once spawned in, there is no way to avoid interacting with her; she will always pick one employee to haunt at all times. Once an employee recognizes they are being haunted, they should alert their teammates as quickly as possible. Unlike the [[Jester]], the threat is less immediate because only one employee is affected at a time. Because of this, an experienced team can continue looting while the Ghost Girl is haunting, while the affected employee evades her. Even if an employee is comfortable juggling the Ghost Girl while looting a Facility, she can complicate situations in which other lethal wildlife show up, sometimes forcing you to backtrack and risk triggering the 2nd phase more and more. Upon being haunted, an employee can remain out of the Ghost Girl's reach by climbing on top of the ship's [[Storage Locker]]. This can be achieved by pressing B on the cabinet and then phasing into it by walking into it while still in build mode. Then jump, and when you are at the highest point of the jump, scroll to cancel build mode, which will teleport you above the cabinet. Crouch to ensure she cannot get you from the roof; her legs clip through the roof. Alternatively, staying on the terminal can completely negate the ghost girl but you will have to be the man in the chair for that run. The main way you can avoid the ghost girl is if you hear laughter, lights flicker without apparatus being removed, breathing, heartbeat, etc, do not look behind you. If you feel you need to watch for a Bracken, alert another team member and have them stand behind you, watching for the Bracken to ensure neither of you die. Once you have to turn around to leave, it is best to crouch looking at the floor while the other person guides you to the exit to ensure you do not see the ghost girl and enable her hunting mode. == History == == {{section heading/Notes}} == The Ghost Girl is the only entity with a defined cause of death as Unknown - it is normally used as a default fallback, but for the Ghost Girl, it is specifically defined within the call for KillPlayer. {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

{{Template:Spoilers}}<translate>
<!--T:1-->
Gordion is the [[Moons|Moon]] where [[The Company|The Company's]] Headquarters resides. Unlike any other moon, there is no night and day cycle (although, if you sell any items, a trip to and back does take a day), giving you ample time to sell your [[scrap]] items, or buy from the [[Store]] and retrieve your orders. 

<!--T:2-->
Equally unique is its lack of [[Entities|creatures]], aside from the enigmatic entity that handles [[The Company|The Company's]] paycheck in exchange for credits.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Company description}} == 
{{quote INFO moon|CompanyBuildingInfo}}
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
== Environment ==
The company building has a relatively small map, basically only consisting of a main platform, some containers and a wall. There is also a second layer, containing the drill, underneath the main platform.

<!--T:4-->
==== The Drill ====
Located underneath the moon there is a drill with slots for two [[apparatus]]. The drill is accessible by opening the hatch near the delivery landing sites. Next to the drill there is a slot for a data chip, a broken wire, a slot labelled "BATTERY" and a track leading towards the wall of [[The Company|The Company's]] desk, marked with a spray painted yellow circular marker. As of [[v81]], the drill has no functionality coded in, and the [[apparatus]] cannot be slotted into its slot, although a small hatch on the back of the drill labeled in-game as "trunk" is now able to be opened, with a rectangular slot inside of it. 

<!--T:5-->
=== The Company Monster ===
{{main|The Company#The Company Monster}}
'''The Company Monster''', also known by a large number of other names (The most common pick being '''Jeb'''), is the only entity present on Gordion. It is the company representative and handles the items sold by the employees.

<!--T:13-->
== Data chips ==
A chip containing the log "[[Sigurd_Logs#11._Hiding_-_Sep_30|Hiding]]" can be found at the end of the catwalk below the landing platform.

== Trivia == <!--T:14-->

<!--T:15-->
The Walkie-talkie has [[Walkie-talkie#Trivia|an easter egg]], which activates only on this moon.

<!--T:16-->
In a patreon post by Zeekerss,<tvar name="citation">{{cite Patreon|date=2023-09-26|slug=beta-test-bug-89844466|title=The beta test + bug fixing!}}</tvar> concept art of the drill is titled "[[Lore#Desmond|Desmond]]'s drill".

== References == <!--T:17-->
</translate>

Lasso Man.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom|Lasso}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=LassoMan | name = Lasso Man | altname = Lasso | danger = 30% but it kinda freaks me out | power_level = 2 | max_spawned = 2 | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|LassoMan}} | stun_multiplier = 1.0 }}{{Mbox | name = ScrappedUnused | image = [[File:EndgameStatsMissing.png|85px]] | style = background: #870507; border-color: #000000; padding: 10px | small = no | textstyle = font-size: 16 px | text =
'' "what is the company even using it for"''
This page describes unused content.
| plainlinks = no }} The '''Lasso Man''', often called just '''Lasso,''' is an unused monster in Lethal Company. It can only be spawned via commands. == Bestiary Entry == This creature does not have a proper Bestiary Entry. It uses a slightly edited [[Snare Flea]] Bestiary Entry, which is evident from the Danger Level being "30%", and the video showing the appearance of the creature being the Snare Flea video clip. == Mechanics == Because the Lasso Man is an unfinished creature, it doesn't function properly. Lasso Man's AI is nearly identical to the Thumper's AI , therefore refer to the [[Thumper#Mechanics|Mechanics of the Thumper]] for more information. Below is a description of potentially unintended behavior. The Lasso Man will patrol the facility at the speed of a [[Thumper]]. The Lasso Man's line of sight is very small. After spotting an employee, it will pursue them and investigate their last seen areas to find them again if line of sight is broken. The Lasso Man steadily gains speed while moving and loses some speed when turning. Despite the fact that the entity is unable to [[damage]] employees, it can trigger the adrenaline effect to take place while in contact with employees. The Lasso Man has animations for walking and attacking, however they aren't used. == Trivia == * The Thumper's AI was used as a base for Lasso Man's AI, this is evident from the debug logs that are prefixed with the Thumper's internal name — "Crawler". * When an employee dies to the Lasso Man, the cause of death on their scanned body is "Strangulation", as opposed to the Thumper's cause of death "Mauling". * Lasso Man's "creature file ID" is 8. This is unique to the Lasso Man, as the Snare Flea's creature file ID is 0, and the Thumper's creature file ID is 2. * Its name, Lasso Man, is the game's internal name for it, as no proper in-game name exists. In the [[Template:GameTextExcerpts/Bestiary/LassoMan|unused bestiary entry]], it is also referred to as "Lasso man". However, in the header text, it is called "LASSO". * Lasso Man is suggested to be made of a material similar to chenille. The normal map applied to Lasso Man's model is named "pipeCleanerTex1" == References == [https://i.imgur.com/GYMR1xd.png Assets related to "LassoMan"] [https://i.imgur.com/i0ubkJq.png LassoManAI.cs] vs. [https://i.imgur.com/HwD3GQa.png CrawlerAI.cs] '''LassoManAI.cs''' — component.DamagePlayer(40, hasDamageSFX: true, callRPC: true, CauseOfDeath.Strangulation); '''LassoManFile.asset''' — displayText: "LASSO\n\nSigurd's danger level: 30% but it kinda freaks me out\n\nScientific name: \n\n\n" [https://i.imgur.com/SMYkmdW.png The "LassoMonsterMat" material] {{navbox entities}}

Nutcracker.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity | name = Nutcracker | power_level = 1 | diversity_level = 1 | max_count = 10 | door_speed_multiplier = 2 | pip_size = Medium | internal_id = Nutcracker | attack_damage = 5-100 (Shotgun)
Instant Kill (Kick) | attack_speed = 1s (Shooting)
1.5s (Reloading) | contact_damage = Instant Kill (Kick) | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|Nutcracker}} | stun_multiplier = 0.5 | cause_of_death = Gunshots (Shotgun)
Kicking (Kick) }} The '''Nutcracker''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It initially patrols and surveys its surroundings for any signs of movement. Upon detecting movement from a nearby entity or employee, it chases after them and uses a variety of deadly attacks, such as kicking or blasting the target with a [[shotgun]]. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Rend]]. Nutcrackers are tall, skinny biomechanical entities that appear to be larger-than-life versions of their [[w:Nutcracker doll|real-life counterparts]]. They have hollow white eyes, circular red cheeks, and an outfit resembling that of a [[w:Red coat (military uniform)|British red coat]], and are also armed with a shotgun and a belt containing eight shotgun shells around their waist. Upon opening their mouth, a small, cyclopic entity with a yellow iris, connected by fleshy veins to the Nutcracker's torso, can also be seen. == Bestiary Entry == {{quote bestiary}} == Spawn chances == {{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == The Nutcracker spawns carrying a [[Shotgun]], which serves as its primary form of offense. It has infinite ammo for this shotgun, and can shoot twice before having to reload. The Nutcracker drops its shotgun and 2 [[Ammo|shells]] upon death, with any unfired shells remaining in the gun. The Nutcracker is also capable of kicking any employee if they get too close, instantly killing them and immediately sending the Nutcracker into the attack state, possibly dooming any other nearby employees. The Nutcracker cannot take damage until its eye is exposed, but can register being attacked. A shotgun blast or several shovel hits will make the Nutcracker immediately start attacking the instigating employee. As of version 47, Nutcrackers can aim while moving, making it much more adept at corner kills. The Nutcracker has 3 behavioral states which are described below. === Patrol === The Nutcracker patrols, wandering around the facility. In this state it cannot see, and thus has no awareness of its surroundings. Every 8 to 17 secondshttps://pastebin.com/SDWBuQ9U, a Nutcracker will check a certain list of conditions:https://pastebin.com/84efYzkE At least 4 seconds have passed since the last inspection AND ( The distance to the nearest employee is short enough (27 units - almost maximum shotgun range) OR Successfully pass a 40% chance ) If the above conditions succeed, the Nutcracker enters the scanning state. === Scanning === The Nutcracker stops moving and scans its surroundings, raising its headpiece and turning counterclockwise in increments until it has turned a full 360 degrees or the below conditions are met. In this state the Nutcracker has a field of view of 140 degrees.https://pastebin.com/msJzae0M The Nutcracker will move to the attack state if: * The employee is within line-of-sight or too close to the Nutcrackerhttps://pastebin.com/0PmVZCHF * That same employee is also 'moving'https://pastebin.com/q6a2Tdzb ** Performing an emote ** Moving the mouse/turning ** Crouching down or standing up ** Walking or sprinting Note: Walking or sprinting without moving the camera wouldn't alert the Nutcracker, which seemed to be a bug. This behavior is fixed in v50. If the above conditions are not met, the Nutcracker will return to patrolling. === Attack === [[File:Nutcracker Alert.ogg|thumb|200px| The "metal ringing" sound when a Nutcracker targets an employee]] [[File:Nutcracker Angry.ogg|thumb|200px| The music looped during a Nutcracker's attack]] The Nutcracker targets an employee. A metal ringing will sound when an employee is targeted, and distorted whistling with marching drums will play to indicate the attack state is in effect. At this point, the targeted employee can no longer freeze to avoid being seen until the attack state ends. In this state, the Nutcracker will follow and attack the targeted employee until the employee dies or line of sight has been broken for a sufficient amount of time (at least 12 seconds), at which point the Nutcracker will move to the scanning state, and then back to the patrolling state if no employee is found. The Nutcracker cannot see employees other than the targeted employee unless they move, even in this state. If it sees an employee moving when they are significantly closer to the Nutcracker than its target is, or when it has not seen its target in 3 seconds, it will change its target to that employee, regardless of which sub-phase it is in. There are 3 sub-phases during this state. ==== Pursuing ==== While attacking, the Nutcracker moves towards the employee's last known position at speed of 7 units per second. If the employee was seen in the last 0.5 seconds, it paths directly to the employee, stopping 1 unit away, otherwise it calculates a position 5-6 units away from the employee and stops when within 2 units of said position. While moving, it will strafe to ensure it faces the employee to allow the aiming phase to trigger. ==== Aiming ==== Every frame, if each of the following checks pass, the Nutcracker progresses to the aiming phase: * Line of sight with employee * Time since last shot >0.75 seconds * Not reloading or aiming * Time since last kick >1 second * Angle to employee is within 30 degrees * The held shotgun has shells loaded The time taken to aim changes depending based on the amount of shells in the shotgun. With two shells loaded, it will spend 1.75 seconds aiming, while with one, it takes 1.3 seconds. If the Nutcracker's HP is 1, the aim time is always 0.5 seconds. Every time the Nutcracker enters the aiming phase, it increments a counter for the employee it is aiming at. If the current targeted employee is different to the employee targeted last time it aimed, the counters for all employees are reset. While this counter is less than or equal to 2, the Nutcracker cannot move while aiming, but for all subsequent shots it slows down to a speed of 2.25 units per second. After the aim timer has elapsed, it fires a shot, using a shell from the shotgun. After the shot, it waits 0.35 seconds, retaining the movement speed from the aim phase. While aiming, the Nutcracker cannot kick. ==== Reloading ==== The reloading phase is activated when: * Time since last shot >0.75 seconds * The held shotgun has no shells loaded While reloading, the Nutcracker cannot move. This phase lasts exactly 1.74 seconds during which it is most vulnerable, as it cannot move, shoot, or kick. After the time has elapsed, the shotgun will have 2 shells loaded, and the Nutcracker will return to the pursuing phase. The hit detection and damage is [[Shotgun#Mechanics|explained in the Shotgun article]]. (It's the same logic as when an employee uses a shotgun.) == Strategy == The Nutcracker can be a very dangerous adversary due to its extremely lethal shotgun that can instantly kill any employee caught out in the open, making cover a must for most fights with a Nutcracker. However, despite this danger the Nutcracker is usually an enemy worth killing, as the shotgun it drops is a very powerful weapon and can be sold to help meet quota. Do not be fooled by the ‘grab’ prompt that appears when hovering over an active Nutcracker’s shotgun, a Nutcracker’s shotgun cannot be taken from its hands until it is dead. '''There is no way to get around fighting the Nutcracker if you want its shotgun.''' Be warned when fighting a Nutcracker that it can instantly kill you by kicking you if you come into physical contact with it but cannot kick you if it is firing or reloading its shotgun. Additionally, sometimes the Nutcracker will chase you while running backwards, and is unable to shoot you whilst doing so. If it touches you while this is happening, it will kick you and kill you, so it is advised to circle an obstacle and hope it starts facing you again. This is likely not an intended mechanic and is therefore (probably) a bug. Nutcrackers can also kill other monsters with their shots, however they will not intentionally target other monsters, so getting this to happen will require an employee to be targeted by a Nutcracker and bait its shots into collaterally killing other enemies. If you decide to take the attention of 2 Nutcrackers at once, one may kill the other depending on their positioning relative to you and to each other, giving you the opportunity to take the dead Nutcracker's shotgun and kill the one left alive with it. Technically speaking, it is possible to jump a shotgun blast from a Nutcracker but requires amazing timing and a decent amount of distance. Below are some strategies for dealing with a Nutcracker. === Ambush (Shovel) === This is one of the quickest methods of killing a Nutcracker, but it requires multiple employees with multiple [[Shovel]]s. This strategy is very simple: 2-4 employees grab shovels, group up around the Nutcracker while it's in its patrol state and hit it to death as soon as it enters its scanning state and raises its headpiece, as there is a short delay before the Nutcracker can fire out of its scanning state. Stunning the Nutcracker with either a [[Homemade flashbang]]/[[Stun grenade]] or a [[Zap gun]] can aid this strategy, allowing more time for the Nutcracker to be beaten down without repercussion. Be careful when trying to kill a Nutcracker this way, as it can be very easy to hit or be hit by your fellow employees while trying to gang up on the Nutcracker. === Ambush (Shotgun) === This is much the same as the above method, only it is both easier and faster, as it requires only one employee and one shotgun instead of multiple employees and multiple shovels. Simply walk up to a Nutcracker with a loaded shotgun and shoot it point blank at any point when its eye is exposed. If done correctly the Nutcracker will instantly die, as a shotgun can do up to 5 damage per shot if the target is close enough. === Bait and Hit === This strategy is not reliant on employee count. Find cover you're comfortable with then get a Nutcracker to target you before retreating to the aforementioned cover. Peek out of cover for a moment to bait out its first shot, repeat to bait out its second shot, and then attack once with a shovel while it's reloading and start the sequence over. A Nutcracker will make the same metal ringing noises mentioned in the attack state section every time it's about to fire, which can make knowing when to take cover easier. This strategy can be done with one employee as the bait and another as the attacker, or with one employee as both the bait and attacker. Note that Nutcrackers have 5 hit points and thus take 5 hits from a shovel to die, and that a Nutcracker on its last health point will reload and fire much faster. Because of this it's recommended to hit the Nutcracker a total of 3 times, then finish it off with 2 consecutive hits to avoid dealing with a low health Nutcracker. '''Note:''' due to Nutcrackers being able to aim while moving as of version 47, along with being more aggressive with its kick attack, this strategy now requires constant movement and good reflexes to properly dodge. === Landmine === A landmine can be used to instantly kill a Nutcracker. A Nutcracker will chase a targeted employee while in the attack state, which can be utilized to draw the Nutcracker toward a landmine to kill it. Note that Nutcrackers cannot activate landmines on their own, so it is on the employee to activate it. Things that will trip a landmine include scrap, live or spent stun grenades, and employees. === Spike Trap === If a Nutcracker is underneath a descending spike trap, the trap will instantly kill it. This is much easier to arrange with spike traps that operate on a timed interval than with motion-activated spike traps. A patrolling Nutcracker may walk under a nearby spike trap on its own initiative, or an attacking Nutcracker may pass beneath one while chasing an employee. Once the Nutcracker is dead, the safest method for employees to retrieve its shotgun without themselves being killed by the spike trap is to have another employee turn the trap off at the terminal. Depending on the trap interval and positioning of the Nutcracker's corpse, it may be possible (if risky) for an employee to simply run through and grab the shotgun between intervals. === Avoid === If you wish not to fight a Nutcracker, you must take advantage of the quirks of its patrol and scanning states. While a Nutcracker is patrolling it is effectively blind, meaning you can pass by one safely in this state as long as you keep your distance to avoid being kicked. If a Nutcracker enters the scanning state as you're passing by, stand completely still when it turns to face you and wait for it to continue turning, as it cannot detect you unless you are in its line of sight ''and'' moving. Note that even actions you may think count as stationary, like moving your camera or emoting, count as moving and will allow the Nutcracker to target you.https://pastebin.com/q6a2Tdzb It is recommended to keep your eye on the Nutcracker as you pass by so that, should it enter its scanning state while you're nearby, you know when you're out of its vision and can thus move freely. == History == === [[Version 45]] === * Introduced. === [[Version 47]] === * Nutcracker can now move while aiming. === [[Version 50]] === * Nutcracker can now aim downwards, rendering the "crouch cheese" strategy of crouching just outside of kicking range impossible. * Nutcracker can now see employees walking, sprinting, and jumping while in the scanning state, even if the employees in question do not turn their camera while moving. == Gallery == NutcrackerExposed.png| A Nutcracker with its eye exposed. Screenshot 2023-12-14 1.25.26 PM.png| A Nutcracker right after being fatally shot in the eye. Screenshot 2023-12-14 1.28.27 PM.png| A Nutcracker dead on the floor. Nutcracker_patrol.gif| A Nutcracker in its patrol state. Nutcracker_scan.gif| A Nutcracker in its scanning state. Nutcracker_attack.gif| A Nutcracker in its attack state. == Sources == {{translators' notes| Name is exactly: {{WDLS|324610|2|nutcracker}} (a {{native Wikipedia link|en=nutcracker doll}} is a decorative figurine, which typically looks like a {{native Wikipedia link|en=toy soldier}}, a {{native Wikipedia link|en=Red_coat_(military_uniform)|label=redcoat}}; they originated in Germany, and are associated with {{native Wikipedia link|en=Christmas}}) }} {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

{{Template:Investigation}}
<translate>
<!--T:1-->
'''Ammo''' (short for 'ammunition'), a.k.a. '''Shotgun shell''', is a consumable item in the game, exclusively dropped from [[Nutcracker]]s.
It becomes available for collection after defeating a Nutcracker.

<!--T:2-->
The primary function of Ammo is to facilitate reloading the [[Shotgun]] while held in your inventory when there is space for it in the shotgun, the sole ranged weapon featured in the game, consuming the shell. Notably, Ammo is consistently generated in pairs upon the Nutcracker's defeat.

<!--T:3-->
Players are unable to purchase Ammo through the in-game terminal. Shotgun shells can be sold at the [[Gordion|Company Building]], but sell for nothing.

<!--T:5-->
Despite their minimal size and no sell value, they can't be put in the [[utility slot]].
</translate>
== {{section heading/History}} ==

=== {{section heading/Version X|45}} ===
<translate>
<!--T:4-->
Introduced.
</translate>
