Masked.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=MaskedPlayerEnemy | name = Masked | altname = Mimic | power_level = 1 | diversity_level = 0 | max_spawned = 10 | pip_size = Player | door_speed_multiplier = 2 | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|MaskedPlayerEnemy}} | stun_multiplier = 0.75 | attack_damage = Instant kill | cause_of_death = Strangulation }} {{about|the enemy|the scrap item|Masks (Item)}} The '''Masked''', also referred to as the '''Mimic''', is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. While capable of spawning naturally, it also spawns if an employee wears a [[Masks (Item)|mask]] for a certain period of time, causing them to become possessed. When active, it can freely enter and leave the facility, attempting to hunt down employees and imitate their behaviors in order to grab and infect them into more Masked. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Artifice]]. Masked appear as typical employees with orange uniforms, though with a white comedy mask fused to their heads. Their eyes glow a bright pink while they are in the process of infecting employees. == Bestiary Entry == Cannot be scanned. == Spawn chances == {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances|MaskedPlayerEnemy}} == Mechanics == Masked spawn two ways. The first is naturally as an enemy (these will still show up as a blue dot like an employee, but cannot be switched to and teleported on the cameras), while the other is by an employee using a [[Masks (Item)|Mask]]. Tragedy masks and Comedy masks have a 65% chance every 5 seconds to possess and kill the employee holding it up to their face; they are otherwise harmless. If a Masked is spawned this way, it still appears as an employee on [[Ship|cameras]] and can even be teleported. An employee who is killed by putting on a Tragedy mask will produce a Masked with a tragedy mask; all other Masked will have comedy masks. Naturally spawned Masked will always wear the default orange suit. Employees converted into Masked who were wearing [[suits]] purchased from the store will retain their original suit. Masked can detect sound. Upon hearing a sound, they look towards it but may not always move towards the sound's source to investigate. Masked can operate the elevator in the [[Interiors|mineshaft interior]]. If the Masked gets in range to attack an employee, it grabs them and vomits blood into their face, converting them into another Masked. This will spawn them with employee properties. This attack can be interrupted if another employee hits the Masked with a [[shovel]]. Despite popular belief, there is no indication that a mask can attach to an employee's body postmortem. An employee killed while holding the mask up to their face will drop it near their body, but if the mask attaches to an employee's face and the employee is killed by other means in the short interval before the mask itself kills them, the employee will have the mask attached to their face with red eyes, and will appear as a dead masked. Note that this scenario does not lead to possession of a dead body, as that is not possible. Masked operate in 3 different modes which determine the majority of their behavior: === Roaming === In this state, Masked roam around the map searching for employees and are most easily distinguished on the radar as they look around randomly, often spinning in circles. Masked will also start and stop running based on random chance. This random looking and running adheres to a stamina bar and is governed by the logic described underneath. '''Randomly looking''': Every 0.7 to 5 seconds: Change direction left or right up to 60 degrees at a time. Taking 0.25 to 2 seconds to change. '''Randomly start running''': IF not running: IF has at least 5 seconds of stamina: IF outside: chance to start running = 35% NOT outside: chance to start running = 20% '''Randomly stop running''': IF already running: IF stamina = 0: Stop running immediately ELSE: IF outside: chance to stop running = 20% NOT outside: chance to stop running = 70% -- If a Masked stops running it will set its stamina bar to -6, meaning it is unable to run *randomly* for 11 seconds. If no employees are inside the facility whilst in the roaming state, a mask will consistently stop its search and head for the exit. While roaming, Masked will check their line of sight for any employee. If an employee is seen, it will switch to the Pursuit state. Furthermore, Masked will build up 'interest' in the ship whilst in this state. When enough interest is gathered, they will switch to the Ambush state if the ship is not too far away from their position. === Pursuit === In pursuit mode, the Masked has spotted an employee (or the Masked has been seen in ambush mode) and is pursuing them. When the Masked first enters this state, it stops and stares at the targeted employee for 2 to 5 seconds. This timer immediately ends if the Masked is damaged. In pursuit mode, it retains eye contact with its target and starts heading towards them. Notably, the running randomly logic differs from the logic in the roaming state. When a Masked is in pursuit, the percentage chance to both stop and start running randomly change significantly. '''Randomly start running''': IF not running: IF has at least 5 seconds of stamina: IF outside: chance to start running = 11.66% NOT outside: chance to start running = 6.66% '''Randomly stop running''': IF already running: IF stamina = 0: Stop running immediately ELSE: IF outside: chance to stop running = 84% NOT outside: chance to stop running = 94% Furthermore, if the route to their target is more than 17 units away, they are guaranteed to start running, only stopping when within 12 units of their target or out of stamina. Masked will stop staring at and moving towards an employee if line-of-sight has been broken for at least 3.5 seconds. After this time, Masked will consider their target 'lost in chase', and will start looking for them by walking around. After line-of-sight has been broken for 10 seconds, they revert back to their roaming state. Interestingly, Masked broaden but also shorten their field-of-view when in this mode compared to the field-of-view in the roaming state. Going from a width of 45 and a range of 60 to a width of 70 and a range of 50. Additionally, Masked gain 'proximity awareness' meaning they can 'see' in a circle (of 1 unit) around them. Masked are able to switch their target at any time in this state if another employee is closer in their line-of-sight. === Ambush === Masked switch to ambush mode if enough 'interest' has been gathered by roaming the facility. When they are in this state, they start walking towards the ship and look for a spot inside the ship to hide in. Whilst moving towards the ship, Masked will lose interest in the ship, eventually returning to a roaming state. If a mask is inside the ship, they will no longer lose interest in the ship, staying in this state until seeing an employee or being seen by an employee. Whilst waiting in ambush, Masked will crouch. Notably, this is the only time Masked ever crouch. In this state, Masked change their field of view once again. Going from a width of 45 and a range of 60 to a width of 70 and a range of 20 if compared to their field of view while roaming. == Strategy == It is recommended if you see a Masked to create as much distance and open space as possible, along with making sure all employees know of its existence to not cause more Masked to spawn. Masked do not retain any [[Spray paint]] on them, allowing possible indication of possession. If an employee is transforming into a Masked, the animation of the possession is long enough for another employee to kill the Masked. Because of this, with good reaction time and teamwork you can kill any accidental spawns, and at least damage Masked formed from employees turning. Similar to the [[Forest Keeper|forest keeper]]'s grab, there is a small window to where the employee may be saved by another party especially if multiple employees are present. Hitting the Masked with a melee weapon and killing it, a successful teleport, or stunning it with a [[Stun grenade]], [[Homemade flashbang]], [[Zap gun]], or a [[Radar-booster]]. A [[Shovel|shovel]] can dispatch a Masked with relative ease as a shovel has more range than a Masked's grab. Using a [[kitchen knife]] is possible but much riskier due to its short range. On the monitor, Masked will still seem like employees and there is no visual difference except in their behavior. Masked will frequently spin in circles when roaming. Employees turned into Masked will be seen as normal employees, despite the Masked behavior of the employee and can still be teleported, bringing danger to other employees on the ship. Masked that are not employees (i.e. spawned Masked) will not have a switchable spot on the monitor, therefore giving clues as to where a Masked is. The [[inverse teleporter]] cannot remove a Masked from the ship. Masked also seem to be capable of simple strategy, as groups of 2 or 3 will coordinate to trap employees. This is most evident when by the ship as one masked will usually hide inside while the others patrol the area around the ship. Masked can be hurt by [[eyeless dog]]s when coming into contact with them, but eyeless dogs do not intentionally attack Masked. [[Old Bird]]s target all entities within their field of vision, including Masked. [[Baboon hawk]]s can attack Masked if they remain in proximity for prolonged periods of time, but this is uncommon, even on [[Adamance]]. == Sounds == {{audio gallery | A mask attaching itself to an employee's face|File:MaskComedyAttach.ogg |br| Distorted laughter audible when a Masked grabs an employee|File:MaskAttackPlayer.ogg |br| A Masked's eyes lighting up while attacking|File:MaskFaceLightUp.ogg |br| A Masked spewing blood on an employee|File:MaskPuke.ogg }} == Gallery == File:Ruh roh.png| A masked that has been teleported onto the ship. MaskAttack.png| A masked infecting an employee by vomiting blood on them. MaskDead.png| A dead masked after being shot. File:It looks so peaceful.png| A dead masked outdoors. CrouchingMasked.png| A masked crouching. GreenSuitMasked.png| A masked produced from an employee who was wearing an alternate suit. == Trivia == * Masked are, despite popular belief, '''NOT''' inspired by [https://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/scp-035 SCP 035], as stated in a patreon post. * Employees killed by a Masked do not drop items or scrap. * There is such thing as "Mask Roulette", where all employees take a turn putting on a mask, and whoever gets Masked is killed, therefore making the game dangerously fun. * Both living and dead Masked can make the same laughing noises as the comedy mask item. == History == === {{section heading/Version X|69}} === Fixed some wonky behavior with the masked enemy that had to do with the elevator. === [[Version 49]] === Unknown hotfix. === [[Version 47]] === Fixed an issue which occurred when the masked enemy was interrupted while converting an employee, which could result in the employee becoming stuck or unable to move their mouse and sync position to other employees. Prevented a case where the masked enemy could create dozens of duplicates of the same employee if that employee was somehow not allowed to die, resulting in chaos. === [[Version 45]] === Introduced. == Sources == DetectNoise - https://pastebin.com/hKviAUFy HauntedMaskItem main event loop - https://pastebin.com/dEmHqCJr LookAndRunRandomly - https://pastebin.com/NqBTmZEC DoAiInterval - https://pastebin.com/RewHgkNe Update - https://pastebin.com/v35bTvw4 HitEnemy - https://pastebin.com/G1r1uzRX [https://www.tumblr.com/romolossesit/786504719452553216/ghggh-not-enough-trust-in-myself-to-edit-the-wiki Tumblr blogpost by romolossesit] {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

Old Bird.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity | name = Old Bird | power_level = 3 | diversity_level = 1 | max_count = 20 | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|RadMech}} | attack_damage = Lethal (Grab)
~40 (Rockets)
~10 (Stomp) | id = RadMech | stun_multiplier = 1.2 | pip_size = Large | cause_of_death = Burning (Grab)
Blast (Rockets)
Crushing (Stomp) }} The '''Old Bird''', also known as '''Al (A16-L31)''' or the "'''Walking Ransom Letter'''", is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. While initially appearing inert, it reactivates after either a certain period of time passes or when the [[apparatus]] is unplugged from the facility. It is capable of flight and detects nearby threats with its searchlight, before it fires missiles or grabs and burns them alive with a flamethrower when aggravated. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Embrion]]. Old Birds are colossal, dark gray mechs covered in rust, a yellow searchlight in place of their head, and a trapezoidal torso. Their bodies have three speakers attached to them that emit "brainwashing waves": a large, rectangular one is built into its chest, and two smaller speakers are attached to their shoulders. Their left arm carries a missile launcher and flamethrower, while their right arm has a four-digited claw. Their nozzle-shaped legs function as rockets, allowing them to fly or hover in midair. == Bestiary entry == {{Template:Terminal|text= OLD BIRDS The Old Bird is an autonomous, offensive weapon of war with a humanoid design. Measured at 19 feet tall and 11 feet wide, its two most defining visual features are the spotlight positioned on its head, capable of emitting 100,000 lumens, and the long-range acoustic device on its chest, sometimes referred to as a sound cannon. There are additional speakers on its shoulders used to broadcast sound a long distance. The Old Bird's left arm is a claw, and its right arm is a nozzle capable of both launching rocket-propelled grenades and torching at close-range with a very hot flame. Old Birds are one of the first extra-orbital weapons to be used and mass-produced. The subject of who developed the Old Birds has been an intense debate since their first recorded appearance on December 18 of 2143, when a large number of Old Birds invaded the

The design of the Old Bird, down to the tempering of metals, appears intended to conceal its origin. It has been called the "walking ransom letter." Old Birds' legs function as rockets, which allows them to travel long distances and find targets efficiently. But the most likely reason this feature exists is to help them enter and land from orbit. The material and fuel Old Birds rely on is similar to those of passenger space craft from around 2130. Old Birds land and stay at their target planet forever. Although they often have more than enough fuel for just one trip, no behavior has been found in their programming which suggests they could choose to "migrate". Still, there are unverified accounts of Old Birds travelling autonomously to a new planet after hundreds of years of dormancy. The Old Bird was historically referred to as the "Al", based off the code name it was given by the English military, the "A16-L31". In 2384, however, the song "Old Birds" released as a cult hit by the post-punk project Under Remora; just three years later, in his most famous work of the same name, street artist Land Eyrie depicted the automotons flying and landing between planets in an arrangement similar to a flock of geese--this is generally considered one of the most iconic and influential images of modern culture and solidified the modern nickname. In 2356, 5-Embrion was classified as Condemned for the purposes of travel or settlement by the Boat; Old Birds were described by witness accounts as "lining the horizon". Though to this day they appear inoperative, there is a reasonable risk that many are still in sleep mode, so the planet will likely keep its status. The small moon was likely used as a testing ground for the Old Bird's flying and landing capabilities.}} == Mechanics == === Inactive === Old Birds spawn in this state at the start of the day. While inactive, they remain stationary and harmless, with their searchlight off. They can be scanned in this state, but only from the front. === Roaming === Old Birds will activate autonomously at a time pre-determined during generation (and also upon an employee pulling the [[Apparatus]] from its socket), after which they will begin to roam with their searchlight on in search of employees or [[entities]]. During an eclipse, roughly half of the Old Birds will already be activated when employees land on the moon. Regardless of its current state, their steps deal a medium amount of damage to employees or entities underneath it, even if the Old Bird is unaware of it. Old Birds will sometimes have a special roaming state where they randomly utilize their rocket feet to fly high into the sky in an attempt to detect employees from a much greater distance (they make distant airplane sounds when doing this). If they do not detect an employee after some time, they will land somewhere random and proceed their normal roaming habits. Otherwise they will fly right over the target employee, and drop onto them. Employees can take significant damage if they are crushed from the dropping Old Bird, and can also be quickly grabbed if the employee doesn't distance themselves from it quickly. === Chasing === [[File:oldbirdchasing.webp|thumb|An Old Bird chasing an entity.]] If an Old Bird detects a target with its searchlight, it will either start attacking immediately if the target is close enough, otherwise it will jet to the target's location and start attacking once it reaches them. === Attacking === Once a spotted target is within range, the Old Bird will engage it with a series of different attacks until it is killed or no longer in sight: * '''Missiles''' ** Being its most common attack, the Old Bird can launch a barrage of missiles at a high rate of fire but with low accuracy. The missiles have a curving path (but do not actually home in on targets) and deal high damage upon direct hit. If the missile happens to explode on the ground directly beneath an employee, they will be killed instantly. * '''Stomping''' ** When a target is in close range, an Old Bird will try to crush it with its foot. Due to the Old Bird's large collision box, the area of effect through which damage is dealt is quite wide. * '''Squishing''' ** An Old Bird will occasionally take off into the sky before quickly attempting to land on top of a target, dealing high damage and is difficult for the target to avoid. [[File:Oldbirdgrabattack.webp|thumb|175x175px|An Old Bird grabbing and burning the player.]] * '''Grabbing and burning''' ** An Old Bird will very rarely grab employees with its vice arm before incinerating them with its flamethrower arm. The flamethrower deals damages at a rate of 20 HP per 0.25 seconds, rapidly killing them from full health in about 1.25 seconds. Unlike the [[Forest Keeper]] using any form of stunning on an Old Bird will not cause grabbed employees to be dropped, but also unlike Forest keepers, the employee's [[body]] and items will be retrievable after being grabbed and burned. == Occurrence == {{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Strategy == Old Birds can usually be a benefit, killing off dangerous creatures for you. Doing your best to avoid it entirely while this happens is vital to making it an advantage instead of a detriment. If spotted, running in a small semi-circle around it to force it to slowly turn while you gain ground is a good way to lose it. Getting behind cover before it turns to face you can make it lose you entirely. == {{section heading/Sounds}} == {{audio gallery | Ambient sounds for an active Old Bird|File:RadMechAmbientSFX.ogg |br| An Old Bird activating|File:RadMechWake.ogg |br| Alarm that plays when an Old Bird is attacking|File:LRADAlarm3.ogg |br| Tune that can play while an Old Bird is attacking|File:RobotTune.ogg |br| The "brainwashing signals" that are randomly heard when an Old Bird is attacking|File:LradBrainwashingSignal1.ogg|File:LradBrainwashingSignal4.ogg|File:LradBrainwashingSignal6.ogg|File:LradBrainwashingSignal7.ogg|File:LradBrainwashingSignal8.ogg |br| An Old Bird's footsteps|File:RadMechStomp1.ogg|File:RadMechStomp2.ogg|File:RadMechStomp3.ogg|File:RadMechStomp4.ogg }} == History == === [[Version 50]] === * Introduced. == Trivia == Old Birds can be devoured by [[Earth Leviathan|Earth Leviathans]]. Earlier versions of the version 50 beta had much more information about Old Birds in its bestiary. Part of it was removed completely in a later version 50 beta. Around version 60, it was intentionallyFrom ''The Dark Place Travelers'' discord, in [https://ptb.discord.com/channels/750645598293590077/974101195545280542/1452432984299929661 #patreon-hangout] partially restored and left in the state it is today with an incomplete sentence, possibly to suggest file tampering. The missing text is as follows:
The subject of who developed the Old Birds has been an intense debate since their first recorded appearance on December 18 of 2143, when over fifty Old Birds invaded the Anglen Capital. This is considered one of the first major causes for the downfall of the Anglen Empire. The most commonly upheld theory takes into account the tension between the Anglen and Buemoch military throughout the 2100's, however nothing has been proven in the centuries since.
It is debatable if this information can be seen as official worldbuilding information anymore due to its removal. [[Sigurd]] was also once present in the bestiary entry, though was removed due to timeline inconsistencies that have little implications to Old Bird lore. It was given a danger level of 95%, and his additions at the bottom of the page are as follows:
DON'T MESS AROUND OR THEYLL GIVE YOU A RIDE.tHEY LOSE TRACK QUICK AND THEY CANT TURN VERY FAST, THEYRE DUMB AND THEY WONT SHUT UP sorry caps
== {{section heading/Gallery}} == File:MechEnemyDesign.webp| Old Bird concept art. == {{section heading/References}} == {{Navbox entities}}

Butler.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity | name = Butler | danger = 70% | attack_damage = 10 | attack_speed = 0.25s | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|Butler}} | power_level = 2 | diversity_level = 2 | max_count = 7 | stun_multiplier = 0.6 | pip_size = Medium | door_speed_multiplier = 0.6 | cause_of_death = Stabbing }} {{Template:Direct Quote}} The '''Butler''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. Initially appearing docile around groups of two or more employees while sweeping with a broom, it draws a [[Kitchen knife|knife]] to murder any employees that are left alone in a room with it. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Dine]]. Butlers are dried, bloated corpses with tan-colored skin and swollen faces resembling that of an [[w:Mr Blobby (fish)|exploded blobfish]]. Other facial features include a round, stubby nose, tiny dark eye sockets, small patches of black hair on top of their heads, and puffy cheeks with skin flaps concealing their mouth. Butlers wear a black overcoat with a striped crimson sweater and a white button-up shirt underneath, and wear small purple bowties. == Bestiary entry == {{quote bestiary}} == Spawn chances == {{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == The Butler has 4 possible states: Roaming, lingering, murder, and berserk. === Roaming === While roaming, Butlers wander around and search for employees (typically near the main entrance), occasionally pulling out a broom to sweep the floor. If the Butler has not spotted an employee after 6 seconds of roaming, it begins to move significantly faster. === Lingering === Upon seeing an employee, the Butler sets the employee as its target, and a 12 second timer will begin. During this time, the Butler stays near the employee. When the Butler is in this state, multiple things can occur: *If the Butler is alone with the targeted employee and the 12 second timer runs out: It will enter the murder state. *If the Butler loses track of the targeted employee during the 12 seconds: It will check the last place it saw them. *If the Butler loses track of the targeted employee when the 12 second timer runs out: It will return to roaming until the target employee has been spotted again, in which case the Butler will immediately enter the murder state. *If the Butler loses track of an employee: It will remember the employees location for 6 seconds, regardless if they're the target. *If the Butler sees a different employee than the one it is currently targeting and they are alone: The new employee will become the Butler's target and a new 12 second timer will begin. *If there is more than 1 employee near a Butler: It will roam and wait until an employee is alone, and the closest employee will be the Butlers target. *If employees leave a single targeted employee behind with a Butler: It will remember the other employees location for 6 seconds, then once those 6 seconds are up the 12 second timer for the targeted employee will begin, totaling in 18 seconds. === Murder === [[File:Butlerchasing.webp|thumb|200x200px| A Butler charging an employee with a kitchen knife.]] If a targeted employee is left alone with a Butler for 12 seconds, it briefly looks around for witnesses before charging at them with a [[Kitchen Knife|kitchen knife]], relentlessly attacking until the employee is killed. If another employee enters the room, the Butler immediately puts the knife away before resuming roaming and sweeping the floor with a broom. If the Butler loses track of a targeted employee for 12 seconds during this state, it returns to its roaming phase. However, it will immediately attack the employee the next time they are seen, unless the Butler is targeting a different employee. Upon killing an employee, the Butler runs away from the corpse for 15 seconds, then returns to roaming. If the Butler sees an employee during these 15 seconds, it will enter the lingering state. A Butler immediately attacks if it is caught with the corpse of an employee it just killed. While a Butler is in this state, an intense eerie melody will start playing. === Berserk === Upon being hit by an employee, the Butler immediately begins attacking the closest employee and moves incredibly quickly, regardless of how many employees are present nearby. This state lasts for 8 seconds. If any other Butlers see a Butler in this berserk state, they also enter the berserk state. Butlers made berserk in this way will return to the murder state at the same time as the original berserk Butler they saw. There is also a 5% chance for a Butler to become berserk upon contact with an employee in the roaming or lingering states, in which case they will immediately attack the employee and remain berserk for 3 seconds. After the berserk state ends, the Butler will return to the murder state. === Death === Upon death, a Butler inflates and explodes, dealing high knockback to employees within close range. It also drops its [[kitchen knife]] alongside a horde of [[Mask Hornets]]. The knockback from a Butler exploding does not deal damage itself, but an employee can be damaged from a resulting fall. == Strategy == The Butler will not attack if another employee is nearby while keeping distance. Because of this, it is advised to stick together with at least one other employee if a Butler is present. If caught alone, it's ideal to run instead of fight as Butlers have a low attack cool down so they can get multiple hits in a short amount of time. In singleplayer, Butlers only have 2 health instead of 8, so killing them is a viable option as Mask Hornets can be easier to deal with than the Butlers themselves. Regardless of the number of employees, it is recommended to wait right before leaving the facility for good before killing any Butlers, as the Mask Hornets are extremely dangerous to deal with because of the close quarters in the facility and their persistent tracking. It is incredibly dangerous to attack a Butler in front of other Butlers, so waiting until one is alone before attacking it is advisable. == Notes == [[Kitchen knives]] will always kill a Butler in one swing. *Butlers can hear employees, but this only causes the Butler to look at the employee it hears. *Butlers can see through doors (meaning they will keep line-of-sight and not lose track of hunted employees), but only Facility and Mansion doors. Mineshaft doors can break line-of-sight. *Stunning a Butler will have no effect on its state or timer. *The Butler has teeth which can be revealed in slowed down footage of most butler encounters. == Sounds == {{audio gallery | A Butler's footsteps|File:ButlerStep1.ogg|File:ButlerStep2.ogg|File:ButlerStep3.ogg|File:ButlerStep4.ogg |br| A Butler sweeping with its broom|File:BroomSweep1.ogg|File:BroomSweep2.ogg|File:BroomSweep3.ogg|File:BroomSweep4.ogg |br| The droning music that plays in a Butler's presence|File:ButlerIdleAmbience.ogg |br| The muffled buzzing of the Mask Hornets inside a Butler|File:ButlerBuzzingAmbience.ogg |br| A Butler drawing or putting away its broom|File:ButlerSuitRustle.ogg |br| A Butler taking out its knife|File:ButlerUntuckKnifeSFX.ogg |br| A Butler stabbing an employee with its knife|File:ButlerStab.ogg |br| The music that plays while a Butler attempts to commit murder|File:ButlerMurderTheme.ogg |br| A Butler immediately before it explodes|File:ButlerBlowUp.ogg }} == History == === [[Version 50]] === * Introduced. == Gallery == {{translators' notes| Name is exactly: {{WDLS|30336|1|butler}} (an occupation name; a {{native Wikipedia link|en=butler}} is a {{native Wikipedia link|en=domestic worker}} who would stereotypically wear a {{native Wikipedia link|en=suit|label=three-piece suit}}; it's said to be the male counterpart of a {{native Wikipedia link|en=Housekeeper (domestic worker)|label=housekeeper}})
A trope in {{native Wikipedia link|en=whodunit|label=murder mystery}} narratives is that the butler is a suspect. }} File:Butlerdesign2.webp| Initial concept art. File:ButlerConcept2.webp| Additional concept art. File:ButlerModellingProcess.webp| Modeling process. {{Navbox entities}}

Mask Hornets.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{Infobox entity|id=ButlerBees
| name = Mask Hornets
| power_level = 2
| diversity_level = 0
| attack_damage = 10
| stun_multiplier = 0.6
| door_speed_multiplier = 0.08
| hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|ButlerBees}}
| altname = Butler Bees, Facility Bees
| attack_speed = 0.5s
| contact_damage = 10
| dps = 20
| see_through_fog = No
| cause_of_death = Stabbing
}}
<translate>
<!--T:1-->
'''Mask Hornets''' are [[entities]] found [[indoors]] that are spawned from killed [[Butler]]s. Once spawned, they roam the facility looking for employees and act similarly to [[Circuit bees]]. They stay in the facility until the ship leaves the [[moon]]. 
</translate>
== {{section heading/Bestiary entry}} ==
{{quote bestiary|MaskHornet}}

== {{section heading/Mechanics}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:2-->
Mask Hornets spawn when a Butler is killed, and although invincible, they cannot exit the facility or open locked doors. They attack the nearest employee and give chase at a slightly slower speed than the employee's default walking speed. When roaming the facility, they move slower and can be heard by their loud buzzing.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Strategy}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
If possible, try to avoid spawning them until later in the afternoon to keep their presence to a minimum. If spawned, employees can close doors in front of the hornets to buy time and gain distance.

<!--T:4-->
Because Mask Hornets behave very similarly to [[Circuit Bees]], they can be handled in a similar manner.

<!--T:5-->
Mask Hornets take 6 seconds to open doors, meaning trapping them behind a door is a useful strategy, such as with [[Coil-head]]s and [[Jester]]s.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Notes}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:6-->
Mask Hornets are invincible, so talk with your teammates before killing [[Butler]]s, as secretly releasing these can be a detriment to your run.

<!--T:7-->
There are certain places where Mask Hornets cannot reach you, allowing you to hide until getting teleported out or getting help.
</translate>

== {{section heading/History}} ==

=== {{section heading/Version X|50}} ===
<translate>
<!--T:8-->
Introduced.
</translate>

Artifice.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox | moon = Artifice | routecost = 1500 | image = Artifice_map.png }} {{spoilers}} '''Artifice''' is a secret moon. It has a hazard level of "S++", the highest in the game and the only accessible moon with this level. The cost to change the ship's orbit to Artifice is 1500 credits. It does not appear in the list from the Terminal's MOONS command. Instead, the only reference to it is on [[Adamance]], painted into the walls inside of the shack. == Description == {{Template:Terminal|text=68-Artifice ---------------------- CONDITIONS: Abandoned facilities are littered across the landscape, which was once teeming with life. HISTORY: Weapons and classified technology has been found at various sites on the surface, dating back at least two hundred years. FAUNA: It's rumored that active machinery has been left behind.}} == Environment == Artifice is a strangely empty and eerie map. Everything around looks decayed and abandoned with a fallen powerline near the front. Around the moon, there are many convenient hiding areas such as a shack on the right side to be used as a hiding spot. This shack contains a secret entrance in the back that leads to outside the fence. This map contains four warehouses, one of which is inaccessible. All of the warehouses have a switch inside to open or close their garage door. The closest warehouse appears to be a hospital with an office. Both of these rooms make great hiding spots. The opposite warehouse is a large, empty garage with some catwalks and a single room that leads outside. This room can be used as a great way to evade certain enemies. The furthest warehouse is filled with fog for an unknown reason. To enter it, you either must enter through its back door, the adjacent warehouse's catwalk, or the stairs around the side. == Strategy == Spawning the second most amount of scrap of any moons on average, Artifice can be high-risk, high-reward moon. Unique to this moon are a set of warehouses with interactable levers. This can be used against [[Old Bird]]s and [[Forest Giant|Forest Keepers]] that spawn here to remove the threat they pose to any travels to and from the ship. The ship spawns on a hill relative to the main outdoor area, making it hard to get back and forth without running uphill to reach the ship, which could be dangerous with any nearby entities. To the right of the main entrance (and straight ahead out of the Fire Exit) is a small hut with a damaged wall that can be used to enter/leave the fenced off area if the main way in is too risky, although this route can be far slower. Occasionally, Old Birds will spawn inside the warehouse; it may be best to close the warehouse if they do, effectively trapping them. Alternatively, keeping one Old Bird roaming allows it to kill Forest Keepers, [[Baboon Hawk|Baboon Hawks]], and [[Eyeless Dog]]s, which may be harder to deal with than one Old Bird. Aside from Old Birds, you can lure in and trap other entities inside the warehouses. == Loot table == {{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Artifice}} {{special scrap spawn explainer|hives=y|facility=y|nutcrackers=y|butlers=y}} == Entities == === Indoor === Every indoor entity can spawn here. The [[Nutcracker]]s are by far the largest threat due to their range and high damage output, especially if more than one appear. While the [[Ghost Girl]] has one of the lowest chances of appearing, it is a bit of a step up compared to the easier moons she can spawn on: [[Experimentation]], [[Adamance]] and [[Assurance]]. {{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}} === Nighttime === {{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}} === Daytime === {{Template:WikiAutomated/DaytimeEntities}} == Data chips == There is 1 data chip on the surface:
"[[Sigurd_Logs#16._Work_-_Oct_15|Work]]" is behind the left warehouse closest to the main entrance. {{navbox moons}}

'''Embrion''' is a secret moon in Version 50. The cost to change the ship's orbit to Embrion is 150 credits. It does not appear on the list with the Moons command. Instead, its only reference is located in the Bestiary entry for [[Old Bird]]. It has a hazard level of "S".

== Description == <!--T:2-->
</translate>

{{Template:Terminal|text=5-Embrion

<nowiki>----------------------</nowiki>
<translate>

<!--T:3-->
CONDITIONS: Desolate and flat with unbreathable air, Embrion's surface layer is almost entirely made of amethyst quartz, which is made possible by Embrion's distance to its nearest star and its resulting geothermal activity. The surface of Embrion is scorchingly hot.

<!--T:4-->
HISTORY: Embrion was discovered in just the last century. It appears to have been used as a testing ground for Old Birds long before it was officially discovered. As a result of this danger, its status has been marked as Condemned. There are theories that a diverse geothermal ecosystem may be thriving within a vast cave network under Embrion's surface.

<!--T:5-->
FAUNA: Embrion is devoid of biological life.</translate>}}
<translate>

<!--T:14-->
== Environment ==
Embrion is a purplish moon with several ridges and shallow craters. The main entrance is located on a ledge requiring employees to jump to access it. 

== Strategy == <!--T:6-->

<!--T:7-->
As the Old Bird's bestiary entry implies, this moon has a considerably high spawn rate for Old Birds, and the highest entity power of any moon, meaning that Old Birds can swarm the map very quickly over the course of the day. The low amount of scrap means that this moon is hardly viable for looting purposes, resulting in low reward for such a high risk.

Adamance.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{#invoke:Moons/AutoInfobox|infobox | moon = Adamance | routecost = 0 | image = Adamance moon map.png }} '''Adamance''' is a free moon added in version 50 as one of the three intermediate moons along with [[Offense]] and [[March]]. It has a hazard level of "B" and is arguably the most dangerous free moon. == Description == {{Template:Terminal|text=20-Adamance ---------------------- CONDITIONS: Erosion and geographical forces have resulted in a landscape of deep valleys and mountains. HISTORY: Adamance is the biggest and oldest of what are colloquially referred to as the "Green Witches": Vow, March, and Adamance, which orbit No Service. FAUNA: Adamance is home to a lively, diverse ecosystem of smaller-sized omnivores.}} == Environment == Adamance has a forest landscape, similar to that of [[Vow]] and [[March]], with many red-leaved trees across its surface. However a key difference between other forest moons being the prominently steep terrain which should be navigated with caution.
The area in front of the ship door, the main entrance, and the bottom of the valley have large numbers of flowers. There is a small cabin at one end of the valley similar to the one on [[Rend]], which contains one of the [[Sigurd Logs]] and the words "[[Artifice|68 ART]]" painted on a wall. The cabin is always locked at the beginning of the day, requiring a [[Key]] or [[Lockpicker]] to enter. The interior is most often the [[Factory]], but can also be the [[Mineshaft]], or on rare occasion the [[Mansion]]. == Strategy == Getting to Adamance's main entrance is relatively easy, only requiring a brief trek up the cliff opposite the ship. However, due to Adamance's cliff faces near the ship and the main entrance, it can be difficult to reach those places if in a hurry and the narrow paths can be easily blocked by most outdoor entities. There are 2 bridges on Adamance: The one spanning from the ship to the main entrance uses the [[Bridge|normal bridge mechanics]]. The shorter one, providing easier access to the fire exit, always breaks the 4th time an employee lands on it, including jumping in place. The hills can be quickly descended by hugging close to them. It is possible to circumvent the valley and the bridge by scaling the left side of the hill behind the cabin, where the slope is the least steep. There is a permanent quicksand pit to the right of the main entrance. === Weather === Adamance can have any type of weather. When flooded, it has the maximum water level at the beginning of the day (requiring the use of the bridges to reach the building) and slowly drains over time, making trips through the valley possible. == Loot table == {{#invoke:Moons/AutoTables|lootTables|Adamance}} {{special scrap spawn explainer|hives=y|facility=y|sapsuckers=y|nutcrackers=y|butlers=y}} == Entities == === Indoor === {{Template:WikiAutomated/IndoorEntities}} === Nighttime === {{Template:WikiAutomated/NighttimeEntities}} === Daytime === {{Template:WikiAutomated/DaytimeEntities}} == Data chips == There are 2 data chips on the surface: "[[Sigurd_Logs#9._Idea_-_Sep_18|Idea]]" is at the end of the valley bridged by the broken bridge to the fire exit. "[[Sigurd_Logs#14._Team_Synergy_-_Oct_10|Team Synergy]]" is inside of the cabin, on a shelf in the back. == {{section heading/Trivia}} == Upon routing the ship to Adamance, instead of "Please enjoy your flight", the terminal will say "Good luck." This message is shared with [[Titan]] and [[Embrion]]. In early v50 development, Adamance was instead named Constancy, while the name "Adamance" was used for the moon that became [[Artifice]].{{cite Patreon|date=2024-04-07|slug=version-50-post-101884472|title=The Version 50 Post}} == {{section heading/Gallery}} == ExcessiveGrass.gif| A frankly absurd amount of grass placed on Adamance while optimizing performance.{{cite Patreon|date=2025-11-14|slug=marching-on-143536002|title=Marching on!}} Grasswave.gif| Grass swaying in the wind. GrassFog.png| Pre-[[Version 80]] grass visible through fog. EarlyV80Grass.png| An early version of the renderer optimizations made in v80. Note that grass fades into fog properly. == {{section heading/Notes and references}} == '''{{section heading/Notes}}''' Cadaver Growths utilize the [[Mechanics#Special_Enemies|special enemy system]] to guarantee spawns, meaning one will always spawn at the very beginning of the day on Adamance. '''{{section heading/References}}''' {{navbox moons}}

== Mechanics == <!--T:4-->

=== Attacking === <!--T:5-->

<!--T:6-->
Upon spawning, Tulip Snakes begin to roam the map. Upon seeing an employee, they enter an attacking phase in which they hiss and lunge at the employee in an attempt to cling to them. If the Tulip Snake misses, it turns around and lunges again if it still sees the employee. If it does not see the employee, it returns to roaming.

<!--T:7-->
If a Tulip Snake sees multiple employees, it attacks the employee with the most Tulip Snakes on their head. If every employee has the same number of Tulip Snakes on their head, it attacks the employee with the highest client ID; that is, the employee whose name is listed highest on the crew list in the pause menu.

=== Clinging === <!--T:8-->

<!--T:9-->
After a successful attack, the Tulip Snake clings to the employee, and it begins flapping its wings after 4 seconds in an attempt to lift the employee off the ground. If there is 1 Tulip Snake clinging to the employee, it flaps its wings for 4 seconds, while if there are 2 or more, they flap their wings for 6 to 25 seconds. Once they stop flapping, there is a cooldown of 4 to 9 seconds before they can start flapping again.

<!--T:10-->
2 or more Tulip Snakes must be attached in order to successfully lift an employee, and up to 6 Tulip Snakes may cling to one employee at a time. While an employee is being lifted by Tulip Snakes, they may maneuver in the air as if they were using a [[jetpack]], but they cannot control their height. The more Tulip Snakes attached to an employee, the higher and faster they will fly.

<!--T:11-->
Tulip Snakes remain clinging for 30 to 60 seconds, and after this timer runs out, all Tulip Snakes release the employee, flee for 10 seconds, then return to roaming.

<!--T:12-->
Each clinging Tulip Snake weighs the employee down by {{pounds|3}}.

<!--T:13-->
Tulip Snakes can be brought into the facility if the employee they are clinging to goes inside.

=== Despawning === <!--T:14-->

<!--T:15-->
At 3:21 PM, all Tulip Snakes fly away and disappear from the map, releasing any employees they are clinging to.

== Strategy == <!--T:16-->

<!--T:17-->
Unlike most entities, Tulip Snakes can be extremely helpful for an employee who knows how to use them to their advantage. By purposely chasing and getting Tulip Snakes to attach, and then timing a jump as they begin to flap allows employees to quickly traverse cliffs, long stretches of terrain, or get to places normally inaccessible.

<!--T:18-->
It is recommended to always make sure you are fairly close to the ground while flying with Tulip Snakes, or be aware of when you will reach ground before flight runs out. Otherwise, gravity will take a toll on your physical health.

<!--T:19-->
Tulip Snakes are a very scrappy and inconsistent entity, and because of this, always make sure to keep an eye out for opportunities to use them! If a giant is patrolling the path back to the ship, see if you can gather enough lizards to simply fly past it and ignore the risk.

<!--T:20-->
Should you want to kill a Tulip Snake, they will die to a single [[Shovel]] or [[Knife]] swing,
despite ostensibly having 3 health.

== Trivia == <!--T:24-->

<!--T:25-->
Tulip Snakes utilize the same mechanic as [[Jetpack|jetpacks]]. If an employee carries a Tulip Snake on their head while flying a jetpack, they will plummet the moment the Tulip Snake starts flapping its wings, effectively overriding the jetpack.

Barber.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity|id=ClaySurgeon | name = Barber | altname = Clay Surgeon | power_level = 1 | diversity_level = 1 | max_count = 1 | door_speed_multiplier = 0.3 | pip_size = Small | attack_damage = Instant kill | attack_speed = | dps = | contact_damage = | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|ClaySurgeon}} | stun_multiplier = 1.35 | cause_of_death = Snipping }} The '''Barber''' is a hostile indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It moves by intermittently hopping short distances, accompanied by a drumroll noise when preparing to move, and a honking sound upon moving. It only renders itself visible to employees within close proximity to it, and if it moves in the way of one, it swiftly chops them in half with its giant scissors. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Embrion]]. Barbers are ghostly purple humanoids with mostly featureless bodies except for a pair of white gloves on their hands, with which they wield a giant pair of scissors with circular red handles. == Bestiary entry == {{quote bestiary|ClaySurgeon}} == Spawn chances == {{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == Mechanics == Barbers are invisible to employees outside of a small radius. Their position can be estimated by the sounds they make as they move around: a snare drum roll while charging, and a loud honking sound when they step forward. Barbers target and attempt to move toward employees within their line of sight. A Barber's movement is unpredictable, as it teleports to a random position to get closer to the employee it is targeting, though it is likely that it teleports to a position the employee was last standing at. If an employee touches a Barber's scissors at any point, they are instantly cut in half and killed. When an employee has been cut in half, only the top half of their body can be picked up. The first Barber that spawns becomes the "Master Barber", which leads every other Barber on the map. Other Barbers only make sounds and move around in sync with the Master Barber. This mechanic is no longer present past Version 62, as Barbers were changed so only one can spawn per day. Barbers' charge time starts out at 2.75 seconds between steps and decreases by 0.1 seconds every hour, causing them to become faster and more dangerous the longer that employees stay on a moon. == Strategy == * Use the Barber's snare and music to indicate when it'll attack. * The note played when it moves is very loud, so listen to it to know where it is. * In versions below 62: If you hear a barber, be prepared for another one to be close behind. They spawn in packs of two and stick close to each other due to the master barber system. == Trivia == * The Barber has the second shortest bestiary entry, consisting of only three sentences and being only beaten by the [[Nutcracker]]. == Sounds == {{audio gallery | A Barber preparing to move|File:SnareBuildup.ogg |br| The sounds a Barber makes upon moving|File:ParadeMarch1.ogg|File:ParadeMarch2.ogg|File:ParadeMarch3.ogg|File:ParadeMarch4.ogg|File:ParadeMarch5.ogg |br| A Barber snipping an employee in half|File:ScissorsSnip.ogg }} == History == {{translators' notes| Name is exactly: {{WDLS|16126|1|barber}} (an occupation name; a {{native Wikipedia link|en=barber}} does their work using {{native Wikipedia link|en=scissors}}) ID and other names: * ClaySurgeon: {{WDLS|16245|1|clay}} (as in {{native Wikipedia link|en=modelling clay}}) + {{WDLS|12753|2|surgeon}} (archaic synonym for 'barber') }} === [[Version 62]] === * The Barber's max spawn count has been decreased from 8 to 1. === [[Version 55]] === * Introduced. {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

{{Direct Quote}}
<translate>
<!--T:2-->
The '''Kidnapper Fox''', known internally as the "Bush Wolf", is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. It hides within patches of [[Vain Shroud]]s, residing within the largest patch and ambushing approaching employees. It uses a sticky tongue to snag individual employees, dragging them into its territory before decapitating and eating them. Unlike most entities, their ability to spawn is tied to Vain Shrouds, which have no restrictions on what [[Moons|moon]] they can appear.

<!--T:31-->
Kidnapper Foxes resemble giant [[w:Fox|foxes]], with dark red fur that fades into a lighter crimson around their neck, legs, and tail. Their fur is denser around their neck and tail. Their head appears to split into two separate, sideways-facing foxlike heads, with a mouth fitted with a pair of jaws with sharp teeth, as well as a very long, adhesive pink tongue which ends in a bulbous growth used for latching onto prey.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:3-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|BushWolf}}
<translate>
== Mechanics == <!--T:4-->

<!--T:5-->
The more [[Vain Shroud]]s are on a moon, the more likely a Kidnapper Fox is to appear. Using [[Weed killer]] on Vain Shrouds will lower this chance.
Once there are more than 15 shrouds, the Kidnapper Fox has a '''(# of shrouds) / 70''' chance of spawning every 2 hours. (The chance ranges from ~21.4% at 15 shrouds to 100% at 70 shrouds.)
The Kidnapper Fox hides within the largest group of shrouds on the moon, this is its "Favorite Spot," and it will usually stay near it. The Kidnapper Fox will only attack if an employee comes within its 'nest,' at which point it will grab them with its incredibly long tongue, causing them to drop everything they're holding, and slowly pull them towards its jaws. If an employee makes contact with the Kidnapper Fox's mouth, they will be chomped, and killed instantly.<tvar name="citation">{{cite YouTube|video-id=HtMPiuhExog|title=The AI Behind Kidnapper Fox|creator=alter ego}}</tvar>

<!--T:6-->
The range at which the Kidnapper Fox becomes aggressive is called its 'nest.' The range of the nest starts around 7.25m, but similar to the [[Maneater]], it will continue to grow the longer you stay on the moon. This growth has no limit and can easily cover the whole moon. To decrease the nest range, all (or all but one) employees should stare at the Kidnapper Fox while outside its nest. In solo play, a single employee looking at it will suffice.<!-- sourced from Fandom around 2024-12-27 -->

== Strategy == <!--T:7-->

<!--T:8-->
If a Kidnapper Fox encounters a group of employees, it may attempt to latch its tongue onto one of them and drag the employee away to devour soon after. Because of this, it's important to always have one member of the group carry a shovel to help any employees that get grabbed.

== Trivia == <!--T:18-->

<!--T:19-->
The Kidnapper Fox was removed from the game in [[Version 60]] due to widespread frustration with its mechanics. Frequent complaints included its aggressive spawns, high lethality, and managing [[Vain Shroud]]s being too expensive and time-consuming. However, they were eventually readded in the [[v80]] update with revised mechanics, such as Vain Shrouds not growing immediately at the ship's landing point.

== History == <!--T:21-->

=== [[Version 80]] === <!--T:22-->

<!--T:23-->
* Kidnapper Fox was readded.
* The Kidnapper Fox is now more afraid to leave its territory and more accurately determines the size of its territory.
* The Kidnapper Fox cannot spawn at the very beginning of the day.

=== [[Version 60]] === <!--T:24-->

<!--T:25-->
* Removed.

=== [[Version 55]] === <!--T:26-->

<!--T:27-->
* Introduced.

{{Direct Quote}}
<translate>
<!--T:1-->
The '''Maneater''' is a deceptively cute indoor [[Entities|entity]] resembling an insect.
While it initially appears to be a sensitive, carriable infant requiring constant care and maintenance,
it transforms into a monstrous, extremely fast and deadly enemy if upset or provoked.
The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Vow]].

<!--T:2-->
Maneaters initially appear in their infant form, with a round, segmented body, soft white skin, light pink eyes, six legs with two-toed feet, and a short pair of horned pedipalps.
Upon transforming into adults, they become much larger and gain a more hunched posture.
They have much longer legs, darker red eyes, a larger jaw with longer pedipalps as well as a row of sharp teeth, and patches of white, bristly hair along their back and chest.

== Bestiary entry == <!--T:3-->
</translate>
{{quote bestiary|CaveDweller}}
[[File:Maneater running away.png|alt=<translate><!--T:4--> A Maneater running after being removed from the facility</translate>|thumb|<translate><!--T:5--> A Maneater running after being removed from the facility.</translate>]]
[[File:Enraged Maneater.png|alt=<translate><!--T:6--> A signifficantly more scrungly, enraged Maneater lunging towards and employee</translate>|thumb|<translate><!--T:7--> An adult Maneater mid-lunge.</translate>]]

<translate>
== Spawn chances == <!--T:8-->
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}
<translate>
<!--T:9-->
''Note: Maneaters spawn chances are multiplied by 1.5 if the interior is a [[Mineshaft]]''

== Mechanics == <!--T:10-->

<!--T:11-->
If no [[employee]] has been spotted before, the Maneater will wander around using the default roaming AI most entities use for 26 seconds.
After this, it will walk towards the nearest employee for 14-26 seconds; if none are found, it will repeat this behavior until it finds an employee, dead body, scrap, or equipment.
If it sees an employee, it will begin to follow them. If it finds scrap or equipment, it will stop and stare at it for 12 seconds.

<!--T:12-->
If the Maneater is near any piece of scrap or equipment, every 1/5 of a second there is an 8% chance that the Maneater will eat it, with no way of recovering it.
Upon doing this it will also grow fatter up to 3 times.<ref name="ielucian-guide" />

<!--T:13-->
Maneaters follow employees for 22 seconds before wandering around again.
After seeing an employee for the first time, its behavior will change, searching for employees for 8-20 seconds before sitting for 8-20 seconds and repeating until it finds an employee.
Every 3 seconds the Maneater is sitting, there's a 10% chance it will fall over and start crying. 

<!--T:14-->
There are 2 important variables a Maneater uses around employees: The like meter, and the loneliness meter. 

<!--T:15-->
A Maneater will always like the first employee it meets, and whether it likes any subsequent employee or not is a 50% chance.
If a Maneater is being held by a disliked employee, it will start squirming and eventually start crying.

<!--T:16-->
After some time following a employee, the Maneater will start wandering around again, but this time in a curious state, where it will look for a new employee for around 15 seconds.
If no employee has been found in this time, the Maneater will become lonely, now looking for any employees for about 9 seconds.
If it fails to find anybody, it will start crying. 

<!--T:17-->
Maneaters can cry for any of the following reasons: 
* Being hit by any weapon ([[Shovel]], [[Stop Sign]], [[Shotgun]], etc.)
* Hearing any employee-made sound (Dropping [[scrap]], turning on/off [[Flashlight]]s, etc. Note that speech and movement will not trigger the crying.)
* Staying near a [[Body|dead body]] too long
* An employee being hit while carrying it
* Falling over while sitting
* An unliked player carries it for too long
* Being rocked for too long
* Being shaken
* Being brought outside

<!--T:18-->
The Maneater can be soothed by picking it up and rocking it.
If a disliked employee holds it or if it's rocked for too long, it will instead stop crying when dropped.
When it is brought outside, it will only stop crying if it is brought inside again and rocked.
If an employee double-clicks or has a high fear level, they will shake the Maneater, taking longer to calm it down in the process.
When shaken by an employee, it will run away from that employee when dropped.

<!--T:19-->
Maneaters can also run away while crying, which can be caused by any of the following reasons:
* Being hit with a weapon
* Hearing employee-made sounds while already crying
* Observing the dead body of an employee it liked{{ref code|CaveDwellerAI.DoBabyAIInterval}}
* The Maneater is already crying and the employee carrying it is hit
* When dropped after the employee carrying it dies
* Observing an employee that is [[Player Stats#Health|critically injured]]

<!--T:20-->
The crying indicates a countdown to its transformation, which cannot be reversed; While the Maneater is crying, the timer will start, and when it is calm, it will stop.
It starts at 17 seconds, but it will drop faster in the following scenarios:
* If it is crying while running, the timer will drop 40% faster.
* If it starts crying because a disliked employee is holding it, the timer will drop 3x as fast.
* If it gets hit with a weapon, this will subtract 10 seconds from the timer.

<!--T:21-->
If it starts crying before encountering any employee, the timer will instead drop considerably slower.

<!--T:22-->
The only way to gauge this time is if the Maneater vomits. If it does, then the timer at around 5 seconds remaining.{{ref code|CaveDwellerAI.BabyUpdate}}

<!--T:23-->
Once the timer is up, the Maneater will rapidly mature over the course of approximately 3 seconds.
After growing up, the Maneater will designate a random node to be its nest, and will stand still it in that node.
If it spawns on a Factory or Manor [[Interiors|interior]], it will be a random node, if it spawns on Mineshaft, it will be a random node inside the caves.
The nest will "grow" when at least 1 employee is inside the facility, and shrink when no employees are inside.

<!--T:24-->
While employees are inside its nest, the Maneater will always know where they are.
It will start to stalk employees, trying to get as close as possible without being spotted, just like [[Bracken]]s.
While in this phase, the Maneater will fake cry in 2 different ways to lure employees closer; one can be differentiated by the low-pitched sound it makes.

<!--T:25-->
If it gets closer than 7 units away from an employee, it will attack. The Maneater will squat down and lunge towards the closest employee, killing them instantly.
If the employee dodges the attack, it will begin chasing the employee and will leap again if it's closer than 4 units.
It stops chasing if the employee is 15 units away and has no line of sight with it, or if the employee exits the nest.

<!--T:26-->
When outside, it will forgo the nest system and will attack any employee closer than 23 units away.

<!--T:27-->
The Maneater has the properties of an item, and is programmed to attract lightning in [[stormy]] weather,
but this does not always function correctly.<ref group="note" name="conductive" /> This behavior is likely unintended.

=== Noise Sensitivity === <!--T:71-->

<!--T:72-->
In baby form, the Maneater can cry in response to noises that are too loud. Whether or not it will cry is based on the [[Audible_sounds|loudness]] of the noise and the distance between the maneater and the noise source. Sounds with a loudness of under 0.5 will not cause it to cry at any distance. Sounds with a loudness or 0.5 to 0.85 will make it cry if made less than 2 units away, and sounds at 0.85 or louder will make it cry if made less than 8 units away.

<!--T:73-->
If there is not a direct line of sight between the Maneater and the noise source, the effective noise distance is 3 units longer and the loudness is reduced by 40%.

== Strategy == <!--T:28-->

<!--T:29-->
Since it can be carried in baby form, the Maneater can be neutralized by simply dropping it into certain pits, trapping it for the rest of the day.
Deep pits within the cave system usually work, as well as the pit in the large elbow room in the Factory, but the mature form can teleport out of most other pits when transformed.
As of v62, most of these "cheese" strategies have been blocked.

</translate>[[File:Maneater.png|thumb|<translate><!--T:30--> An adult Maneater trapped in a pit.</translate>]]<translate>

<!--T:31-->
The Maneater has 5 HP and can be killed. This is not recommended, however, due to its speed and high attack, and can only be dealt 1 damage per hit (meaning it takes 5 [[Shotgun]] shots to kill it).

<!--T:32-->
The Maneater becomes extremely dangerous outdoors and should not leave the facility under any circumstance.

<!--T:33-->
When it transforms, as much distance should be made from it as possible. It attacks similarly to an [[Eyeless Dog]], in lunges with a short cooldown.

<!--T:34-->
It can be killed as an infant by being placed underneath a [[Spike Trap]].
This method requires a Spike Trap with a short timer, or one that is motion-activated, in order to work.
This is because a Spike Trap takes two hits to kill a Maneater, and the first will cause it to transform instantly.<tvar name="citation">{{cite YouTube|ref-id=ielucian-guide|video-id=5yCuicTt2DE|title=MANEATER GUIDE: Lethal Company <nowiki>[v60/v62 update]</nowiki>|creator=iElucian}}</tvar>

<!--T:35-->
The Maneater can also be consistently killed with [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=akCfu_4SY_Y door cheese] if done correctly.

== Trivia == <!--T:36-->

<!--T:69-->
The baby Maneater's design is based off the [[fandom:gravityfalls:Shape_Shifter|Shape Shifter]] from the animated television series ''[[w:Gravity Falls|Gravity Falls]]'', as can be seen in its concept art.

<!--T:70-->
The adult Maneater's design is based off ''[[w:Plutomurus ortobalaganensis|Plutomurus ortobalaganensis]]'', a species of springtail found in the [[w:Krubera Cave|Krubera Cave]] in Abkhazia, Georgia, the deepest known cave on Earth.

<!--T:37-->
The Maneater is the only creature that can be picked up.

<!--T:38-->
If the Maneater is spawned on [[Gordion]] with mods, it will sell for {{credits|0}}.

== Sounds == <!--T:39-->

<translate>
<!--T:5-->
The '''Giant Sapsucker''', also known internally as the "Giant Kiwi", is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. It is a potentially dangerous threat that protects its nest containing three [[egg]]s, rapidly attacks employees who get too close to its nest, and becomes enraged if its eggs are taken from it. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Vow]].

Giant Sapsuckers are large flightless birds with a head similar in appearance to that of a [[w:Pileated woodpecker|pileated woodpecker]], with a white face, black stripes around its eyes and beak, a vibrant red triangular crest, and glowing orange eyes. It has a brown stripe along the top of its beak, a crimson stripe wrapping around its cheeks and upper neck, and a sharp, elongated black beak. The rest of its rotund body is coated in dark black feathers, and it has long, thin yellow legs with three-digited toes, tipped with black talons.

== {{section heading/Bestiary entry}} ==
{{quote bestiary|GiantKiwi}}

<!--T:9-->
== Spawn chances ==
{{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}}

<!--T:10-->
== Mechanics ==
The Giant Sapsucker has six phases: resting, roaming, foraging, guarding, aggravated, and berserk.

<!--T:11-->
It will go from resting to roaming to foraging in a cycle for the entire day unless disturbed.

<!--T:12-->
In the resting phase it will go to its nest, crouch down, and sleep for around 15 seconds. Noises will cause it to wake up, do note that it cannot hear you speaking through your microphone.

<!--T:13-->
After waking up, it will enter its roaming phase and begin roaming around its nest. After roaming for a short time, it will begin foraging. In its foraging phase, it simply pecks at trees. It will usually peck one tree for a few seconds before stopping for a moment and then pecking again before going back to its rest phase. If there are no trees nearby it will simply roam for an extended period of time.

<!--T:14-->
The Giant Sapsucker also has a random chance to “dance” during its roaming phase.

<!--T:15-->
If anything gets within a certain distance of its nest, it will enter its guarding phase. When it notices something enter this area it will loudly squawk at them and quickly stand between them and the nest. If they get any closer the sapsucker will enter it’s aggravated phase and begin attacking by violently pecking, dealing 10 damage per peck to employees and 4 to other entities. The longer it attacks, the faster it's attack becomes. If you manage to get far away enough it will eventually stop attacking, although escaping this can be rather difficult. Baboon hawks will attempt to steal the sapsucker's eggs, usually ending rather poorly for the baboon hawks, and eyeless dogs may wander near the sapsucker's nest and get killed. The Giant Sapsucker appears to ignore or at least not attack [[Manticoil]]s, [[Tulip Snake]]s, and [[Forest Keeper]]s.

<!--T:16-->
If the Giant Sapsucker takes damage it will enter its berserk phase, where it will relentlessly attack the source of the damage, even if it is something that cannot be killed or that it has already killed, such as old birds, entity corpses, Exploded easter eggs, and conductive items, if it is struck by the lightning from said item. In its berserk phase you can take one of its eggs, however when the egg hatches and begins calling out, the sapsucker will target you.  

<!--T:17-->
Similar to beehives, the eggs values are determined via their distance from the ship, with the eggs being either “Near” (within 100 meters from ship, worth 40-69) or “far” (further than 100 meters from ship, worth 70-119) with “far” eggs also having a 30% chance to be worth over 119 with a a maximum of 199. The eggs weigh 19 lb and are 2-handed items 

<!--T:18-->
When an egg is taken it will shortly hatch and begin calling out. The hatchling will then stare at the employee for a second before calling out again. When the second calling begins, the sapsucker will know the employee's location and will chase the employee holding the egg until either the employee is killed or the egg is dropped. If an employee enters the facility with the egg, it will squawk endlessly, and the Giant Sapsucker will wait at the door the employee entered from. If an employee attempts to hide in the ship by closing the door, it will pry open the door with its beak, before letting out a loud shriek and beginning to attack. When the sapsucker reaches the egg, it will pick it up with its beak and return it to its nest, beyond this point, if the sapsucker sees any employees it will immediately attack them.

<!--T:19-->
== Strategy ==
Taking Sapsucker eggs, although highly valuable, is an extremely risky move and usually not worth it if you intend on keeping the Giant Sapsucker alive, because it essentially forces you to leave the moon after you have stolen an egg, due to its ability to break into the ship. If you would still like to try to take its eggs, there are many different methods to acquiring them.

<!--T:20-->
The simplest strategy is to sacrifice one of your teammates. All four employees go in near the end of the day after looting the facility and wait until she falls asleep, Three employees sprint jump in and then crouch, grab an egg and crouch away until you are far enough away to walk, and walk until you’re far enough away to sprint. One employee will stay behind to distract the Sapsucker for just long enough to allow the rest to escape, and after the 3 employees make it back to the ship they must leave immediately.

<!--T:21-->
If you are either going solo or do not want anyone to die, you can use [[TZP inhalant]], as this will allow you to reach the ship quickly enough so that you do not have to sacrifice anyone. 

<!--T:22-->
The [[Company Cruiser]] is by far one of the most effective tools you have against the Giant Sapsucker. Park the cruiser relatively near the nest and have one employee as the get-away driver. The other employees go in while the Sapsucker is sleeping, take the eggs, and quickly get into the back of the cruiser and drive away. Even if the Sapsucker begins chase, she is unlikely to halt the cruiser. Furthermore when the cruiser is attached to the ship magnet the Sapsucker becomes unable to hurt anyone inside, although do note that the Sapsucker can fling the the driver out of their seat.

<!--T:23-->
Although situational, the cruiser can be used to run over and kill the Giant Sapsucker. At high speeds the cruiser can deal up to 12 damage as of V70 meaning it would take 2 high speed collisions and either one more high speed collision or at least two slow collisions. The most reliable method is to floor it and run the Sapsucker over at high speeds once and then get the cruiser to be perpendicular to the ground, up against a wall, this way the Sapsucker cannot reach you. After this try to keep bouncing the cruiser on top of the Sapsucker till it dies, despite being relatively reliable it does take rather long to do this, and is only really a viable strategy if the Sapsucker spawns in [[Vow]]’s fire exit valley.

<!--T:24-->
On [[Adamance]], if the Sapsucker spawns near the fire exit bridge, it is quite easy to simply take an egg while she sleeps to then jump across the bridge and to the ship, as the Sapsucker will have to run across the entire map to reach you. Do note that in this case the Sapsucker will reach the ship only a mere few seconds after you do.

<!--T:25-->
There are several spots on each of the moons that the Sapsucker spawns on in which the Sapsucker cannot reach you. In these spots you can stand with a shovel and hit the Sapsucker until it dies, these spots include: behind the rock at main entrance on adamance, the stable flat part of the adamance fire exit bridge, a rock up against the hill in Adamance's fire exit valley, a rock up against a hill nearby Sigurd’s logs on assurance, an oddly shaped rock nearby Assurance's fire exit, parts the scaffolding nearby the ladder leading to fire exit on assurance, on top of the protruding decoration on Vow’s main building structure, a part of the debris of Vow's bridge when collapsed, and the protruding part of Vow’s dam, although the Sapsucker can easily push you off of this. You usually need to take one of the eggs to these spots and put it where the Sapsucker cannot reach so that it aggroes towards you, although if it roams near these spots it is possible to hit it with a shovel and run to safety. You can also stand on the seats of the company cruiser and the ship magnet to do this.
