Employee.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{infobox entity |image=Lethal-Company-Suit-Rank.jpg |image2=Waving_Employee.png |altname=Player |hits-to-kill={{hits to kill|10 |extra={{icon knife}}{{#expr:1+ceil((100-20)/10)}} |override-shotgun={{#expr:ceil(100/100)}}–{{#expr:ceil(100/20)}} }} |stun_multiplier=1 |attack_damage=N/A (see weapons' articles) }} {{about|the player-controlled entities as they appear to others |the informational display shown to players|HUD |the employment relationship|Lore#The Company }} The hazmat-suit-wearing player characters of ''Lethal Company'', known as ''''employees'''', 'great assets', or simply ''''players'''', are naturally the most important and complex entities in the game.
They only spawn when a player joins a lobby, Actually, there are always 4 employees in the lobby, with inactive employees stored out of bounds. only despawn when the player dies (see below) or leaves the lobby, and automatically respawn when the [[ship]] returns to orbit. An employee appears as a humanoid, covered head to toe in an [[w:Hazmat_suit|NBC/hazmat suit]], by default in [[Orange_suit|bright orange]] but other [[suits]] can be unlocked with [[credits]]. No facial features are visible through the visor.
Employees wear oxygen tanks on their backs, but this is purely decorative and has no relation to the game's [[drowning]] mechanic. Players' [[Employee_rank|ranks]] are displayed in the form of a patch on their lapel.
Others can see the item an employee is holding, but not any "pocketed" items. The exception to this is the [[Belt bag]], which appears on the waist when not held. == Mechanics == Like other entities, [[damage]] will decrease an employee's [[health]], and with enough damage they will reach 0 health and die (with the player becoming a [[spectator]]). Damage sources have a different effect on employees than on other entities.
Depending on the cause of death, the employee may or may not be replaced by a [[body]], and may or may not drop all their held items. Notably, equipping a [[mask]] or dying to a [[Masked]] will cause the employee to become a Masked. Every employee has a 4-slot "inventory" or "pockets" which can each hold a single item of [[equipment]] or [[scrap]]. There is no maximum carry weight, however [[weight]] does reduce the employee's agility, and certain items will put the employee into the [[hands full]] state. Employees can hear all [[Audible_sounds|diegetic sounds]], beyond what other entities can hear. There are also sonic hallucinations tied to paranoia (not to be confused with [[fear]]). == Notes ==

Backwater Gunkfish.wikitext: {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity | name = Backwater Gunkfish | sciname = Infectum linteum | altname = | danger = 0% | power_level = 0.5 | diversity_level = 1 | max_count = 6 | door_speed_multiplier = | see_through_fog = | pip_size = | attack_damage = 0 | attack_speed = | dps = | contact_damage = | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|Stingray}} | stun_multiplier = 1 | id = Stingray}} {{redirect|Goopy Goblin|scrapped/teased entities|Cut Content}} The '''Backwater Gunkfish''', known internally as the "Stingray", is a docile, nonlethal indoor [[Entities|entity]]. It lies motionless on the ground, but flees while releasing a trail of goo when attacked or disturbed. The goo makes the ground slippery, while also temporarily covering an employee's visor upon aggravating it, distorting their vision. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[March]]. Backwater Gunkfish resemble terrestrial [[w:Stingray|stingrays]], having a flat green disk-shaped body with lighter green spots on their fins. They have two pairs of small, black beady eyes and five bright purple tentacles near their mouth. The area around their eyes and upper back, as well as their stinger and tail, have a deeper purple color. == {{section heading/Bestiary entry}} == {{quote bestiary|Stingray}} == {{section heading/Spawn chances}} == {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances|Stingray}} == {{section heading/Mechanics}} == The Gunkfish has 2 main states. === Evading === When threatened by an employee (either by them being within 1.5 units of the Gunkfish, within 5 units and in its 110° line of sight for 10 consecutive seconds, or by attacking it), the Gunkfish enters an aggressive evasion mode during which it attempts to flee, squealing and leaving behind a trail of goo while moving at a speed of up to 11 units per second. After 10 seconds of continuous evasion, the Gunkfish calms down, resulting in it ceasing to produce goo, switching to a flopping animation, slowing down to a speed of 6 units per second, and ceasing vocalisations. During this calm phase, it will path to a suitable hiding spot. After entering this state, the Gunkfish will attempt to "spit" at any nearby employees. The check window, starting at ~0.1 seconds after the evading state is triggered, lasts for 0.15 seconds. During this time, any employees that pass these checks are sprayed: * Positioned in an area spanning from the Gunkfish's head to 5 units in front '''AND''' ** Less than 2.5 units from the body's position '''OR''' ** Gunkfish's head has line of sight with the employee's head. The spray severely impedes the employee's vision, covering their visor in a thick green-yellow goo, persisting for 10 seconds. === Hiding === After it has reached its desired hiding spot and 10 seconds have elapsed without the Gunkfish detecting any threats, it enters its hiding state where it "cloaks". After 45 seconds, if no employees are detected within 30 units of it, it will transition back to the evasion state. Otherwise, it stays in the hiding state, repeating this check every 15 seconds. === Goo === The goo produced lasts indefinitely, drastically reducing employee friction, functioning similar to ice, causing uncontrollable sliding. === Hiding Spots === The Gunkfish chooses hiding spots based on employees in its proximity. * It initially targets locations far away from the main entrance. * When an employee is detected within 4 units, it selects a hiding spot in a node close to the employee's last known position. * If the Gunkfish has recently sprayed an employee, it selects a hiding spot far from them (approximately 40 units away) * If no employees are visible, it hides near scrap items within a 28-unit radius, positioning itself 2-6 units away from the item. The hiding spots are calculated in the evasion state, after spitting. == {{section heading/Strategy}} == If you encounter a Backwater Gunkfish while on a moon, it is best not to disturb it. While they are weak, they are not aggressive and cannot harm employees directly. The Gunkfish can easily be jumped over should one block your path, which may be seen as a better tactic as opposed to forcing it to move. == {{section heading/History}} == {{translators' notes| Name is: {{WDLS|316650|2|backwater}} (a run-down town or region, where nothing important ever happens) + {{WDLS|16873|1|gunk}} (synonym for dirt or slime) + {{WDLS|4080|1|fish}} ID and other names: * Stingray: {{WDLS|328529|1|stingray}} (a kind of {{native Wikipedia link|en=ray (fish)}}) }} The Backwater Gunkfish was added in the Version 80 public beta. {{navbox entities}}

Feiopar.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity | name = Feiopar | sciname = Felis arborum | altname = Hide-behind, Gougar | danger = 70% | power_level = 2 | diversity_level = 2 | max_count = 6 | door_speed_multiplier = | see_through_fog = Yes | pip_size = | attack_damage = 7 | attack_speed = | cause_of_death = Scratching | dps = | contact_damage = | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|Puma}} | stun_multiplier = 0.3 | id = Puma }} The '''Feiopar''', internally known as the "Puma", is a hostile outdoor [[Entities|entity]]. Often seen hunting in packs, it slowly stalks and approaches its prey from behind, before tearing them to pieces with its claws once it gets close. Upon being spotted, it hides behind trees, and can either retreat or approach from great distances by climbing and leaping between treetops. The [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Vow]]. Feiopars are large, slender big cats with glowing yellow eyes and a gaping mouth with sharp teeth, similar in appearance to [[w:Black panther|black panthers]]. They have deep black fur speckled with rosettes, and have a long, prehensile tail. They emit a low growling sound while stalking prey. == {{section heading/Bestiary entry}} == {{quote bestiary}} == {{section heading/Spawn chances}} == {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances}} == {{section heading/Mechanics}} == Feiopars roam the outdoors in search of prey, climbing between trees. Once they spot prey, they emit a growl and begin to stalk the prey on the ground. If they are spotted, they will freeze or take cover behind a nearby tree until the prey looks away. However, if the Feiopar gets within 3.5 meters of its prey, it will instead attack when spotted.{{ref code|PumaAI.Update}} Looking at a stalking Feiopar while it is not within attack range makes it more scared; past a certain point, it will give up pursuit. The Feiopar has 3 states. === Tree Hiding === This is the default/idle state of the Feiopar. While in this state, it will run to a nearby tree, climbing it at a speed of 14 units per second after a 0.24 second delay. If no employee has been spotted recently, it will attempt to leap to a nearby tree after 0.75–4 seconds. If there are no nearby trees to leap to, it will drop to the ground after 6 seconds and attempt to find another tree to climb. If an employee has been spotted, it will stay in the tree. It will then drop if either of the below checks pass: '''Check 1''' * An employee is within 4.5 units of the Feiopar's tree * The employee is looking almost directly at the Feiopar (view angle < 36°) '''Check 2''' * The Feiopar has been on its target tree for 6 seconds or more * The targeted played is within 60 units of the Feiopar * The employee is not looking at the tree (view angle > 70°) === Stalking === In this state, the Feiopar has dropped to the ground with a definite target in mind. It will try to approach the employee at a speed determined by its distance from the employee: * < 4.5 units away: 9 units per second * 4.5–7 units away: 5 units per second * 7–10 units away: 6 units per second * > 10 units away: 8 units per second If at any point an employee looks at the Feiopar (view angle < 67°) for 5 consecutive frames, it will freeze. If after 1.5 seconds of being frozen the employee is less than 15.5 units away from the Feiopar, as well as not directly looking at it (view angle ≥ 25°), it will unfreeze and charge towards the employee at speeds from 3.5–7 units per second, inevitably triggering the attack phase. === Attacking === The Feiopar will switch to this state if either of the following checks pass: '''Check 1''' * The employee has direct eye contact with the Feiopar (view angle < 25°) * The employee is less than 3.5 units away from the Feiopar '''Check 2''' * The employee is less than 2.5 units away from the Feiopar '''Check 3''' * The Feiopar is frozen * The employee is less than 15.5 units away from the Feiopar * The employee is not looking directly at the Feiopar (view angle ≥ 25°) * The Feiopar has been frozen for more than 1.5 seconds While attacking, it will "scratch" the employee every 0.2 seconds on average if it is within 6 units of the Feiopar, dealing 7 hitpoints of damage with each scratch. == {{section heading/Strategy}} == Hitting a Feiopar with a [[shovel]] or other weapon can frighten it away, even without doing enough damage to kill it. Despite the bestiary entry making mention of the Feiopar being agitated by the color orange and high-visibility markings, changing into a different [[Suits|suit]] has no effect on its behaviour at all. == {{section heading/Trivia}} == The Feiopar was inspired by the "Hide Behind", a creature from the animated series ''[[w:Gravity Falls|Gravity Falls]]''.{{cite Patreon|date=2025-12-30|slug=reviewing-2025-146974057|title=Reviewing 2025}} Even though the terminal entry states that Feiopars are found in the "Green Witches" ([[Vow]], [[March]] and [[Adamance]]), they cannot spawn in Adamance. Feiopars and [[Eyeless dog]]s are closely related, both belonging to the family Saeptivus. == {{section heading/History}} == {{translators' notes| Name is: completely original; possibly formed by merging {{WDLS|33091|1|feline}} (more general term for cat) + {{WDLS|37061|1|leopard}} (a species of 'big cat') ID and other names: * Puma: exactly {{WDLS|326112|2|puma}} (another species of 'big cat') }} === [[Version 80]] === * Introduced. == {{section heading/References}} == {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

Cadaver Growth.wikitext:
{{interlangbox}} {{Infobox entity | name = Cadaver Growth | sciname = Moderantum flos | danger = 100% | power_level = 2 | diversity_level = 2 | max_count = 1 | door_speed_multiplier = | see_through_fog = | pip_size = | attack_damage = N/A | attack_speed = | dps = | contact_damage = | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|CadaverGrowths}} | stun_multiplier = 0.25 | id = CadaverGrowths | cause_of_death = Suffocation }} The '''Cadaver Growth''' is a tall, infectious fern [[Entities|entity]] that grows indoors. They spread autonomously at a quick rate and emit a cloud of spores wherever they are present. Should an employee linger in the spore cloud for too long, they will eventually become infected and, without treatment, die and be turned into a [[Cadaver Bloom]]. == {{section heading/Bestiary entry}} == {{quote bestiary|Cadavers}} == {{section heading/Spawn chances}} == {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances|CadaverGrowths}} == {{section heading/Mechanics}} == === Growth === Cadaver Growths will spawn through a vent like other indoor entities, though will pick a random indoor tile to spawn on and will not necessarily be next to the vent they spawned from. When picking a tile to spawn on, all room tiles are valid, but in a [[Mineshaft]], only a cave tile will be selected. This tile is made to be the first "growth tile".{{ref code|CadaverGrowthAI.GetRandomTileToStart()}} A Cadaver Growth's plant growth rate is first determined by the time of day. An [[Mechanics#Animation_curves|Animation curve]] that is sampled according to the time of day and then multiplied by 8 is utilized to determine the interval between the growth of each plant. This rate is slowest at the beginning of the day at around 8 seconds, decreases to approximately 0.26 seconds around 3pm, and increases back to 2 seconds by the end of the day.{{ref code|CadaverGrowthAI.DoAIInterval()}} As a result, Cadaver Growth growth rate is not determined by time since it spawned, but simply by the time of day. Cadaver Growths will also experience growth bursts, in which the interval that plants will grow at is set to a flat 0.15 seconds for 4 seconds. Growth bursts occur on a 25 second interval and last for 4 seconds each, meaning there are 21 seconds of "downtime" between growth bursts. Before 9:36 AM, "downtime" progresses 66% as fast, meaning the first growth burst occurs after 38 seconds and there are 32 seconds between bursts. When a Cadaver Growth plant is destroyed using [[Weed killer]], a 2 second buffer is subtracted from the time since a plant last grew. This buffer stacks for each plant destroyed and maxes out at 8 seconds, meaning that with consistent weed killer usage, plant growth can be prevented indefinitely. If the plant destroyed was the last plant in the tile, then it will be marked as "eradicated". When a Cadaver Growth plant is growing, each growth tile is iterated through to decide what tile it is grown on. A tile's plant capacity is 40 by default, but is set to 10 if it is A) a cave tile, or B) not a room tile and either within a [[Interiors#The_Mansion|Mansion]] or not a tunnel tile. If there are less plants than the tile's capacity present, an 80% chance is passed and the current growth tile is not eradicated, then the plant will grow either near an existing plant or an AI node if none exist.{{ref code|CadaverGrowthAI.GrowInTile()}} After a plant grows, a random adjacent tile that was eradicated over 45 seconds ago is reset to allow plant growth again. This is skipped if there are less than 7 plants grown in the current tile, there are no valid adjacent tiles to spread to, or a roll of spreadChance/100 fails. spreadChance is calculated by picking a value between -12 and 14 at the percentage of (# of plants / tile capacity), then clamped between 0 and 100. If there are more than 5 growth tiles, it will be reduced by 34%. === Becoming infected === To become a Cadaver Bloom, an employee must stay within a certain proximity of the Cadaver Growth's spores. This chance is set when entering the same room as a Cadaver Growth and is changed in the following ways: * Increased as proximity to a plant increases * Increased over time spent near plants * Increased when standing near an infected employee * Increased (20%) if health is at or below 60% * Increased (50%) if critically injured * Decreased over time spent away from plants * Decreased as percent of employees infected increases * Decreased (25%) if completely healthy In multiplayer, employees are given 4 seconds of complete immunity per day from infection. In solo, they are given 7 seconds, and a message displaying filter quality is displayed:
HEALTH RISK!
Air filter overwhelmed by particulates;
Filter quality: '''x'''%
If the chance of infection is equal to or higher than 1.5%, a notice will appear in the HUD warning the employee of a health risk posed by particulate matter overwhelming their air filter. As a result, infection can begin while the warning is not actively shown, but not unless the warning has been seen at least once, as the immunity does not decrease unless the warning is being shown.{{ref code|CadaverGrowthAI.InfectPlayers}} === Infection phase === When an employee is infected, the "Infection" phase begins. In this phase, an employee can be cured by entering a [[shower]] before 8% or by spraying them with weed killer. Weed killer will only deal damage to an infected employee. When taking a shower before 8% infection, the following HUD message is displayed:
FIGHTING INFECTION!
Reduction in fever
When infected, a 60% chance for the infection to be "severe" will roll. If this succeeds, the employee will be severely infected. Severe infection will give a chance to hear the sound of vines growing in the infected employee's head at or above 80% infection. Infection progression begins at a rate of 0.78% per second, meaning with no changing circumstance, infection will reach 100% in 128.2 seconds, or about 3 in-game hours. This can be accelerated by being near other employees, health being below 40%, or being outside directly in sunlight before 4:48PM. In multiplayer, yellow petals start sprouting out of an employee's back according to a chance that increases from 5% to 50% over the infection phase percentage. These petals will be able to be scanned by other employees using {{keybind|Ping scan}} and will display the same node as Cadaver Growths. It will also be visible on the terminal as a small red dot on the employee (but will not be visible to the infected employee themselves). In solo, no petals are grown, and instead a HUD warning that reads as the following is displayed at or above 80%:
HIGH FEVER DETECTED!
REACHING '''x'''°F
Once an employee has reached 35%, they will bloom on death in most cases - dying to [[Eyeless dog]]s, [[Barber]]s, and [[Giant Sapsucker]]s are the only cases in which this will not happen. === Burst phase === Once the "Infection" phase reaches 100%, the "Burst" phase begins. At this point, an employee can no longer be cured with weed killer, and will instead instantly bloom once sprayed. Burst progression is 31.25% per second in solo. In multiplayer, it begins at 25% per second, but is multiplied depending on the proximity of the closest employee. The multiplier ranges from 0.015x when 30+ tiles away from an employee to 3x when inside of one. As the last employee alive, a base rate of 18.75% per second is used instead. Once this phase reaches 90%, a flat timer is used to progress to 100%; for solo employees and the last employee alive in multiplayer, the timer is 18 seconds, and in other multiplayer situations the timer is 1.7 seconds. This timer stops the moment the ship begins to leave, so employees cannot bloom after the ship lever has been pulled. When burst progression is 90% or higher, the infected employee's vision will immediately become blurry and will turn progressively red over time, and a ringing and rumbling sound plays constantly. This effect is more noticeable in 18 second timer cases. In multiplayer, even if they are the last employee alive, their movement will also become hindered, in a similar way to being critically injured. For solo employees and the last employee alive in multiplayer, a HUD warning is displayed that reads the following at 90%:
HIGH FEVER DETECTED!!!
FOREIGN BODIES DETECTED!!!
IRREGULAR BRAINWAVE DETECTED!!!
=== Blooming === Once the "Burst" phase reaches 100%, the employee "blooms" and is replaced with a [[Cadaver Bloom]]. Nearby employees that are at 85% or more of the infection phase will also bloom. == {{section heading/Strategy}} == Like [[Vain shroud]]s, Cadaver Growths can be removed when sprayed with weed killer. However, due to its short range, an employee will often find themselves within range of infection when trying to kill Cadaver Growths, so this is not recommended. Their growth rate is also high enough that, if not caught at the very beginning of the day, it will quickly spiral out of being controllable. Alternatively, it is much more cost-effective and low-maintenance to simply cure infected employees. Due to the low cost and high spray count for a single bottle of weed killer, it is much more reasonable to keep a bottle indoors to use on each other while being mindful of the time that is spent in Cadaver Growths. Employees should not worry about spraying each other and possibly triggering a premature bloom -- due to the proximity multiplier for the bloom phase, an employee would already bloom extremely quickly (about 3 seconds) when in range of being sprayed. It is more beneficial to risk this premature bloom if it is possible to cure the infected employee. While visual signs such as yellow petals can be used to identify infection, these will usually display at higher levels, which will require more sprays to cure the infected employee. As weed killer will only damage infected employees, spraying them can be used to "check" for infection, not requiring any visual signs and at zero risk to the employee. Despite this, it is still useful to keep an eye out for yellow petals and small red dots on terminal employee icons, in case a group gets separated for an extended period of time. == {{section heading/Sounds}} == {{audio gallery | A Cadaver Growth growing a new plant.|File:PlantGrow.ogg |br| A Cadaver Growth emitting spores.|File:PlantSpores.ogg |br| An infected employee coughing spores when hit with Weed killer. |File:SporeDamage.ogg|File:SporeDamageB.ogg|File:SporeDamageC.ogg|File:SporeDamageD.ogg |br| Vines growing in an infected employee's head, only audible to themselves.|File:VineInHead1.ogg|File:VineInHead2.ogg|File:VineInHead3.ogg|File:VineInHead4.ogg |br| Head ringing an infected employee hears in the late-stage burst phase.|File:HeadSwellingRinging.ogg |br| "Dizziness" an infected employee hears in the late-stage burst phase.|File:PoisonDizziness.ogg }} == {{section heading/History}} == === Version 80 === Introduced. == {{section heading/Notes and references}} == '''{{section heading/Notes}}''' The fever temperature begins at 101°F and increases steadily over the infection period to 112°F. This percentage decreases from 100% over the 7 second timer, and is replaced with "Filter inoperative!" when depleted. Cadaver Growths utilize the [[Mechanics#Special_Enemies|special enemy system]] to guarantee spawns, meaning one will always spawn at the very beginning of the day on Adamance. '''{{section heading/References}}''' {{navbox entities}} [[Category:Entities]]

Cadaver Bloom.wikitext:
{{interlangbox}} {{infobox entity | name = Cadaver Bloom | sciname = Moderantum flos | danger = 100% | power_level = 3 | diversity_level = 0 | max_count = 8 | door_speed_multiplier = | see_through_fog = | pip_size = | attack_damage = 30 | attack_speed = 650ms | dps = | contact_damage = | hits-to-kill = {{#invoke:Entities/AutoHitsToKill|for|CadaverBloom}} | stun_multiplier = 0.25 | id = CadaverBloom | cause_of_death = Mauling }} The '''Cadaver Bloom''' is a hostile [[Entities|entity]]. It does not appear naturally, and instead grows out of an [[employee]] infected by the spores of [[Cadaver Growth]]s. Infected employees that remain untreated will eventually die and become controlled by the plant themselves, regardless of whether they are inside or outside of the facility. Once active, it runs at very high speeds towards employees within its line of sight to maul and decapitate them. Due to their connection to Cadaver Growths, the [[Moons|moon]] they are most commonly found on is [[Adamance]]. True to its name, the Cadaver Bloom uses the corpse of an employee as its host. Two splitting vines form a stem connected to a large, blooming flower that grows out of the helmet's visor. The vine's color fades from green to yellow, while the flower's seven petals sport a bright orange coloration and spherical black anthers. Several white and yellow flowers can also be seen growing out of the corpse's helmet and upper chest. A mouth with a pair of needle-like teeth resembling that of a [[w:Venus flytrap|Venus flytrap]] grows out of the corpse's stomach. Four large vines adorned with small clusters of leaves extend from the corpse's abdomen, allowing the plant to move. == {{section heading/Bestiary entry}} == {{quote bestiary|Cadavers}} == {{section heading/Spawn chances}} == N/A; Blooms do not participate in the normal entity spawning, they can only appear through the [[Cadaver Growth]] corruption mechanic.
Here are the spawn chances for Growths: {{WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances|CadaverGrowths}} == {{section heading/Strategy}} == If you find yourself in a position where you need to fight a Cadaver Bloom, it is best done in an open area with plenty of space to move around. If an infected employee is about to turn, try and guide them to such an area if you wish dispatch of the Cadaver Bloom quickly. Upon being hit with a weapon they will momentarily become stunned. Employees should keep their distance, strike when the Cadaver Bloom approaches, and back up to a safe distance again while it is stunned. Because the Cadaver Bloom only takes 1 damage from [[Shotgun]]s, employees may want to use a [[Shovel]] or traffic sign as the weapon of choice. == {{section heading/Sounds}} == {{audio gallery | A Cadaver Bloom flower blooming. |File:BeautifulBloomB.ogg |br| A Cadaver Bloom flower breaking out of an employee's helmet.|File:BloomedPlayerHeadBurst.ogg |br| A Cadaver Bloom's foliage bursting out of an employee's chest.|File:ChestBurst.ogg |br| The flytrap mouth of a Cadaver Bloom snapping open and shut.|File:ChestChomperSnapping.ogg |br| A Cadaver Bloom's footsteps.|File:CadaverBloomPlantFeetHit.ogg |br| A Cadaver Bloom's breathing. |File:CadaverBloomBreathing.ogg |br| A Cadaver Bloom's "roaring". |File:BloomRoar1.ogg|File:BloomRoar2.ogg|File:BloomRoar3.ogg |br| A Cadaver Bloom being hit.|File:HitBloomEnemy.ogg |br| A Cadaver Bloom dying.|File:KillBloomEnemy.ogg }} == {{section heading/History}} == === [[Version 80]] === Introduced. == {{section heading/Trivia}} == Due to the nature of how the Cadaver Bloom spawns, it is the 5th entity that can be encountered both indoors and outdoors. Other indoor and outdoor entities are the [[Ghost Girl]], [[Masked]], [[Tulip Snake]], and [[Maneater]]. == {{section heading/Gallery}} == File:Cadaver Bloom Transformation.gif| An employee transforming into a Cadaver Bloom after being infected. File:Cadaver Bloom Top.png| A Cadaver Bloom from above. File:Cadaver Bloom Nodes.png| A Cadaver Bloom being scanned, showing both scan nodes. == {{section heading/Notes}} == Cadaver Growths utilize the [[Mechanics#Special_Enemies|special enemy system]] to guarantee spawns, meaning one will always spawn at the very beginning of the day on Adamance. '''From infobox''' The Cadaver Bloom has the special behavior of always taking only 1 damage from [[Shotgun]]s. {{navbox entities}}

Extension ladder.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{autoinfobox item |two-handed=No |physics=Solid when deployed |audible=When deploying |battery=No |weapon=Only against employees |primary-use=Pop up ladder |secondary-use= |tertiary-use= |inspectable=Yes (undocumented) }} == Company description == {{terminal| "The extension ladder can reach as high as nine meters! Use it to scale any cliff and reach for the stars! To save batteries the extension ladder automatically stores itself after 18 seconds." }} == Overview == The '''Extension Ladder''' can be used to reach otherwise difficult-to-reach or inaccessible locations. It weighs nothing, making it easy for employees to carry. The ladder remains extended for {{nowrap|18 seconds}}, as listed in its INFO. Note however that it does ''not'' have a battery in-game. If the ladder falls on an employee, it kills them. == Strategy == An obvious strategy for this item is to quickly get up to areas or escape danger by climbing up something. Some noteworthy uses include reaching the fire escape on [[Offense]] without needing to jump onto the pipe at the start. It also is useful for maneuvering over the small bridge on [[Adamance]] when it has broken. And even can be used to move up and down the nearby cliffs to the fire escape allowing for safe movement of scrap. An interesting property of the Extension Ladder is that it blocks both the line-of-sight and the stream of bullets coming from [[Traps|Turrets]]. You could use this to temporarily clear a passage way if it is under overwatch of a Turret. == Trivia == It is possible to stop employees from being ejected using the ladder, but this serves next to no practical purpose since the game will still be reset after a short time. == History == === [[Version 40]] === Fixed an issue that would occur if the ladder were placed with 0 rotation Fixed an issue that could cause the player to die on the ladder before its animation played. === [[Version 39]] === Introduced {{navbox items}}

== {{section heading/Strategy}} == <!--T:5-->

Jetpack.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{autoinfobox item |physics= |audible=While firing |battery=Yes |primary-use=Use jetpack (hold) |secondary-use= |tertiary-use= |inspectable= }} == {{section heading/Company description}} == {{terminal| "This device will get you around anywhere! Just use it responsibly!" }} == Overview == The '''jetpack''' is a versatile yet clunky mobility tool used to travel around the surface of the moons. It is both the heaviest and most expensive piece of equipment in the game, but it makes up for this with its incredible aerial mobility. Its near-exclusively vertical thrust and incredible weight make it practically worthless within the interior areas of [[moons]]. While it appears that you hold it with both hands, it does not make your [[hands full]], so in that sense it is not a two-handed item. == {{section heading/Mechanics}} == Holding {{keybind|Item primary use}} will propel the user upwards. Their trajectory is then determined by tilting the device forwards and backwards with {{keybind|Walk forward|short=y}} and {{keybind|Walk back|short=y}}, and tilting from side to side with {{keybind|Strafe left|short=y}} and {{keybind|Strafe right|short=y}}. Their pitch is controlled relative to the horizontal direction the user is facing. This is very important to keep in mind as utilizing both pitch controls and view controls is the key to mastering the cumbersome control scheme. The longer the trigger is held, the faster they will be propelled. Releasing the trigger will rapidly decrease the user's propulsion. The jetpack has a limited battery usage that allows for just over a minute of continuous flight time. When it drops to around 15%, it emits a rapid, low-pitched beeping when used, signaling that the power is low. Once it drops to 0%, it stops functioning until it is recharged. Crashing into terrain or objects can result in varying levels of damage, dependent on speed and rotation. At 6 units per second, crashing when angled more than 60 degrees from standing upright will deal 30 damage at a 0.6s cooldown. At 9 units per second, 30 damage will be dealt regardless of rotation at a 0.25s cooldown. At 17 units per second, 85 damage will be dealt regardless of rotation at a 0.6s cooldown. Damage dealt from crashing, [[Damage#Employees|like other sources]], will only kill critically injured employees (except for the 85 damage call). Crashing will not deal damage if the cooldown is still active, but the instant kill condition can still be triggered during cooldown.{{ref code|PlayerControllerB.Update}} When traveling above 50 units per second, the jetpack will kill the user on collision with anything. The way this functions currently allows for the employee themselves to be included in this "collision", meaning that traveling at high speeds can result in employees colliding with themselves and dying to "Gravity". This is not guaranteed, but is much easier to encounter when wearing a [[belt bag]].{{ref code|JetpackItem.Update}} This "mechanic" is commonly misinterpreted as an overheat system - however, the jetpack has not and never has had any overheat system, and this is simply the result of a bug that has possibly intentionally been left in. The "jetpack controls" state is activated when it is turned on, and deactivated after touching the ground and being alive for one frame. If the user dies from any source while jetpack controls are active (including fall damage), this causes any jetpacks present in their inventory to explode. This can also be triggered by [[Tulip Snakes]], regardless of if the jetpack held was used or not. == {{section heading/Trivia}} == The jetpack was one of several features that were locked during the public demo of Lethal Company. == {{section heading/References}} == Patreon post ''[https://www.patreon.com/posts/time-is-flying-102810602 The time is flying (and so are the jetpacks and tulip snakes)]'' by Zeekerss, 2024-04-22:
''"(Maybe due to its high price and absurdity, many players treat the jetpack as the current "win condition" of Lethal Company? I wouldn't really want to drag down the victory of soaring straight up into the sky and exploding like a firework.)"'' {{navbox items}}

Mug.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom|Coffee_Mug}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{autoinfobox item

|audible=<!--no-->
|primary-use=<!--none-->

|inspectable=Yes (undocumented)
}}
The '''Mug''' (or, "Coffee mug") is a low-to-mid-value Scrap that can spawn in one of 5 different variants. 

The Coffee Mug is one of the few non-rare items (implying [[Gold bar]]s, [[Cash register]]s and [[Zed Dog]]s are excluded here) that have a large price fork, similarly to the [[Rubber Ducky]] or [[Laser Pointer]].

== Item Variants ==
[[File:Mug Variants.png|thumb|Each variant of Mug placed next to each other in the [[Facility]].]]
There are 5 possible appearances of the Mug:

* Lavender with a small white creature drawn on the front.
* Plain red
* Red with a graphic on the front reading: "#1 MUG".
* A light green mug with a graphic on the front depicting a cow & rising sun behind it
* A dark gray mug with large yellow text with an orange outline reading: "MORNING! MORNING! MORNING!".

The default appearance is the lavender mug, which will be chosen upon reloading a save file containing the item due to the game not remembering item variants.

== Trivia ==

The "small white creature" found on the lavender variant of Mug comes from the profile picture of Puffo, who is one of [[Zeekerss]]' friends & original testers of Lethal Company.

The light green variant of Mug uses a cow graphic taken from the painting [[W:Wivenhoe Park (painting)|Wivenhoe Park]], by John Constable. This is the same painting that's used in the "fake window" room of [[Mansion|Mansions]], as well as in both variants of the [[Painting]] item.

The name of this item is different in its scan node, being called "Coffee mug" instead of "Mug" which is how it's referred to everywhere else in the game.

== Spawn chances ==

{{#invoke:Scraps/AutoTables|spawnChancesOnAllMoonsTables|Mug}}

The Laser pointer is a mid-to-high-value [[Scrap]] that can be powered on like a [[Flashlight]]. When turned on, it emits a small orange light beam with an estimated range of 20m and 5 minutes of battery life.
Impractical use for navigation, but it can be used as a replacement for Flashlights if you're out of options.
The laser dot will be obscured by fog, steam, blizzards, and toxic fumes.

Monsters that can normally see do not react to a laser pointer's beam, even if held, it does not give a Player's location away.
Similarly, the Laser Pointer cannot blind any of them, even if attempted to direct the beam directly into their eyes.

Hive.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom|Bee_Hive}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{autoinfobox item
|physics=<!--normal-->
|audible=<!--no-->
|primary-use=<!--none-->
|secondary-use=<!--none-->
|tertiary-use=<!--none-->
|inspectable=<!--no-->
}}
<translate>
<!--T:2-->
The '''Bee Hive''' is home to [[Circuit Bees]].

== Strategy == <!--T:3-->

<!--T:5-->
The best time to scout for beehives is early in the morning, to avoid encounters with dangerous outdoor entities. Employees should also be as lightweight as possible (ideally 0lbs). 

<!--T:12-->
Approach the hive at a large distance from your planned escape route. Attract the Circuit Bees and start circling away from your escape route - the Circuit Bees will follow. Once there is a clear enough path, dive back to the hive. Grab it and run toward the Ship. The Circuit Bees will be angered and will chase you at full speed. After exhausting about 3/4 of your stamina, drop the hive and keep running. The Circuit Bees will calm down and return to their hive. Rinse and repeat these steps until you are near enough to the ship. Drop the hive on the side or back railing of the ship and, depending on the situation, either run away from or into the ship.

<!--T:13-->
Once ready, activate the [[Ship#Main_monitor|Control Lever]] to leave the Moon. The Circuit Bees will be left behind as the Ship leaves the atmosphere. Keep note of where the hive is placed, as the bees will travel backwards from their placement as the ship leaves the moon, which can still injure and kill employees. Make sure to quickly grab your amassed hives, as you have about 10 seconds before the Ship door permanently closes and all the hives outside are lost.

<!--T:7-->
[[TZP-Inhalant]] is a good item to have on hand for hive carrying. The increased run speed will help the process, but the limited visibility can cause trouble if the employee is not careful enough.

<!--T:14-->
It is not recommended to take hives indoors or hide from a swarm using the ship door. This will cause the bees to roam and become incredibly dangerous to employees outdoors. Other employees should also give the hive collector a large amount of space to avoid drawing unintended aggression.

== Spawn chances == <!--T:8-->

<!--T:9-->
(These are the odds for [[Circuit Bees]] to spawn on a moon's surface—see [[Mechanics#Spawning algorithm (Daytime Entities)]] for details.)
</translate>
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances|RedLocustBees}}
<translate>
== Notes == <!--T:10-->

<!--T:11-->
* All Bee Hives found on the same day will have the same value.
</translate>

<translate>
<!--T:2-->
A small golden key. Used to unlock locked doors within interiors or the door to the Cabin on [[Adamance]] and is consumed on use.

<!--T:4-->
The key is made of metal and can trigger lightning strike on a [[Weather#Stormy|stormy]] moon.

== Strategy == <!--T:5-->

<!--T:6-->
Employees can take keys back to the ship and bring them during the next day in order to unlock a door in the interior without having to search for another key. If no locked doors come up, the employee can hang onto the key or discard it in favor of a piece of [[scrap]].

<!--T:7-->
When going to a [[Weather#Stormy|stormy]] moon, keys that were generated inside the interior can be used effectively as a lightning rod when brought outside, only for that day. This is because keys generate before [[scrap]], and take lightning priority over any items already present on the ship while landing.

== History == <!--T:8-->

=== [[Version 81]] === <!--T:9-->

<!--T:10-->
Now counts towards the ledger total when sold, at its claimed value of {{credits|3}}.
</translate>

Gift.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom|Gift_Box}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{autoinfobox item
|physics=<!--normal-->
|audible=<translate><!--T:1--> When opened</translate>
|primary-use=<translate><!--T:2--> <q>Open gift</q></translate>
|secondary-use=<!--none-->
|tertiary-use=<!--none-->
|inspectable=<!--no-->
}}

{{Stub}}
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
The Gift is a [[Scrap]] item that can be opened to reveal a different, random scrap item inside. The dropped scrap item can be anything that's normally able to spawn on whichever moon the Gift was originally found. The item may be two-handed, despite the Gift being a one-handed scrap item.

<!--T:4-->
The dropped item is usually worth more than standard scrap, with a min value increased by {{credits|10}} and a max value increased by {{credits|14}}. The item that spawns from the present is chosen from the scrap pool of the moon it was found on.

<!--T:5-->
The Gift will be worth {{credits|50}} on [[single scrap day]]. Note that the Gift may be worth more than what is inside.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Strategy}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:6-->
Due to Gift boxes having a chance to give you a two-handed item, it can be a good idea to not open the Gift until it's been taken to the ship. Conversely, it could also be a good idea to open the Gift as soon as you find it, due to the possibility of receiving a small item that weighs less than {{pounds|19}}.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Trivia}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:7-->
The Gift box has the smallest inventory icon out of any others in the game, at 177x177 pixels in size. Most other icons for equipment are 256x256 pixels in size.
</translate>

Flask.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{#invoke:Scraps/AutoInfobox|infobox
| scrap = Flask
}}
The Flask is a somewhat low-value [[scrap]] item that is deceptively heavy at {{pounds|19}}, which is equivalent to the weight of [[Radar-booster]]s. There are measurement lines printed on both the front and back of the glass, with legible numbers reading "''0, 10, 19, 20, 40, 60, 80, 100''".

== Variants ==
There are 2 possible appearances of the Flask: 

* A flat-bottomed/Florence flask (commonly referred to by players as the "Round flask")
* A conical/Erlenmeyer flask (commonly referred to by players as the "Triangle flask")

The default appearance is the round flask, which will be chosen upon reloading a save file containing the item due to the game not saving item variants.

== Trivia ==

Despite the round flask being the default variant, it appears the texture for measurement lines was made for the triangle flask model, due to the distortion seen on the round variant.

{{topicon Fandom|DIY-Flashbang}}
{{interlangbox}}

<translate>
<!--T:1-->
The '''Homemade Flashbang''' is similar to the [[Stun grenade]], but its "fuse" is extremely short. Pulling the pin will cause it to explode almost immediately, flashing everything in the vicinity, but also dealing minor [[damage]] (20HP) to the employee that was holding it.

<!--T:7-->
In terms of their stun effect, they're identical to Stun grenades, so this is how long they will stun each kind of [[entity]] for:
</translate>
{{#invoke:Entities/AutoStunTimeTable|homemade_flashbang}}

== {{section heading/Strategy}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:2-->
Due to having copied code from the Stun grenade, it's possible to throw the flashbang after the pin has been pulled by quickly pressing {{keybind|Item primary use|short=y}} again. This will prevent you from taking any damage from the blast.
</translate>

== {{section heading/Trivia}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
Pulling the pin of a Homemade flashbang and quickly placing it on the [[Gordion#Selling|Company Desk]] will still successfully sell the item, despite it seemingly not existing anymore.

<!--T:4-->
The purchase price is given as {{credits|40}} in the game files (where other scrap items are explicitly {{credits|0}}),
indicating there may have been plans to include it in the [[Store#Equipment|equipment store]] at some point.

<!--T:5-->
Upon picking up a Homemade flashbang, the Primary Action tooltip will refer to it as a "DIY-Flashbang". Scrolling to a different inventory slot and then back onto the flashbang will cause switch the text to say "Pull Pin".

<!--T:6-->
The capitalization of the Homemade flashbang's name is different in the item's scan node (instead being called "Homemade Flashbang").

Masks (Item).wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom|Dramatic_Mask}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{autoinfobox item
|Tragedy<!-- TODO split page -->
|name=Tragedy (mask)
|physics=<!--normal-->
|audible=Yes
|primary-use=<q>Wear mask</q>
|secondary-use=<!--none-->
|tertiary-use=<!--none-->
|inspectable=<!--no-->
}}
{{autoinfobox item
|Comedy<!-- TODO split page -->
|name=Comedy (mask)
|physics=<!--normal-->
|audible=Yes
|primary-use=<q>Wear mask</q>
|secondary-use=<!--none-->
|tertiary-use=<!--none-->
|inspectable=<!--no-->
}}
{{Template:Investigation}}
{{split|Tragedy}}
{{about|the scrap item|the enemy|Masked}}

== Description ==
The Tragedy and Comedy '''Masks''' are scrap items resembling plastic or ceramic theatre masks. For 10 seconds after a mask is initially picked up, it will not be able to possess a employee. After this, while held up to a employee's face, there is a 65% chance every 5 seconds that the mask will possess the employee. This 65% chance is also rolled every time the player starts wearing it. An employee being possessed will die and turn into a [[Masked]] after a few seconds.

Masks cannot possess an employee if the employee does not put it on themselves. Dying with a mask in one's inventory or holding it without putting it on does nothing.

== Spawn chances ==

Both masks have independent spawn chances, but they spawn on the same [[moons]], with [[Offense]] as the one exception.

<tabber>|-|
Tragedy={{#invoke:Scraps/AutoTables|scrapSpawnChancesTables|TragedyMask}}
|-|
Comedy={{#invoke:Scraps/AutoTables|scrapSpawnChancesTables|ComedyMask}}</tabber>

== Sounds ==
Masks have a chance of playing a crying or laughing sound, depending whether or not it is a Tragedy or Comedy. [[Eyeless Dogs]] are able to hear these cries/laughs.
{{audio gallery
|<translate>Tragedy mask crying</translate>|File:MaskCry1.ogg|File:MaskCry2.ogg|File:MaskCry3.ogg|File:MaskCry4.ogg
|br|<translate>Comedy mask laughing</translate>|File:MaskLaugh1.ogg|File:MaskLaugh2.ogg|File:MaskLaugh3.ogg
}}

== Gallery ==

<gallery>
File:Screenshot 2023-12-14 1.42.54 PM.png|A comedy mask possessing a player after being put on.
</gallery>

== Trivia ==

* The Comedy Mask and the Tragedy Mask are, despite popular belief, not inspired by SCP-035, as stated in a Patreon post. <!-- which one??? cite it-->
* The Masks have a chance to make a sound. Either a laughing or a crying one, depending on the mask type.
* Putting on either of the masks while on [[Gordion]] will result in the employee dying without being converted into a Masked. The Mask and the player's other items will still be lost.
* Employees killed while the mask is infecting them will have a death pose similar to that of an employee, but with a mask on their face with red eyes, instead of black eyes.

Body.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{autoinfobox item |name= Body of <playername> |physics= Ragdolls jankily |audible= |primary-use= |secondary-use= |tertiary-use= |inspectable= }} A '''Body''' is the corpse of a deceased [[employee]], effectively a ragdoll which has inherited their appearance. The majority of methods to die result in the deceased employee becoming a Body. However, there are some exceptions in which a body cannot be recovered by any means, including: * Being consumed by a [[Forest Keeper]]. * Being consumed by an [[Earth Leviathan]]. * Being consumed by [[The Company#The Company Monster|The Company Monster]]. * Being ripped apart by a [[Giant Sapsucker]]. * Being transformed into a [[Masked]]. * Sinking in Quicksand on [[Adamance]] or a rainy moon. * Falling off the map. Despite Bodies generally matching the appearance of the deceased employee, certain deaths modify the Body's appearance, including: * Dying to a [[Coil-Head]] replaces the Body's head with a spring and removes its forearms. * Being scorched by an [[Old Bird]] gives the body a black, charred appearance. * Being consumed by a [[Hygrodere]] causes the body to appear as an empty suit. * Dying to the [[Ghost Girl]], a [[Kidnapper Fox]], or a [[Cadaver Bloom]] results in a headless body. * Being cut in half by a [[Barber]] separates the body into a top and bottom half. Only the top half can be picked up. * A body that has been picked up by a [[Bunker Spider]] and suspended from a ceiling will be wrapped in silk. This can happen even if the body was killed by something other than the Bunker Spider. * A body that has been used as a host for a now-dead [[Cadaver Bloom]] will have flowers growing out of the helmet and upper chest. Bodies can be carried back to the [[ship]] like [[scrap]] to "recover" them. They can also be targeted on the ship's monitor and [[Teleporter|teleported]] back. Teleportation is the only way to retrieve a body that has been taken by a Bunker Spider or fallen into an inaccessible area such as the pits in the [[Interiors|Factory]] and [[Mineshaft]]. Upon returning to orbit, a fine is deducted from the current [[Credits|credit balance]] based on how many employees died; "recovering" a Body reduces the fine as described below. Any Bodies are deleted when dead employees are respawned in orbit. A Body can be sold to [[the Company]] for {{credits|5}}. Since Bodies are not retained when leaving a moon, the employee will have to be killed after landing on [[Gordion]]. Note that this is mutually exclusive with recovering the Body, so it's advisable to only use this strategy in dire situations, or if equipment is purchased on Gordion to reduce the credit loss prior to leaving. As mentioned above, dying to [[the Company Monster]] destroys the Body and doesn't count as selling it. Bodies count towards the total in the [[weekly challenge]]. == Penalty == For each dead employee, you can obtain their body before leaving the moon, so called 'bodies recovered' in game. If the body was not obtained, you lose 20% of your current credits for each body. Otherwise, you lose 8% of current credits.ApplyPenalty - https://pastebin.com/NkErBW2m Formula: totalPenalty = Math.Floor(currentCredit * 0.2) * unobtainedBodies + Math.Floor(currentCredit * 0.08) * obtainedBodies == {{section heading/References}} == {{navbox items}}

Mapper.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{autoinfobox item |name=Mapper (tools) |purchase-price=30 |two-handed= |physics= |battery=Yes {{nowrap|(20 uses)}} |primary-use=Update map |secondary-use= |tertiary-use= |inspectable=Yes }} {{unused}} The '''Mapper''' (called '''Mapper tools''' in the store) is a utility item, which may eventually be purchasable but is currently inaccessible in-game. (A purchase flow exists, but it has the price of the [[Shovel]] and will add one of those to the [[Dropship]] instead.) {{terminal| The most advanced map device, using light-detection and ranging to give you an overhead view of your surroundings. }} Mappers are functional if spawned in with mods.
They will display a copy of the main ship monitor—however the image only updates continuously while someone is in the ship watching it (presumably this is just the ship monitor skipping drawing for performance reasons, and not a mechanic of the Mapper).
You can manually update the screen with {{keybind|Item primary use}}, consuming some battery. The battery holds enough charge for 20 such updates.
You're also able to bring the Mapper closer to your eyes (camera) with {{keybind|Item inspect}}. The Mapper uses the same HUD icon as the [[Pro-flashlight]]. Like other equipment, dropped Mappers won't be highlighted by the [[scanner]]. == History == According to unconfirmed information, it was actually a replacement item before the launch of the ship main monitor. {{navbox items}}

Easter egg.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom|Easter_Egg}}
{{interlangbox}}
{{autoinfobox item
|physics=<translate><!--T:1--> Slides after thrown</translate>
|audible=<translate><!--T:2--> When dropped or exploding</translate>
|primary-use=<translate><!--T:3--> <tvar name="en"><q>Toss egg</q></tvar></translate>
|secondary-use=<!--none-->
|tertiary-use=<!--none-->
|inspectable=<!--no-->
}}
<translate>
<!--T:4-->
The Easter egg is a small, colorful egg with 5 [[#Variants|variants]]. It can be thrown with {{keybind|Item primary use}}, and has a small chance to explode upon hitting the ground. The chances of an Easter egg exploding are the same regardless of whether it's been dropped, thrown, or placed inside of another object such as the [[Cupboard]]. Upon exploding, it will shoot confetti and deal up to 5 damage to entities (or 100 damage to players) if they're close enough. Easter eggs will also give extremely high knockback to employees when exploded.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Mechanics}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:6-->
The Easter egg works off of a coordinate system where some spots will always lead to explosions and other spots will always lead to no explosion. These spots are randomized each time When an egg is thrown, it may snap to a location on the ground, as it is locking to the closest coordinate to where the egg should have landed. For example, if one egg that is placed at the company counter explodes, it should be expected that other eggs will also explode when placed in the same spot (a different spot in the counter can contain a coordinate leading to no explosion).
</translate>
== {{section heading/Strategy}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:8-->
A [[landmine]] can be detonated safely by throwing an Easter egg across it, which still risks the egg exploding.

<!--T:9-->
The explosion from an Easter egg can instantly set a [[Forest Giant]] on fire, causing it to die shortly afterwards.

== Variants == <!--T:11-->
</translate>
[[File:Easter eggs on shelf.png|thumb|<translate><!--T:12--> Each variant of Easter egg resting on a [[Facility]] shelf</translate>]]
<translate>
<!--T:13-->
There are 5 possible appearances of the Easter egg:
* Striped red/blue/yellow
* Solid blue
* Green with a yellow pattern
* Rainbow
* Brown-ish with purple spots

<!--T:14-->
The default appearance is the red/blue/yellow striped egg, which will be chosen upon reloading a save file containing the item due to the game not remembering item variants.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Trivia}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:16-->
The audio for dropping an Easter egg can be heard throughout the entire map in-game, although it is quiet.

<!--T:10-->
<u>Before [[v62]]:</u> It's possible to entirely avoid the possibility of Easter eggs exploding. This is done by setting your game's framerate to 30FPS, then performing the following steps (as the ''host of the lobby'') - Hold the Easter egg, crouch on flat terrain without moving, look all the way down, and drop the item. If done right the egg will be placed gently without making a drop sound. It is very important that this is done by the host, because although a client can perform this exploit without it exploding on their end, the egg still may explode for the host anyways which will cause it to be unretrievable.
</translate>

<translate>
<!--T:2-->
The '''Kitchen Knife''' is a type of [[scrap]] that can also function as weapon for eliminating [[entities]] or destroying [[Bunker Spider|spider]] webs. It has a fixed sell value of {{credits|35}}.

<!--T:3-->
The kitchen knife deals 1 HP of damage to entities (20 HP of damage to full health employees, 10 HP otherwise). Its cooldown depends on whether the user is the host or not. When used by the host, the knife inflicts damage on a 0.375s cooldown that is slightly shorter than a full [[shovel]] swing time. For non-host clients, the knife deals damage every time it is swung.

<!--T:4-->
The way the kitchen knife is utilized in gameplay is incredibly similar to the shovel, aside from the fact that you cannot hold and release the swing. 

== Spawn chances == <!--T:5-->

<!--T:6-->
The Kitchen Knife does not naturally spawn on any moon. It can only be acquired by killing a [[Butler]].
These are the spawn rates for Butlers:
{{Template:WikiAutomated/EntitySpawnChances|Butler}}

== Trivia == <!--T:7-->

<!--T:8-->
* The swing animation will always play when a player clicks, but damage will only be applied according to the weapon cooldown.
* A blood spew particle effect will occur when hitting an entity, even on unkillable or mechanical entities.

Clipboard.wikitext:
{{#ifeq:{{PAGELANGUAGE}}|en|{{DISPLAYTITLE:clipboard{{#translation:}}}}|}} {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{autoinfobox item |clipboard |name=clipboard (Training manual) |primary-use= |secondary-use=Turn page (prev.) |tertiary-use=Turn page (next) |inspectable=Yes }} The '''clipboard''' (a.k.a. '''Training manual''' a.k.a. '''Service manual''') is a pedagogical item which generates in the [[ship]] and in the [[Company Cruiser]]. Both contain multiple pages of gameplay information, interspersed with worldbuilding. They have little use beyond that.
The "Training manual" clipboard will always appear to the left of the [[brake lever]] upon creating or loading a save, even if moved, left in a [[Belt bag]], or lost. This is the same spot as the button to activate the [[Teleporter]], making it advisable to move the clipboard before attempting to teleport someone under pressure. It contains some pointers for new players (see [[#Trivia]] section below for caveats).
A "Service manual" clipboard will always be on the low, central shelf of the Cruiser upon delivery. It will disappear if the save is reloaded. It explains the basics of driving and hints at the alternate use for [[Weed killer]]. (Again see the [[#Trivia]] section for caveats.) While holding a clipboard, {{keybind|Item inspect}} toggles bringing it close to your eyes (camera) so the text is legible, and {{keybind|Item secondary use}} and {{keybind|Item tertiary use}} flip between the pages. The current page is "remembered"; the pages don't fall back into place on their own when not held. The "Training manual" clipboard contains a cover page, with no useful information, and 3 pages of content (numbered 146, 139, and 140): * The first describes the [[Echo Scanner]]. * The second gives an overview of the [[Terminal]] and [[moon]] difficulty. * The last explains how to fulfill your job and meet [[quota]], and ends with some miscellaneous tips. The "Service manual" clipboard contains the same cover page, with no useful information, and 3 pages of content (numbered 146, 391, and 391 again): * The first warns against trying to accelerate while in park but is mostly satirical safety guidelines. * The second explains most of the dashboard controls and the gearstick. * The last explains how to monitor and refill the Cruiser's health and how to use the magnet, and reminds you about the warranty without explaining it. Both clipboards are worth zero credits when sold to [[the Company]]. While the "Training manual" adds {{credits|5}} to the ledger, this doesn't count towards the total.As demonstrated in [[:File:Item names in ledger v62 part1.jpg]]. == Trivia == The "Training manual" contains several details about game mechanics which don't align with how the game actually functions, making it even less useful for new players (though poor documentation is arguably lore-accurate). As of v81: * [...] ping the scanner while looking towards objects of interest—This doesn't always work, sometimes missing things directly in front of you and other times catching things in your periphery, but those are likely just bugs. * Green: A place or object of interest; Yellow: An object which can be returned to the Company as valuable scrap; Red: Biological matter, such as wildlife.—Scrap is marked in ''green'', [[entities]] ''and [[traps]]'' in red, and misc. points of interest in ''blue''. (Also, this is a black-and-white photocopy.) * '''TIP:''' In general, safer and closer moons are cheap or even free, and the risk analysis team recommends sticking to these safer areas for the entire duration of your contract.—Opinions differ over how exactly the moons compare on difficulty, but it's hard to dispute that going to the later moons such as [[Titan]] and [[Artifice]] is necessary for "high-quota" play.And assuming the contract duration is about 3 months, as can be inferred from the [[Sigurd Logs]] (though the player is hired [[Lore/Timeline|centuries later]]), the game's current rules preclude any employees from completing it. * You can purchase items in bulk (under 30 lb.)—The [[Dropship]] has no weight limit; rather, it's limited to 12 items per trip. * The Survival Kit is a recommended package of essentials for beginners.—The {{credits|138}} pricetag of the [[survival kit]] means it can't be purchased with the starting allowance of {{credits|60}}. * The conversion rate of scrap to Credits will fluctuate over the course of several days [...]—Technically correct, but the rate is consistently low on the first day and increases linearly to 100% on the final day; there is no random aspect to [[the Company]]'s rates at present. ** The logs "[[Sigurd_Logs#Swing_of_things_-_Aug_27|Swing of things]]" and "[[Sigurd_Logs#Sound_behind_the_wall_-_Sep_4|Sound behind the wall]]" respectively mention rates of 70% and 120%, with the latter specifically saying that it was unusually high and that [Desmond has] been watching it like the stupid stocks. The "Service manual" contains a couple details about the [[Company Cruiser]] which don't align with how it actually functions. As of v81: * Did you know: Studies show that proper sitting posture while driving the Company Cruiser is correlated with higher efficiency [...]—This is questionably true in-universe, but since you as the player have no control over your employee avatar's posture, it's impossible to know how much it affects efficiency in terms of gameplay. In any case, it seems that employees all diligently follow the manual's guidance. * Did you know: the Company Cruiser may even be able to hear you speak! Remember to give it kind words of affection at the beginning of each day.—The Cruiser doesn't seem to have any sound-based mechanic besides the engine being audible. And if it did, it probably wouldn't distinguish words of encouragement from any other noise. * This page has all the info you'll ever need to operate your Cruiser.—It does not. * [...] in order to heal completely, the truck must be taken into orbit with you.—As the red scrawlings on this page allude to, you can also restore engine "health" with [[Weed killer]]. * If your Cruiser is damaged or lost, we will replace your next Cruiser!—See [[Company Cruiser#Warranty]]. Flipping between pages is [[Audible_sounds|audible to entities]]. It is one of the few equipment items that can't be put in the [[utility slot]]. The [[scanner]] nodes for both clipboards read Training manual and have a subtitle of Useful info! Both clipboards share the same cover page, but the copy in the "Training manual" has the table blurred out. Each of the pages has its texture, reversed, on the back, giving the impression of razor-thin paper where the ink has bled through. In-universe, the pages must have been photocopied from multiple source manuals, as the pages numbered 146 and 139 in the "Training manual" have two different sections each numbered 19, and the "Service manual" has two pages numbered 391, again each with a section 19. This item's name is uniquely spelled in all-lowercase. == Gallery == Training1.png|Training manual cover page Training2.png|Training manual page 1 (No. 146) Training3.png|Training manual page 2 (No. 139) Training4.png|Training manual page 3 (No. 140) CruiserManual1.png|Cruiser manual cover page CruiserManual2.png|Cruiser manual page 1 (No. 146) CruiserManual3.png|Cruiser manual page 2 (No. 139) CruiserManual4.png|Cruiser manual page 3 (No. 140) == Notes and references == {{notes and reflist}} {{navbox items}}

Belt bag.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{autoinfobox item |name=(Tactical) Belt bag |physics=Solid to other items |audible=Only to players |primary-use=Check bag |secondary-use=Store tool |tertiary-use= |inspectable= }} The '''Belt bag''' is a blue fanny pack that can be purchased from the [[Store|equipment store]] for {{credits|45}}, used for carrying multiple equipment items with only a single hotbar slot taken up. {{terminal| The Company's very own tactical belt bags are an absolute must-have for those who want to bring their all! They can hold 15 utility items at one time. You'll never feel useless again! Just press [Q] to store an item in the bag, then click to check its contents and drag items out. Other players can also use the bag while it's fastened around your waist. }} When on the ground, pressing {{keybind|Interact}} while looking at the belt part lets you pick it up as usual, whereas looking at the bag part and holding {{keybind|Interact|short=y}} opens the inventory HUD without picking it up. You can't open the HUD with [[full hands]].
When in your hand, looking at a tool and pressing {{keybind|Item Secondary use}} will store it in the bag, while {{keybind|Item primary use}} opens the inventory HUD. Again this won't work with full hands, such as if you're climbing a ladder. Note that you can still pick up any item like normal with {{keybind|Interact|short=y}}, and that if you open the HUD, you can still use {{keybind|Item Secondary use|short=y}} to store items. You can't stow an item that another employee is holding, just like you can't pick it up out of their hand.
When on your person (hotbar) but not selected, it appears on your waist, and you can look down to use it just as if it was on the ground. Your fellow employees can also use it while you're holding or wearing it. If an employee accesses the [[Terminal]] with a Belt bag ''in their hand'', it becomes inaccessible to others. Only one employee can have the HUD open at once. Any [[Store#Equipment|purchased equipment]] can be stored in the bag, as well as [[Key]]s, [[Ammo]] (but not [[Shotgun]]s or [[Kitchen knives]]), and the [[Sticky note]] and [[clipboard]]s.
A bag holds up to 15 items, and weighs a constant {{pounds|15.5}} regardless of what's stored in it, making it more viable to take equipment across unsound bridges.
Specific item behaviors: * Items which drain power while active, such as the [[Flashlight]], will continue to do so if not switched off before being stowed. Holding the Belt bag will not show a battery indicator, nor can you charge items while they're in the bag. * [[Walkie-talkie]]s remain active when stowed, but incoming transmissions are not audible, and you must withdraw it to broadcast. * Active [[Lockpicker]]s continue running from within the bag. You can withdraw them and re-stow them and they will remain "paired" to the door they're operating on, unless and until you pick them up directly. * Thrown [[Stun grenade]]s ''cannot'' be "cooked" by stowing them; they will go off uselessly inside the bag. * [[Boombox]]es remain active when stowed but are not audible. * The timers of [[Extension ladder]]s continue counting down while in the bag, but it doesn't matter since an extended ladder will retract upon being withdrawn. * Active [[Radar-booster]]s continue to function when stored, but they appear to be in a void, presumably out-of-bounds. * Belt bags can hold other Belt bags. This creates a way to exceed the 15-item limit, if you're okay with the greater risk. The nested bags must be withdrawn to be used. Neither the pickup jangle sound nor the opening zip sound are [[Audible_sounds|audible to entities]]. However, as with other items, dropping a bag ''is'' audible. While it appears that you hold it with both hands, that does not make your [[hands full]], so in that sense it is not a two-handed item. However, having the HUD open has the same restrictions as HANDS FULL, despite not showing that message. Dropped Belt bags have a small collision box, meaning other items dropped on the same spot will land on top of it. It's also there when the bag is being worn. As of v64, items stop processing gravity once they've landed, so items can be made to hover using this mechanic. You can sell a Belt bag for zero credits. Contained items won't show up in the ledger, nor will they count towards the 12-item sell limit. When loading a save, any items which were stored in a Belt bag instead appear in a pile against the port (left) wall, by the speaker. This is likely either unintentional or a limitation of the save system.{{cite bug|(nitpick) Some furniture's and items' states aren't included in saves}} == Inventory HUD == Hover the cursorThe cursor's effective position is the center of the hand icon. over an item, hold LMB, move the cursor off the side of the HUD, and release to drop the item where the bag is.
Due to an oversight, when {{keybind|Item primary use|short=y}} is not bound to the default of LMB, the controls are more complex.
See * Press {{nowrap|LMB/RMB/MMB}} (hardcoded) to "grab" an item. * Press {{keybind|Item primary use|short=y}} to "release" an item. ** If the cursor is over the slots, the item will be left in the bag. ** Otherwise, the item is dropped. *** If the cursor is over the close button then it will only drop the item, not close the HUD. * While an item is "in the cursor", closing the HUD with {{keybind|Pause|short=y}} will cancel the withdrawal. for details.
You can't move items to or from the hotbar using this HUD. Press {{keybind|Pause}}, or hover over the close button and press {{nowrap|LMB}} (hardcoded), to close the HUD. While the HUD is open, you can still perform any action, including moving around. The only limitations are that if you get too far from the bag, the HUD will close, plus some edge cases involving unorthodox keybinds like discussed above. Only one employee can have the HUD open at once. The HUD is drawn under/behind the [[scanner]]'s overlays, meaning those can possibly block you from clicking on an item or the close button. == {{section heading/Trivia}} == Pausing during the opening animation results in having both the HUD and the pause menu open. In this state, pressing {{keybind|Pause}} closes the HUD rather than unpausing. The "void" that's shown on the monitor when targeting a stored Radar-booster—the Belt bag's "pocket dimension", if you will—is actually out-of-bounds far below the map, at the fixed point {{nowrap|(3000, -400, 3000)}}, and items are teleported to and from this spot when stored or withdrawn.
Items inside a sold bag are deleted. The label on the front reads COMPANY-ISSUED. Its zipper is framed by two button eyes and hides fabric in the shape of jagged teeth, making the bag resemble a face like that of an [[w:Otomatone|Otomatone]], or more aptly a generic "monster" as might be marketed to children in real life. Seeing as the bag can hold 15 [[Radar-booster]]s and get no larger or heavier (and they appear to be in a void on the monitor), it may be a [[w:Bag_of_holding|Bag of Holding]] or similar supernatural artefact. In the v64 public betas, the HUD was keyboard-enabled, but it didn't work correctly. Crucially, it lacked a way to withdraw the selected item. The addition of the Belt bag was effectively a rebalance to make equipment more viable, as teased by Zeekerss in the v60 patch notes: In the next update I'd like to make store-bought items easier and more efficient to bring with you, even while carrying scrap. Steam announcement [https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/1966720/view/6940696210369307295 ''Version 60 - The Stuffy Update''] == {{section heading/Notes and references}} == {{notes and reflist}} {{navbox items}}

Moons.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{stub en only}} '''Moons''' are celestial bodies employees can land on to explore and collect [[scrap]]. Some moons are more dangerous but offer more loot. Moons can also be affected by [[weather]] like storms or eclipses. == Moon list == === The Company Building === {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! About |- | style="background:rgba(200,200,250);" | [[:71-Gordion|71-Gordion]] || While it behaves like a moon, it only contains the Company Building. Listed as The Company building in the terminal. Variants of gordion don't work for ROUTE or INFO. |} === Standard Moons === {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Name !! Hazard !! Cost (c) !! Layout ! Size Multiplier !! Min Scrap !! Max Scrap !! Indoor Power !! Night Power ! Weather |- | style="background:rgba(250,225,200);" | [[:41-Experimentation|41-Experimentation]] || D || 0 || Factory | 1 || 8 || 11 || 4 || 8 | Rainy, Stormy, Foggy, Flooded, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(225,200,175);" | [[:220-Assurance|220-Assurance]] || C || 0 || Factory | 1 || 13 || 15 || 6 || 8 | Rainy, Stormy, Foggy, Flooded, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(200,250,200);" | [[:56-Vow|56-Vow]] || C || 0 || Factory | 1.15 || 12 || 14 || 7 || 6 | Stormy, Foggy, Flooded, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(200,175,150);" | [[:21-Offense|21-Offense]] || B || 0 || Mineshaft | 1.25 || 14 || 18 || 12 || 8 | Rainy, Stormy, Foggy, Flooded, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(175,225,175);" | [[:61-March|61-March]] || B || 0 || Factory | 1.75 || 13 || 16 || 14 || 12 | Stormy, Foggy, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(150,200,150);" | [[:20-Adamance|20-Adamance]] || B || 0 || Factory | 1.18 || 14 || 16 || 13 || 11 | Rainy, Stormy, Foggy, Flooded, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(250,200,250);" | [[:85-Rend|85-Rend]] || A || 550 || Mansion | 1.8 || 18 || 25 || 10 || 6 | Stormy, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(225,175,225);" | [[:7-Dine|7-Dine]] || S || 600 || Mansion | 1.8 || 200 || 249 || 10 || 9 | Rainy, Flooded, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(200,150,200);" | [[:8-Titan|8-Titan]] || S+ || 700 || Factory | 2.2 || 28 || 31 || 18 || 7 | Stormy, Foggy, Eclipsed |} === Hidden Moons === {{spoilers}} {| class="wikitable sortable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" ! Name !! Hazard !! Cost (c) !! Layout ! Size Multiplier !! Min Scrap !! Max Scrap !! Indoor Power !! Night Power ! Weather |- | style="background:rgba(250,200,200);" | ''[[:5-Embrion|5-Embrion]]'' || S || 150 || Factory | 1.1 || 14 || 16 || 8 || 70 | Foggy, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(250,250,150);" | ''[[:68-Artifice|68-Artifice]]'' || S++ || 1500 || Mineshaft | 1.8 || 26 || 30 || 13 || 13 | Rainy, Stormy, Flooded, Eclipsed |- | style="background:rgba(175,175,225);" | ''[[:44-Liquidation|44-Liquidation]]'' || S++ || ??? || Mansion | 1.6 || 28 || 44 || 13 || 13 | Rainy, Stormy, Flooded, Eclipsed |} {{expandable div| ''As of v81, Liquidation exists only as an unfinished definition in the game files, and since there's no moon surface map, there's no way to visit it in-game.'' |Spoiler Moon Note}} ''Note: Colors are unofficial, chosen for visualization only. Green represents the forest biome moons that orbit Planet "No Service", referred to canonically as the "Green Witches". Tan represents the desert biome moons that orbit Gas Giant Planet "Big Grin". Purple represents the three snowy non-hidden paid moons.'' === Other Moons === * The [[weekly challenge]] moon has a random name (e.g., Rapo-87) but uses one of the standard moon templates. == Moon properties == === Difficulty/Hazard Level/Cost === Moons are ordered roughly by difficulty in the ship's terminal. Easier moons have fewer hazards and lower scrap, while harder moons cost credits, feature more hazards, and offer more loot. Cost applies once per orbital change. Hazard level is a fixed in-game value for each moon, serving as a difficulty indicator, but it's just an estimate not based on anything. === Map size multiplier === Moon maps are procedurally generated. The multiplier determines indoor map size—larger multipliers produce more complex layouts. [[8-Titan]] has the largest (2.2×), while [[41-Experimentation]] and [[220-Assurance]] are the smallest (1×). === Min/Max scrap === Each moon has a defined scrap spawn range. Scrap value is randomized. More scrap generally means higher potential loot. ''Note: Internally, Max Scrap is one higher due to exclusive maximums used during generation.'' ''Note: Additional items like [[Apparatus]] and [[Bee Hive|Bee Hives]] may exceed Max Scrap count. For example, [[21-Offense]] may show 19 items due to 18 scrap + 1 Apparatus.'' === Max powers === Max indoor and nighttime power influence potential entity spawns. See [[Mechanics#Spawning requirements]] for details. === Default layout === Each moon has a default layout—[[Factory]], [[Mineshaft]], or [[Mansion]]—used most often. Other layouts may rarely appear, except for [[61-March]], which is fixed. === Possible weather === Weather includes Rain, Storms, Fog, Flooding, and Eclipses. Weather does not affect loot, only difficulty. == Lore == {{main|Lore#Cosmography}} == Notes == * [[20-Adamance]] has the highest chance of [[Mansion]] layout among free moons (~2% as of v81). '' Special thanks to '''McMessenger''' for his [https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3075328887 Moon overheads and flooded timelapse guides].'' {{navbox moons}}

== Company Description == <!--T:1--></translate>
{{terminal|<translate>

"With an extra battery life and even brighter bulb, your colleagues can never leave you in the dark again!"

<translate>
<!--T:3-->
== Overview ==
The '''Pro-Flashlight''' is brighter and has a longer range than the normal [[Flashlight]].
It costs {{credits|10}} more ({{#expr:100*25/15 round 0}}% as expensive) and has a weight to it (the Flashlight weighs nothing).
A fully charged Pro-Flashlight lasts for 300 seconds ({{#expr:100*300/140 round 0}}% as long).

<!--T:4-->
== Strategy ==
When on sale, Pro-Flashlights may be bought in place of regular [[Flashlight|Flashlights]] for cheaper.

<!--T:5-->
Though Pro-Flashlights only weigh 5 pounds, it is enough to noticeably affect running speed and stamina depletion. For example, when trying to secure [[Bee Hive|Bee Hives]], it is advised to leave these flashlights at the ship for an extra few seconds of running.

== History== <!--T:6-->
</translate>
