Turret.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Stub}} == Overview == {{Infobox trap | image = turretrender.png | image2 = turretinhallway.png | damage = 50 per shot | damage_speed = 0.21 seconds | damage_type = Hitscan (AOE) | cause_of_death = Gunshots }} '''Turrets''' are stationary tripod mounted guns found [[interiors|indoors]]. Scanning left to a right in a pendulum, they will open fire on employees within their range. Turrets can be temporarily disabled using the [[Ship|ship's terminal]]. == Mechanics == === Scanning === In this state, the turret will slowly rotate, searching for targets. It will change the direction of its rotation every 7 seconds and check for valid targets every 0.25 seconds. If a valid target is spotted, it will switch to the '''aiming''' state. === Aiming === After detecting an employee, it will shine an reddish-orange beam toward its target while turning towards it for about 1.5 seconds before switching to the '''firing''' state, unless the target gets out of its field of view, in which it'll return to the '''scanning''' state. === Firing === In this state, the turret will start repeatedly firing bullets at it's target. Each shot is fired in an interval of 0.21 seconds, doing 50 damage each. The turret will cease firing and return to the '''scanning''' state if it's target has either died or left its field of view for 2 seconds. === Berserk === Upon being hit with a [[Shovel]], [[Stop sign]], [[Yield sign]] or [[Kitchen knife]] when not firing, the turret will wind up for 1.3 seconds before starting to shoot and rotate repeatedly for 9 seconds. The shots behave the same as they do in the regular firing state. Once the 9 seconds have passed, the turret will return to the '''scanning''' state. === Disabled === When disabled, a 4.5 second reactivation timer will start. Once this timer is up, the turret will reactivate, and will go back into the '''scanning''' state. Upon reactivating, the turret will go in a short cooldown, in which it cannot be turned off. == Strategy == When a moon rolls a trap hazard type, it will often generate the trap in a batch as it rolls a random chance. Because of this, if a turret is spotted anywhere in the facility, it's presence should be communicated to the other employees as further progression, especially in dimly lit or unlit passageways, should be expressed with caution to avoid running into a long hall guarded by a turret. In addition, large rooms, like the facility's conveyor belt room (dubbed the big room) and some of the mansion interiors can hide the turrets rather well, as the turret has an infinite sight range, so progression through these rooms should be taken lightly when turrets are present on the moon. Turrets will telegraph their presence with a laser light that is visible from very, very far away. Because of this, the employee can play "Minesweeper" with the hallway intersections and rooms, peeking around and looking out for turret laser sights. Many turrets can be spotted preemptively through this method, allowing for safer travel through the indoors. The turret, when it spots an employee, will lock on a target, and will continue to fire at the target until it cannot see it, and will fire a bit longer at the last sighting of the employee. It can only focus on one employee at a time, and as such, distracting the turret with another employee can be a risky but viable strategy to ensure others sneak past the turret. The turret also has a maximum angle it can normally turn, which it rotates around when idle; using this, a smart employee can run around the turret and prevent it from firing. If it is not possible to go around the turret, it is also possible to stand on top of the turret since it cannot aim upwards well. Finally, a risky strategy involves hitting it with a shovel; when this happens, the turret will spin 360 degrees in the direction it rotated last for three rotations while constantly firing. Because of this predictable rotation, it may be possible to use this to get around a turret. Doors have an odd interaction with turrets. When a door is closed on a turret, the turret's initial aiming line of sight is broken, but if a turret is already firing at an employee, the turret will still see the employee through the door and continue to fire. The turret can also kill the employee through the window of the door in the facility, and sometimes the door itself by sheer luck if the employee is too close to the door. This can allow a turret to constantly fire at the employee hiding behind the door. The turret sightline and angle of detection is visible on the computer terminal. This can be used to see where a turret guards from a bird's eye view. They can also be temporarily disabled in the terminal too, but keep in mind that once a turret reactivates it imposes a disabling cooldown visible on the monitor as a green bar. With good timing, it is possible to ensure that a turret never fires a single bullet, as it takes time to rev back up and then lock on a target. If you need to disable multiple turrets at the same time, the VIEW MONITOR command will render the monitor's contents onto the terminal display, saving you the trouble of having to go back and forth between the two displays and allowing you to type the ID of the turrets more swiftly. == Sounds == {{audio gallery | Turret spotting an employee|File:TurretSeePlayer.wav |br| Turret heard firing from a distance|File:TurretFireDistance.wav |br| Turret while in berserk mode|File:TurretBerserkMode.wav |br| Turret firing|File:TurretFire.wav |br| Turret's bullets hitting walls|File:TurretWallHits.wav |br| Turret activated|File:TurretActivate.wav |br| Turret deactivated|File:TurretDeactivate.wav }} == History ==

Landmine.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox trap | image = landminerender.png | image2 =1966720_80.jpg | damage = 50-100 | damage_type = AOE | cause_of_death = Blast }} == Overview == '''Landmines''' are grey disc-shaped explosive [[traps]] that can be found placed throughout the floors of all interiors including, as of version 72, the Mansion, the Factory and the Mineshaft. You can step on a landmine without exploding but as soon as you step off, it will detonate its surroundings, killing or heavily injuring those within its blast radius. No [[Entities]] except the [[Maneater]] in Baby Form can trigger landmines, but they can be damaged by its detonations. == Mechanics == The landmine is a passive, stationary hazard whose lethality only occurs when triggered by (most often) an unsuspecting player. At certain intervals, the landmine can be heard beeping on occasion and emitting a quick red flash, at a volume discernible enough to alert cautious players of its presence. Like [[turret]]s and [[Interiors#Security_doors|secure doors]], all landmines that spawn are visible on the ship's radar, and given an associated code that can be used to temporarily disarm them through the [[Ship#Terminal|terminal]], for a very short period of time. Much like other traps, landmines do not react to the behavior of entities. The landmine only clicks when one of the following scenarios occur; either a player walks on top of it, or an item falls on top of it. === Detonation === Detonation occurs when a player steps off the landmine (meaning they must be on it first), an item that is placed on it is retrieved, an item slides or rolls ''off'' of it,{{#tag:ref |As of v62, this only applies to [[Easter Egg]]s, [[Soccer Ball]]s, and [[Stun Grenade]]s, and to [[Body|employees' corpses]] dragged by [[Bracken]]s.{{cite YouTube|video-id=tkQY7SQfL14|title=Trio High Quota World Record in Lethal Company! [15837]|creator=Bread|start-timestamp=1:50}} Note that this won't work if something else is keeping the mine weighed down. |group=note }} or a player hits it using a weapon, either through a melee hit by [[shovel]] or [[Stop Sign]], or shooting it with the [[shotgun]]. This process invokes a blast radius that implicates any surrounding player or killable entity. Any within approximately 4.5 metres is significantly injured. Any within 4 metres is killed. The detonation is not instant; the landmine first plays an activation sound, suggesting that it charges up before exploding. The radius, and the player's default movement speed ensures that they cannot escape from its blast, killing any who set it off through movement. == Strategy == For experienced players, the sheer size of a landmine alone makes it easy to spot in a room, and its other tells (the occasional blink of light and sound) means it does not pose nearly as dire a threat as entities or even turrets. When paired with steam leaks, landmines become completely invisible normally, but cannot hide from the scanner, and can still be heard. Landmines can spawn anywhere there the employee can stand, which includes railings, catwalks, wall insets, and more. They can be particularly hard to spot if partially inside a wall or embedded in a flight of stairs, so continued scanning is usually recommended when the landmine beeps are heard. They can be proactively disarmed from the terminal using their unique code. This assumes play with at least one other player, who stays on the ship monitoring the crew through the terminal, disarming any landmines that are visible, much like [[Turret|turrets]] and secure doors. This will disable the landmine temporarily, allowing employees to venture past it for a limited time. However, due to their not so pressing risk when hazards like turrets and crushers are around, it's usually recommended to focus on them first. Remember, the VIEW MONITOR command will render the monitor's contents onto the terminal display, saving you the trouble of having to go back and forth between the two displays. The imminent threat can also be nulled with a [[teleporter]], if your crew has earned enough credits to buy one. While the reactive nature of the landmine means you can safely warp back to the ship unharmed, the landmine will still trip and detonate when you disappear, so make sure other players are not within range if you decide to teleport back. An [[Easter Egg]] could also potentially launch you further than its blast radius, due to the delay of the landmine's detonation and the egg's exceptionally high knockback upon combustion, though the strategy is not reliable as the chance of an egg's detonation is not very common. It is possible to trick a landmine into detonating to remove the threat entirely. A rather dangerous but always accessible method is to drop a [[Shovel]] as that it barely holds the landmine down, and grabbing the opposite edge while sprinting away. This works best when going at a top speed sprint. A much less dangerous method involves the [[Stun grenade]]; after a stun grenade's flash is used, you are still able to toss it out in front of you. With the correct distance, it is possible to skim the stun grenade over a landmine and trick it into detonating; for optimal results, throw straight forward. A final, much easier, but harder to come by method, involves the use of the [[Soccer ball]] scrap item. When on the ground, the employee can kick it by running into it, allowing a much easier and precise detonation of the landmine. Each of these methods can be used to kill a creature in the facility due to the high damage of the landmine. Note that detonation of the landmine does not remove its code from the terminal on the ship, which can cause confusion for an overseeing employee. == History == :'''''Note:''' The history of Landmines before version 39 is unknown. If you have information on its introduction, feel free to add it here.'' * [[Version 45]]: Landmines used to explode instantly when touched. * [[Version 47]]: Landmines no longer explode instantly. * [[Version 50]]: Landmine spawn probabilities on dine were adjusted. == Notes and References == {{notes and reflist}}

Spike Trap.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{Infobox trap | image = Spike Trap Render.png | image2 = spiketrapingame.webp | damage = Insta-kill | damage_speed = 0.8-26.2s (Interval)
Immediate (Detection) | damage_type = AOE | cause_of_death = Crushing }}{{Stub}} '''Spike Traps''' are large mechanical devices found [[interiors|indoors]]. Lined with rows of sharp spikes, they will periodically slam down, killing anything below them. == Mechanics == There are 2 spike trap types, interval traps and detection traps. === Interval Trap === 80% chance for a spike trap to be an interval trap. The fastest and slowest an interval trap will slam is: 0.8-26.2 === Detection Trap === 20% chance for a spike trap to be a detection trap. This type of trap will have an invisible trip wire, and upon touching it, the spike trap almost immediately activates. Slam time: 1/10 == Strategy == === Interval Traps === Wait until the interval trap slams, and then quickly run to the other side of the interval trap. === Detection Traps === An employee can jump over the tripwire. The safest way to tell if a trap is a detection trap is to wait until it slams. If it doesn't slam, then it's a detection trap. An employee with less than 10 lbs of items can sprint straight through a detection trap, since the slam time isn't fast enough to kill a running employee. == Occurrence == {| class="wikitable" ! Moons !! Min. spike traps !! Max. spike traps !! Avg. spike traps |- | [[Experimentation]] || 0 || 0 || 0 |- | [[Assurance]] || 0 || 2 || 1 |- | [[Vow]] || 0 || 0 || 0 |- | [[Offense]] || 0 || 4 || 2 |- | [[March]] || 0 || 18 || 4 |- | [[Adamance]] || 0 || 2 || 1 |- | [[Rend]] || 0 || 7 || 3 |- | [[Dine]] || 0 || 8 || 3 |- | [[Titan]] || 0 || 2 || 1 |- | [[Embrion]] || 2 || 14 || 8 |- | [[Artifice]] || 0 || 5 || 3 |- | [[Liquidation]] || N/A || N/A || N/A |} == Sounds == {{audio gallery | A Spike Trap slamming down.|File:SpikeRoofSlam.ogg |br| Creaking sounds audible while under a Spike Trap between slams.|File:SpikeRoofTrapCreak.ogg }} == History == === [[Version 50]] === Introduced.

<translate>
<!--T:2-->
A generic-looking '''Soccer Ball''', or football. Only Scrap Item in the game that can be moved without being picked up, as Players and other entities are able to kick it. It can still be held normally as a two-handed object. Will bounce off any entity it collides with, including entities most wouldn't expect, like [[Hygrodere|Hygroderes]], [[Circuit Bees]] and [[Roaming Locusts]].
</translate>

{{#invoke:Scraps/AutoTables|spawnChancesOnAllMoonsTables|SoccerBall
}}

== {{section heading/Trivia}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:3-->
Kicking the Soccer Ball against any entity can trigger a chain reaction where the Soccer Ball will bounce multiple times in a row. You may lose track of it if this happens.

<!--T:4-->
To prevent any accidental kicking by players or mobs in the [[Ship]], the ideal spot to deposit soccer balls is below the Control Screens in the corner exactly to the upper right of the Brake Lever Dashboard.

<!--T:5-->
The Soccer Ball is one of the 8 Scrap Items that was only added to the game in Version 60.

<!--T:6-->
There used to be a glitch that made Soccer Balls unscannable, which was patched in Version 70.
</translate>

{{Stub}}
== {{section heading/Company description}} ==
{{terminal|
Deal with those pesky weeds! Repeated, firm presses on the trigger aimed at the root of the weed is all you need!
}}
<translate>
== Overview == <!--T:1-->

<!--T:2-->
'''Weed killer''' is an item used to kill [[Vain Shroud]]s and [[Cadaver Growth]]s, as well as cure Cadaver Growth infection. It has roughly 30 seconds worth of usage, with each full spray taking about a second. When used to cure infected employees, it will deal 8 damage, but will not damage healthy employees.

<!--T:3-->
Weed Killer can also be used to repair and fuel the [[Company Cruiser]]. Each spray fills a tenth of the Company Cruiser's health, and a fifth of it's fuel, or "turbo" bar.
</translate>
== {{section heading/Strategy}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:4-->
Though it is possible to spray it for less than a full second, the effect of said sprays seem inconsistent when used on the Company Cruiser, and it is advised to use full second-long sprays instead.
</translate>
== {{section heading/History}} ==

=== {{section heading/Version X|55}} ===
<translate>
<!--T:5-->
* Introduced
</translate>
=== {{section heading/Version X|60}} ===
<translate>
<!--T:6-->
* Due to the removal of Vain Shrouds, Weed Killer's only interactions was with the Company Cruiser
</translate>
=== {{section heading/Version X|80}} ===
<translate>
<!--T:7-->
* Can now interact with the reintroduced Vain Shrouds and added Cadaver Growths
</translate>
== {{section heading/Trivia}} ==
<translate>
<!--T:8-->
Weed killer audio used to be global, allowing it to be used as a makeshift [[Signal translator]]. This was fixed in [[Version 71]].
</translate>

Sticky note.wikitext:
{{topicon Fandom|Sticky_Note}} {{interlangbox}} {{autoinfobox item |sell-value-min=0 |sell-value-max=0 |physics= |audible= |primary-use= |secondary-use= |tertiary-use= |inspectable=Yes }} The '''Sticky note''' is a pedagogical item which generates in the [[ship]], hidden just to the left of the [[Terminal]]. Its sole purpose is to reveal the otherwise hidden command SIGURD, which is used to read unlocked [[Sigurd Logs]]. The Sticky note does not show up on the [[scanner]].
It is one of the few equipment items that can't be put in the [[utility slot]]. Attempting to sell it will add {{credits|1}} to the ledger, but this doesn't count towards the total. {{navbox items}}

Scrap.wikitext: {{topicon Fandom}} {{interlangbox}} {{redirect|Items|purchasable equipment items|Store#Equipment}} '''Scrap''' are items found on [[moons]]. Collecting and selling scrap is the [[Quota|primary objective]] of the game. == Scrap list == * '''Min/Max Value''': The minimum and maximum amount of money an item can sell for. * '''Weight''': How heavy an item is to carry. Affects the player's movement speed and stamina consumption. * '''Conductive''': If an item will cause a player to be struck by lightning during [[Stormy|stormy weather]]. * '''Two-handed''': If an item requires a player to use both hands to carry. {{#invoke:Scraps/AllScrapTable|allScrapTables}} == Scrap spawn chances == {{see also|Mechanics#Rarity}} Scrap items that spawn on the floor in indoor areas such as the [[Facility]] and the [[Mansion]]. {{#invoke:Scraps/AllMoonsSpawnChances|allMoonsTable}} '''''Note:''' These are the chances to generate as floor loot so items with special spawn conditions such as the [[Apparatus]], [[Hive]], [[Egg]], [[Body]], and [[Shotgun]] will appear as unable to spawn.'' == Mechanics == === Single scrap days === {{main|Single scrap day}} There's a chance that when scraps are generated at the start of a day, they're all the same scrap item rather than random items. This is intentional and there are some safeguards to ensure the total value isn't lower than normal. === Variants === Some scrap items come in several variations. As of v68: * [[Easter egg]] meshVariants even though they seem to differ in UV map only ** f8ed4d6d07726e24289be8d5dbc3ac52 unknown (model Sphere_3) ** 2a66a44fbe626154a82724e8a0a19d32 unknown (model Sphere.001_1) ** 8f4570f2c335c2a43a837adf49a127a8 unknown (model Sphere.002) ** 3c4969f36ac234e46be19ef9031b3a4b unknown (model Sphere.003) ** 848d9bb47d47c5b45808c05dff16d993 R/B/Y (default) * [[Flask]] meshVariants ** 05d228f194dbb9a43a2826f31ea06d5c conical ** 3ecf58d216515ea44ab880dc230f357c round (default) * [[Metal sheet]] meshVariants ** bfa3f55416fb43443bc4d275882bd544 4-hole (default) ** 743c7dd8859195e47813cfda942b99ce 2-hole * [[Mug]] materialVariants ** e47a8b44aa6f9b54586a7f43d81c42d2 blank red ** 9c23ad4bd3f2268498a1fe98d56c7ed3 #1 MUG ** 7c76578fd54cc6e45a8f79d9caa2daae cow ** 84a9ba2f84eda1e4fb957fea456255fc MORNING! x3 ** 738ea9191589de74395611ece4ec1425 doodle creature (default) * [[Painting]] materialVariants ** 8f672fd63e3ba584dbcb0c2438149533 lake ** 3e1c024c84a38c746a26e247394d2f6d sky (default) [[Comedy]]/[[Tragedy]] are programmed as separate items rather than a model swap. The 2 [[clipboard]] variants are also programmed as separate items, with the same behaviour (and model?) but different textures. The item's variant is set upon generation, and is determined by the moon seed. Due to a bug,{{cite bug|(nitpick) Some furniture's and items' states aren't included in saves}} saving and reloading the lobby will reset all these to whichever variant is listed in their prefab. Scrap's variants are set as part of the RoundManager.SyncScrapValuesClientRpc method, paraphrased below. Note that there are 2 parallel mechanisms: meshVariants and materialVariants. But since no item uses both at once, it doesn't complicate RNG. Random rngScrapVariants = new(randomMapSeed + 210); foreach (GrabbableObject component in scrapItems) { // collection comes from callsite in `RoundManager.SpawnScrapInLevel` if (component.itemProperties.meshVariants.IsNotEmpty()) { component.gameObject.GetComponent().mesh = rngScrapVariants.GetNextItem(component.itemProperties.meshVariants); } if (component.itemProperties.materialVariants.IsNotEmpty()) { component.gameObject.GetComponent().sharedMaterial = rngScrapVariants.GetNextItem(component.itemProperties.materialVariants); } } == Notes and references == {{notes and reflist}} {{navbox items}}
